

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 1: [1]A New world
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

The sea waves were crashing unusually, and the sky had lost its luster due to the black clouds gathering on the horizon. The annoying sound of thunder was relentless, while intermittent flashes of lightning lit the way now and then. Amid this fierce maritime storm, there was a semi-destroyed ship sailing quietly, but if you looked closely, you would find many corpses scattered across the battered deck.

Among those bodies lay the corpse of a young man who appeared to be in his prime. However, the sea does not distinguish between the young and the old.

Suddenly, the "dead" man opened his eyes!

"Where am I?" A confused voice emerged from the young man with blue hair.

"Damn... My memories are a bit hazy..." He rose from the bloodstained wooden floor and looked around.

Confusion was evident on the young man's face, and he had no idea what had happened to him. "I remember taking a bullet straight to the head during the battle against the enemy army. So, didn't I die?"

This young man was named Ren, and he had been a soldier in his previous life. While executing a special operation with his team, the enemy had set a trap for him. After all of his team members were killed, he - the commander - was trapped until he was shot in the head after holding out for ten minutes.

"I don't know what's going on, but I'm still alive, at least for now..."

Ren stood up and assessed his condition. He found himself surrounded by a pile of decaying corpses, and a foul odor permeated the air. Although seeing dead bodies and smelling blood was not strange to him as a soldier, he felt uncomfortable being in the same place as these corpses.

So, he quickly decided to clean the ship and threw the bodies into the sea. After half an hour, he had disposed of all the bodies, which numbered in the dozens.

"What's with this turbulent weather?" The storm continued to rage, showing no signs of abating.

"Anyway, I need to clean myself up a bit and change my clothes."

Ren was filthy, and the smell of blood emanating from his body, mixed with sweat and remnants of dirt, made him extremely uncomfortable. His clothes were torn and unfit for wearing.

He entered the ship's cabin and immediately felt the warmth inside, a stark contrast to the cold surface battered by rain and storms.

He found himself in a place resembling a bathroom, washing his body with a bucket of cold, pure water that seemed to be the only source available. Ignoring the biting cold of the water, he thought aloud, "Given my current situation, these dead must be pirates, and the pirate flag I saw earlier confirms that."

"Although this might sound silly, I'm in another world. These old ships haven't existed on Earth for centuries!"

Ren sighed as he recalled the fan fiction he used to read during his holidays. He had enjoyed his time reading manga and novels, gaining some knowledge about such scenarios that the main characters often found themselves in those stories.

After finishing cleaning himself, he searched the ship for usable clothes, and fortunately, he found some garments that looked relatively clean. He dusted off the accumulated grime, put them on, and felt warmth returning to his body gradually.

After that, he looked for anything he could use to ascertain his new world. Although he already had an idea in mind, he wasn't completely sure yet.

His attention was drawn to several papers and documents placed on a wooden desk in the captain's room.

The papers were posters bearing pictures of individuals Ren felt strangely familiar with. Ren calmed his racing heart and grabbed one of the posters, then read aloud:

"Alvida."

"Dead or Alive."

"5,000,000 Berries."

"So my guess was correct after all... I've been reborn in the One Piece world!"

"Damn it..."

Ren felt a wave of anxiety wash over him as he realized which world he had transitioned into or been reborn in. He didn't specifically know what had happened to him, but that was not what occupied his thoughts now.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 2: [2] A Target?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

"So I have a golden finger too... This really makes me feel relaxed!" Ren tried to suppress his growing excitement, but no one could hear him in this vast sea. After all, he was alone on this

ship.

"A Lust System? Its name suggests it's a perverted system... but well, it's not like I don't like that." A sly smile formed on his face, but he quickly returned to his normal demeanor.

Soon, a new message appeared on the blue interface that Ren was sure he could see all by himself! After all, this was the same thing in all the novels he had read, wasn't it?

[After successfully activating the Lust system, I will explain to the host the benefits of the system and how to use it in detail!]

Ren heard its beautiful feminine voice in his mind, and he was sure it was the voice of this strange system.

....

[Five minutes later.]

After reading the system's explanation, Ren finally understood what this golden finger was about-in other words, what the main idea was...

It was... sex.

Yes, Ren was shocked when he discovered just how perverted this system was. Most of the current tasks in the system were related to sex and conquering girls!

"What a fitting system for its name, not that I don't like it, haha!" Ren said with an excited laugh.

Ren returned to more serious matters and carefully analyzed the information he had received. The system's operation was somewhat simple.

There were tasks, and upon completion, the host would receive a random or specific reward, depending on the tasks the host completed.

For example, there were some tasks like:

[Engage in sexual intercourse with three girls within a week, reward: random]

[Take the virginity of one girl, reward: Basic Observation Haki Cake]

[Form a pirate crew, crew requirements: one person beside the host, a pirate ship, a pirate flag, reward: Basic Armament Haki Cake]

There were many tasks, most of which revolved around sex. According to the information provided by the system, new tasks could suddenly appear based on the host's situation. However, Ren didn't need to think about that right now.

"There's also a system shop, but many of the valuable items I can't afford right now. I'm just a poor man at the moment," Ren said with a sigh.

[Host, as a reminder, you haven't opened your status yet. You can just say "status" in your mind, and it will appear automatically!]

Ren didn't waste much time on idle talk and quickly summoned his status. "Status"

Name: Ren Konabar.

Age: 27.

Physical Strength: 500 (Level E).

Skills: None.

Lineage: None.

Devil Fruit: None.

Fighting Skills: None.

"I'm pathetically weak..." Even though Ren felt sad about his weakness, he knew it was reasonable; after all, it had been less than an hour since he opened his eyes in this world.

"But you never know what the future holds for me," Ren smiled as some ideas and plans began to take shape in his mind.

[Host, you can also view the status of crew members, but since the host hasn't formed a crew yet, I will display that in the future.]

Ren nodded, then left the room and headed towards the deck, quickly noticing that the storm had passed.

The heavy rain had stopped, the dark clouds began to dissipate, and sunlight returned to the wrecked ship.

Unfortunately, the owners of this ship had died in this troubled world some time ago, and their ship was now used by a stranger who had arrived in this world due to a mysterious Way.

The harsh ocean world welcomed its new decomposing corpses, as the waves crashed against the pirate ship sailing towards the unknown, driven by the winds. The only living man on this ship gazed toward the horizon with a contemplative expression.

Ren recalled his previous life, his family, his mother, and his sister.

Although he was just an adopted child, he loved them like his true family.

"They must be heartbroken when they learn of my death. I wonder what Leila's reaction will be. Will she cry? And my mother..."

"Ah... there must be a way back, just like I came into this world; there must be a way out of it... right? But... what if I can only return after a century or more? I'll come back to find their graves...sigh... Let's not think about that right now..."

"One thing is certain: I must live now... no matter the cost!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 3: [3]First Battle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

After several hours after the storm ended, Ren noticed an island in front of him.

This was the first time since arriving in this world that he had seen an island! In the past few hours, he had seen nothing but the endless blue sea. However, he knew that most of this planet consisted mainly of seas and oceans.

'As far as I remember, this world is made up of four seas: the East Blue, the West Blue, the North Blue, and the South Blue.'

'In addition to these four seas, there is what is known as the 'Grand Line' or the 'Pirate Graveyard.' It is divided into two halves: the first half is called Paradise and the second half is the New World.'

Ren recalled all the important information related to this world, and he was confident that his memory had not been affected by his mysterious transfer.

'Anyway, I need to determine my location and the timeline to see if the events of the original story have begun or not. If I am in the East Blue and there's still some time before Luffy's journey begins, it will be easier for me when I look for people to join my crew.'

Ren rose from the broken wooden chair and began to look at the island that was becoming clearer before him.

His eyes sparkled with cold determination. He decided he would no longer care about maintaining the original storyline as it was. If there was a benefit in changing this path, he would do it. The same went for the opposite.

To achieve his goals, he wouldn't hesitate to clash or form animosity with his favorite characters from his previous life; this was not just a manga, but reality.

....

Soon, the ship docked on the shore, and Ren disembarked, heading toward the city in front of him.

The port where the ship had docked was filled with merchant vessels, with people boarding and disembarking everywhere. Ren's arrival did not attract much attention, as he had removed the pirate flag from the ship, making his entrance smooth and without any issues.

....

After exploring the city a little, Ren realized he had arrived at "Loguetown," the birthplace of the Pirates King Gol D. Roger and the place where he was executed. He confirmed this fact when he reached a large square, in the center of which was an execution platform that attracted the attention of many tourists.

'I searched through all the wanted posters for East Blue pirates that I saw, but I couldn't find a poster for 'Monkey D. Luffy.' This means the story hasn't begun yet, and I have a chance to recruit... I mean, to enlist good members of my crew...'

The first member Ren thought of was the beautiful navigator, Nami. He couldn't sail without any navigation experience, not to mention traveling in the turbulent sea of the Grand Line...

He didn't even know how he ended up in Loguetown; he had let the winds guide the ship randomly, and after a few hours, he found himself here.

Of course, he also wanted to recruit Nami because of her beauty, and there was also her beautiful sister in Cocoyashi Village...

'The problem is that I'm still weak, so if I go to Cocoyashi Village with my limited strength, I'm sure I'll be killed by that fish-man, Arlong.'

'All I can do now is... complete this mission.!'

Ren looked at the blue facade before his eyes with determination.

[Have sex for the first time, reward: random.]

[Get a girlfriend, reward: low-level energy drink.]

[Get your first kill, reward: learn one of the 'Six Marine Powers' techniques (Rokushiki).]

'Some of these tasks I can complete easily... for example, the first kill,' Ren analyzed calmly. He was accustomed to killing in his previous life, so he wouldn't feel guilty about doing it in this world, but he preferred not to kill randomly; after all, the world was filled with pirates who would help him complete this task.

Seeing the reward for this mission, he felt a sense of urgency and wanted to wander around the city to find a "volunteer" to help him obtain the reward.

'Having sex for the first time and getting a girlfriend are related tasks. I won't sleep with a random prostitute just to get a reward, so I can ignore the first reward. It's not like I'm in a tough situation anyway.'

Ren didn't want to go to a brothel and sleep with a prostitute who had been used by several men. That would leave him feeling uncomfortable and would leave a bad taste in his mouth. He preferred to take things slowly for now.

....

[Half an hour later.]

Ren was now standing in front of five men wielding sharp swords.

They smiled wickedly as they looked at Ren as if they were looking at a dead man.

As for how he ended up in this situation, it all happened shortly after Ren decided to wander the city in an attempt to complete the "first kill" mission.

Then, when he entered one of the narrow and well-hidden alleys, five men emerged and surrounded him.

"I only wanted one person, but five appeared? Is this my luck?" Ren sneered while the men scowled at him.

"You fool, have you gone mad from fear and started to ramble? Give us all your money, or I'll Help you in this!" One of the pirates in front of him shouted, spitting as he glared with a 'typical villain' smile.

It should be noted that Ren's current appearance made people think he was one of the rich families, with his clean clothes that he "borrowed" from the destroyed ship and his handsome face. With his unique blue hair, it was hard for the pirates not to think of robbing him.

"If I said I'm a broke homeless person with no money, would you believe me?" Ren said with an innocent smile.

"Of course not! You fool, don't waste my time any longer and hand over the cash, and maybe I'll let you leave here alive!"

"Alright, alright... No need to get angry, here, take my money."

Suddenly, Ren moved quickly, pulling a gun from his jacket pocket, and fired quickly at the

pirate in the middle!

*Bang!*

"You bastard! You asked for this! Kill him!"

After one of the pirates had his head pierced and his body fell to the ground, the other four attacked Ren furiously with their swords, trying to kill him.

*Bang!*

Ren dodged the sword aimed at his left side, then reloaded his gun and skillfully shot a bullet between the eyes of one of the attackers, who fell dead instantly.

He had no time to relax as another pirate attacked him, his sword close to his waist. Ren used his gun to block the sharp blade and then kicked the pirate, causing him to lose his footing

and balance.

*Bang!*

He shot the stumbling pirate to ensure he couldn't get up again. The bullet struck the pirate's head, confirming his death.

Of the five pirates, three were dead, and two remained. Another pirate attacked Ren, targeting his head with his sharp blade. Turning his head to the other side, Ren dodged the sword and

was close to shooting the attacker in the head.

"!!!!!"

But his calm expression quickly changed when he saw the second pirate pull out a gun and

glare at him with a wicked smile, ready to shoot!

"Damn it!"

Ren cursed, then grabbed the pirate in front of him, pinning him down with all his strength,

using his body as a human shield.

His actions were so quick they took less than a second.

*Bang!*

The second pirate fired, but his wicked smile quickly turned into utter shock when he saw his

bullet strike his friend, who looked at him in disbelief.

'Now!'

Of course, Ren wouldn't let this opportunity slip by when the enemy was in a state of

confusion.

He threw the pirate who had been shot by friendly fire aside, then took his gun and reloaded it again. The distance between him and the only remaining pirate had closed significantly, striking the pirate's hand and causing him to drop his gun, then grabbing him by the neck.

*Bang!*

A single bullet pierced the pirate's head at close range, ending his life. Ren threw the dead

body on the ground and smiled coldly.

"That was a good warm-up."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 4: [4]Tashigi's first appearance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

After Ren killed the five pirates, he began cleaning up the crime scene.

As he pulled out the bloodied bodies and checked their pockets for anything valuable, he noticed that the face of one of these pirates seemed somewhat familiar.

He wondered where he had seen it before and then quickly remembered.

"Oh! This idiot must be the same pirate whose head is worth 5,000,000. What was his name again? Bandin?"

"It doesn't matter. Since you died by my hand, let me send you to the Navy branch. I really need money right now."

Ren felt a little disappointed after checking all five pockets and not finding any Belly.

'Poor freaking people.'

Ren hid the bodies of the four pirates, except for Bandin, whom he planned to hand over to the Marines in order to cash in on the reward.

Then he suddenly remembered something important.

"During the previous fight, I heard a notification from the system in my mind, but I wasn't in a position to check it. Now that I've completed the mission, I must collect my reward!" Ren smiled expectantly.

[Ding! Mission completed and "Rokushiki" obtained. Rankyaku obtained!]

[Description: Rankyaku (Tempest Kick) is one of the six Marine techniques that allows the user to generate slicing winds of force using the strength of their legs. This projectile appears as a wave of energy from the user's leg, generated by someone with a high level of strength and speed in their legs. These compressed air blades are incredibly powerful, able to easily cut through people and objects alike.]

"Oh, I got an offensive style! This came at the right time. I can't fight Arlong with just bullets and guns, but with the Marine's six styles, I can do it!"

Ren was indeed very lucky. With the Tempest Kick, he could approach Arlong Park now with more confidence.

Immediately after the system message, a scroll appeared in Ren's hand with "Rankyaku" written in bold. He executed the system's instructions and internally said: "Consumption."

In the next moment, the scroll disappeared from Ren's hand, and he began to feel a burning sensation spreading throughout his body, especially in his legs. He felt that his legs had become several times stronger!

Ren felt that he could run faster, and his physical fitness and agility had increased. He sensed that he could dodge bullets now after consuming the scroll.

"Oh... what a good feeling! Strength is flowing through my veins and body." "Status"

Name: Ren Konabar

-Age: 27

-Physical Strength Level: 3000 (E)

-Skills: None

-Bloodline: None

-Devil Fruit: None

Combat Skills: Tempest Kick

"My physical strength has increased by 2500 points, yet my body is still level 'E.' I wonder when I will break through to the next level? Anyway, I still have this pirate to hand over to the Marines first."

He grabbed Bandin's body and lifted it. The body was stained with blood and had four visible holes in the chest. Ren wrapped the dead pirate's clothes in the chest area to keep them from getting dirty, then carried him and left the narrow street.

Ren arrived at the Loguetown Marine Branch while carrying Bandin on his shoulder, attracting several glances on the way, but no one dared to stop him when they saw the pirate on his shoulder.

To these simple citizens, a pirate with a 5 million Belly bounty was a terrifying person.

As for Ren, he didn't care about the various opinions of these citizens; his focus was now on the Marine branch, specifically the leader, Smoker.

"Smoker, the man who ate the Logia Fruit 'Moku-Moku no Mi' (Smoke-Smoke) and became a smoke human. Normal physical attacks and bullets no longer affected him, allowing him to deal with many East Blue pirates who knew nothing about Haki."

"Also, Smoker is someone who comes from Marineford, the main Marine headquarters. He was trained there by the best and most skilled teachers. If he hadn't stayed in East Blue for so many years, he would have learned Haki long ago."

"Even without Haki, the powers of Logia Fruits are considered invincible, even to people in the first half of the Grand Line, but things are different in the 'New World,' where there are many monsters who have awakened Haki."

Ren arrived in front of the Marine Branch and saw two Marines guarding the entrance.

"Stop."

One of the soldiers motioned for Ren to stop, then looked at the body he was carrying and said

in a formal tone:

"Are you a bounty hunter?"

"Yes, I met this pirate on the way and thought I would bring him here."

Ren lied shamelessly. There was no harm in acting like a bounty hunter now. He wouldn't go up to Smoker and tell him that he was a pirate and going to become the Pirate King or something like that, right? Ren wasn't Luffy, and he wouldn't let Smoker chase him and bother him while he still needed power.

Ren didn't have "the most dangerous criminal in the world" as a father and didn't have a grandfather called "Marine Hero" who chased down the Pirate King several times. Also, he didn't have a brother who was the second in command of the Whitebeard Pirates.

He only had the system, and the rewards of the system didn't come for free; he had to go through a lot of problems to complete it... except maybe for the sex missions...?

"Okay, come with me."

The Marine's voice quickly brought him out of his thoughts.

Ren followed the Marine and entered the Marine Branch building while attracting the attention of some people, but they didn't stop; it seemed that everyone had work to take care of and didn't have time to mind their own business.

Soon, a beautiful girl appeared before Ren.

Standing in the corridor was a girl of medium height with short dark blue hair and a pair of beautiful black eyes that drew attention.

She was wearing a short-sleeved shirt of different colors while holding her sword in her hand,

looking toward Ren.

"Tashigi-san!"

When the Marine saw Tashigi in front of him, he quickly greeted her in a respectful tone.

Tashigi was very popular in this city. Her beautiful appearance and gentle personality helped boost her popularity, but what was more important was her strength.

Other than Smoker, she was the second most powerful person in Loguetown's Marine Branch.

"Hi, who is this...?"

Tashigi returned the greeting, then looked at the handsome man in front of her with an

inquiring tone.

"He's a bounty hunter who has captured a pirate and wants to claim his bounty," the Marine

quickly explained.

On the other hand, Ren was looking at this beautiful girl in front of him, occasionally letting

his gaze wander.

'I expected to meet her here, but I didn't think things would go this fast. However, compared

to her future self after the timeskip, Tashigi is still lacking... especially in certain areas...

cough!'

"Hi, I'm Ren."

Ren smiled sweetly at Tashigi, who was looking at his handsome face and gentle smile,

blushing.

"So, Ren-san, you said you wanted to get this pirate's reward, right?" Tashigi tried to hide her embarrassment by shifting the topic to the reward.

She also ordered the Marine who came with Ren to return to his work and that she would lead Ren to obtain his rewards after confirming the identity of the pirate.

Tashigi kept chatting with him about various topics, such as what weapon he used and when

he started working as a bounty hunter.

Ren, who had watched the One Piece anime, knew that Tashigi wasn't trying to extract information from him or anything like that; she was just a talkative girl.

In order to gain her interest, Ren told her that he was a swordsman who had broken his sword

in

a fight and had to use his gun to fight.

After Tashigi learned that he was a swordsman, the topic quickly shifted to swords and the methods of swordsmen.

Fortunately, Ren, who was familiar with various information from One Piece, was able to

answer Tashigi's questions.

Then, after a few minutes of talking, Tashigi stopped in front of one of the rooms and

knocked on the door.

"Come in."

Soon, a hoarse voice reached Ren's ears.

It was Smoker's voice.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 5: [5]Robbery
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

Inside Smoker's office, a muscular man with light teal hair and a pair of brown eyes sat. He was smoking two cigars in his mouth at the same time. He was wearing a large, thick, white and blue navy jacket, keeping it open, with green fur lining the neck, wrists, and hem. The jacket had the kanji (1) for "justice" written on the back.

Several cigars were tied to the jacket, and he also wore brown leather gloves, blue jeans with a brown belt, and large brown leather military-style boots.

This man was Smoker.

Ren stood in front of him while Tashigi was next to him, explaining the situation to Smoker.

"Smoker-san, this person is a bounty hunter who eliminated the pirate Pandian, who was recently causing trouble in the city. I sent someone to confirm his identity, and it has already been done," Tashigi said, sounding eager to help this man she had only known for a few minutes.

"Bounty hunter?" Smoker frowned and said in a hoarse voice. In his view, pirates and bounty hunters were the same thing; they were all after money, but bounty hunters tried not to break the law.

However, Smoker knew that most poachers later became pirates because of their greed for money.

"Well, Mr. Ren, have you ever thought about becoming a Marine?" Smoker said, surprising Ren and Tashigi.

After Ren pretended to be thinking seriously for a few seconds, he opened his mouth and said: "Becoming a Marine? I'm sorry, Smoker-san, but I don't feel like I'm fit for this 'noble' job, and I wouldn't be able to bear such a responsibility," Ren said in a steady voice. He really knew how to speak well.

In fact, his previous words had a lot of sarcasm hidden within them.

As someone who knew what the "gods" of this world, in other words, the Celestial Dragons, did, how would he accept becoming one of these pig dogs?

In Ren's eyes, the Marines were nothing more than guard dogs for the Celestial Dragons! Even the highest ranks in this system called Marine, the Admiral, had to leave Marineford as quickly as possible if one of the Celestial Dragons was exposed to any harm or attack.

Hahaha... the Admiral, the strongest man in the Marines, was like a dog rushing to help his owner who was in trouble!

Also, the pirate side has a lot of hot and beautiful girls in it... how could he miss the chance to win the heart of all these beauties?

Returning to the story, after Smoker heard Ren's answer, he seemed to expect it and didn't pursue the matter further, ordering one of the marines to go to the safe and take out the money.

Meanwhile, Tashigi was clearly disappointed when she learned that Ren had no interest in becoming a Marine.

She wanted to ask him why, but seeing that they were still in Smoker's office, she decided to do this outside.

Five minutes later, a marine came over with a bag full of money. There were five million Berries in total, and he gave it to Ren.

"Thank you for your cooperation. If I come across a wanted pirate again, I will be back here," Ren said to Smoker and Tashigi.

But he knew that this would be impossible; after all, he would become a pirate soon.

"Why don't you want to be a Marine, Ren-san?"

Ren and Tashigi were now outside the Marine Branch, and Tashigi took the initiative and asked him why he rejected Smoker's offer.

From Tashigi's viewpoint, being a Marine is a wonderful and honorable job. Even though it is dangerous, if Ren was able to take down a pirate with millions of Berries, he should be strong as well. Why did he refuse?

"Miss Tashigi, my justice doesn't exist in the Marines."

Ren said some bold words to Tashigi. He had already decided that he would try to get her out of this Marine organization and expose all the darkness and corruption surrounding her, so maybe Tashigi would be ready to leave the Navy.

"W-What do you mean, Ren-san?! The Marines are always trying to save innocent people and fight evil pirates who are controlled by their desires and greed. If this isn't justice, then what is??" Tashigi was shocked by Ren's previous words but soon began to stand up for her beliefs.

Seeing Tashigi staring at him while giving him a long speech about justice or something, Ren sighed helplessly and shook his head.

'Maybe if she saw a little bit of Marine corruption, she would change?' he thought in his mind.

"Well, Miss Tashigi, we all have our own convictions and views. The Marines are not a place where I can work comfortably, unlike you. Perhaps when you see things from a broader perspective, you will understand my words one day. But now I have to go. There are still some urgent matters that must be done. I gotta do it."

He decided to think about this matter later; the important thing now was to be stronger and stronger.

"Miss Tashigi, I will go now. I hope we will meet in the future."

After hearing Ren's words, Tashigi nodded to him and said goodbye. Although she still felt a little disappointed that she couldn't convince him, she knew there was nothing she could do.

Ren now arrived in front of a sword shop. This was the place where Zoro had taken two legendary swords after astonishing the old shop owner with a brave display.

Roronoa Zoro, this man used the three-sword style (Santôryû). In this style, the swordsman relies on fighting with three swords, two in his hands and the third in his mouth.

It was Zoro who invented this style.

Zoro had three swords, the best of which was Wado Ichimonji.

For this sword, its previous owner was Kuina, Zoro's childhood friend. After her death, Kuina's father, who is also Zoro's teacher, gave the Wado Ichimonji to Zoro.

He also had two other swords, but they were nothing special, and they soon broke after his duel with the greatest swordsman in the world, Dracule Mihawk.

After many events, Zoro arrives in Loguetown and enters this store in search of two new swords. As a result of the plot, Tashigi helps him buy swords.

After many other events, Zoro happens to find a rare sword in a scrap heap, and after seeing the owner knowing that Zoro can become a great swordsman, he decides to give him his family's heirloom, which was a sword that had been passed down through generations.

The two swords were Sandai Kitetsu and Yubashiri.

Sandai Kitetsu is a legendary sword, but it is a cursed sword, and it is one of the 21 Great Grade swords.

But all Kitetsu swords are cursed, as it is said that anyone who uses a Kitetsu sword dies.

But Ren still wants to own this sword; he doesn't want to give up just because it's cursed or

something.

Yubashiri is also a target in this "mission," in addition to being a sword from the collection of fifty legendary swords in the world.

After remembering all the information about these swords and their whereabouts, Ren waited until he was sure that there was no one in the store except the owner, then he put on

the black mask that he bought from a store in the east of the city.

He quietly pushed the door open and entered.

Ren is a pirate.

Then what's the job of pirates?

Of course... stealing!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 6: [6]First weapons
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

In the best weapons store in Loguetown, a red sign with Japanese writing displayed two crossed swords. Inside, shelves and barrels were filled with various weapons: rifles, sniper rifles, small pistols, daggers, sabers, spears, knives, giant axes, and many others. There was also a variety of ammunition, including bullets and colorful sword sheaths.

The store owner felt disheartened; business had slowed, with no customers around. Sales had been declining recently due to the severe pirate shortage, a result of Smoker's presence in the city. Because of him, many East Blue pirates avoided Loguetown, fearing an encounter with the "White Hunter." This was good for ordinary citizens, but bad news for the owner, whose livelihood depended on pirate traffic.

Just then, he heard the door open. His spirits lifted, and he quickly put on a professional smile, clasping his hands politely to welcome the customer. If he was lucky, maybe he could sell the weapons at a higher price-or even trick a naive buyer.

"Welcome, sir, to our store..." he began, only to stop abruptly upon seeing the customer wearing...a mask? More intriguingly, the stranger carried a bulging bag. The owner's thoughts went straight to money, and greed gleamed in his eyes.

Ren didn't respond. Using the speed he gained from consuming the "Tempest Kick" ability, he reached the owner in an instant.

*Bam!*

Ren struck the back of the owner's neck with precision, just enough force to knock him out without killing him. As an experienced soldier, Ren knew exactly how to make someone "sleep," and with his control over his power, the owner fainted instantly. Catching him before he hit the floor, Ren gently laid him down, minimizing noise.

Ren glanced around, cautious. He didn't know where the owner's wife was and wanted to avoid unnecessary attention.

"If I remember, Zoro found the cursed sword Sandai Kitetsu in one of these barrels..." Ren muttered to himself, recalling the OG.

There were three barrels in front of him. He reached into the middle one and pulled out a sword with a red sheath. He unsheathed it, examining the blade to ensure it matched the Sandai Kitetsu he remembered.

"Yes... This is Sandai Kitetsu!" Ren said excitedly. He quickly searched for a sturdy leather sword holder, selecting a stylish black one, and attached it to his waist before securing the sword.

'I need to hurry before someone shows up,' Ren whispered, moving to the next room.

There, on a wooden table, he spotted a long sword covered by a cloth. Removing the cloth, he found a black-sheathed sword-Yubashiri. He examined it as he had with Sandai Kitetsu, then placed it in another sword holder.

"Now that I have what I came for, it's time to leave." Ren moved toward the exit.

An idea struck him. He made his way to the pistols section and selected a black pistol with a gold-yellow trigger, slipping it into his pocket. He then reached for the door, preparing to leave.

But just then, the door opened on its own. Through the mask's eyeholes, Ren saw an elderly woman entering with a bag full of vegetables, meat, and other groceries.

"You... who are you?" the old woman asked, sensing something was amiss. She noticed the masked man leaving the store in silence and felt her heart sink when she realized her husband hadn't greeted her.

'The owner's wife?' Ren thought, surprised at her timing. Without hesitation, he struck the back of her neck, making her faint as quietly as possible, and left her lying at the entrance. As Ren left, the commotion outside grew.

"He's a thief!"

"The gun shop's been robbed! Someone call the Marines!"

"Did you see his face?!"

"No, he was wearing a mask!"

Shouts and conversations erupted as the Marines were notified. Soon, a squad arrived, led by Smoker.

"For the city's most famous weapons store to be robbed in broad daylight, right under the Marines' noses-what a disgrace!" Smoker growled.

The Marines around him remained silent, familiar with their leader's temper. While he wouldn't kill them, they knew he'd put them on overtime.

....

"Huff... I didn't expect that woman to enter the store at such a time," Ren muttered as he ran. "She caused a huge scene, and there's no doubt the Marines and Smoker are on high alert."

Ren decided on his next move, removing his mask. After buying some new clothes for 30,000 Berries, he found a hotel in the southeastern part of the city, far from the weapons store, and paid for one night.

Standing in front of a large mirror, Ren examined his reflection in his new outfit. He wore long black pants with a sword holder at the side, though he hadn't yet placed his swords in it. He planned to store them more securely later.

He also wore a gray sleeveless shirt, a black jacket with pockets, and the stolen pistol rested in his right jacket pocket. The clothes complemented his dark blue hair and deep Blue eyes, enhancing his sharp, masculine features. Many girls in town found his look attractive. "Sigh... I need to get to Cocoyasi Village soon, but the problem is I don't know the way..." "Oh...This might work!"

An idea came to him about reaching Cocoyasi Village to confront Arlong and gain "two beautiful girls."

"But first, I have to secure these two swords," he said, glancing at Yubashiri and Sandai Kitetsu lying on the bed.

The hotel room was dimly lit, with the curtains blocking most sunlight. Ren approached the bed, exhausted but focused on securing his new acquisitions.

'System,' he called mentally.

A gentle female voice responded in his mind:

[Yes, host.]

'Oh? So the system doesn't only appear for missions? I can actually talk to it? Interesting!'

"Can I store items somewhere? Is there a place for my swords?" Ren asked.

[You can view available commands by saying "commands" in your mind.] Ren did so, and a blue interface appeared before his eyes:

List of Commands:

1-Host status.

2-Crew member status.

3-System Shop.

4-Infinite Space.

"I already know the host and crew members status, and the system shop... but Infinite Space? That's great! I was hoping for an inventory function," he mused aloud.

[Host, Infinite Space is a unique system feature allowing the host to store anything except living organisms, with unlimited capacity as implied by its name.]

Satisfied, Ren nodded. "This is great."

"This system gets more interesting by the minute," Ren thought, reaching for Sandai Kitetsu

and Yubashiri.

He said, "Store," in his mind, and a pale blue light enveloped the swords, which promptly disappeared. Opening the Infinity space, he saw thumbnails for Yubashiri and Sandai Kitetsu

in an infinity interface.

Ren repeated the process for his money, then lay down on the now-empty bed.

"I'll leave Loguetown tomorrow. For now... I need some sleep."

After a long day filled with challenges in this new world, Ren finally drifted off to sleep.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 7: [7]Leaving
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

Soon night fell in Loguetown, and Ren slept peacefully in the hotel room, unaware of what was happening on the other side of the city.

....

Inside the weapons shop, unlike before when it was originally empty of customers, the shop was now filled with many people-specifically, they were Marines.

"So, you're saying the thief was wearing a black mask? Was there anything special you noticed about him before you fainted?" Smoker asked the elderly owner as he quietly smoked a cigarette.

The owner, who had woken up a while ago to find that the most important thing to him- besides his wife had been stolen, was devastated. It wasn't even for sale! It was a family heirloom! How could he sell it? But now... it was gone-no, it had been stolen!

He didn't even know how the thief knew he had Yubashiri!

How?! He hadn't told anyone except his wife! This shouldn't have happened! Damn thief!

He suppressed his frustration and anger, then answered with a stutter: "N-No, I didn't even see him well! When he entered, I was looking at him from afar, but soon I found him right in front of me in a second! Before I could do anything, I had already..."

Smoker looked at the old man, whose eyes were red from crying over his misfortune, and then sighed. Based on this old man's information, the thief was clearly targeting the legendary sword.

Smoker wasn't a swordsman, but he still knew some information about legendary and cursed swords. However, he had summoned someone who knew more than him in this regard-his assistant, Tashigi.

Looking at Tashigi in front of him, who had lost her cheerful and happy expression, he sighed inwardly.

"Another powerful sword falling into the hands of evildoers... unbelievable!" From her tone, it was clear how upset she was over the incident.

As someone whose dream was to collect all legendary weapons and protect them from falling into the hands of the "wrong" people, she couldn't help but feel sorrowful when such a theft occurred.

Smoker turned to the Marine in front of him and asked, while keeping the cigarette in his mouth, "Have you found any trace of him yet?"

The Marine whom Smoker spoke to was nervous, but he quickly replied, "N-no, sir, we have searched every inch of the city over the past few hours, and we haven't found the thief yet..."

"Keep looking for any clues, and if you suspect anyone, just let me know." Smoker wasn't angry; he knew the chances of catching the thief now were low but didn't give up.

Based on witness statements from citizens present at the time, in addition to notes from the owner and his wife, Smoker knew that this thief must have had some strength. Otherwise, he wouldn't have had the courage or power to carry out such a robbery in broad daylight.

Smoker thought to himself and tried to recall any suspicious individuals he had encountered that day. Characters began to pass through his mind, one by one, until his thoughts quickly settled on a man with dark blue hair.

"He said his name was... Ren?" Smoker muttered, frowning.

When he remembered Ren being offered the chance to join the Marines but refusing, his suspicions quickly deepened.

Smoker described Ren's appearance to the Marine: "His name is Ren. Dark blue hair, symmetrical features. He stands out among people, so it will be relatively easy to locate him. According to his claim, he works as a bounty hunter and is also armed. Don't engage him unless necessary. Just inform Commander Smoker."

The Marine finished writing down the suspect's description and then ordered it to be distributed to other officers so they could instruct their subordinates.

"S-Smoker-san? Is there something wrong with Mr. Ren?"

Tashigi, who was in a sad and angry state, spoke to Smoker with tension in her voice. She didn't understand why her superior was suspicious of Mr. Ren.

In Tashigi's opinion, even though she had only known Ren for a short time, he seemed kind.... Even though he refused to join the Marines and had his own sense of justice, he wasn't a bad man!

Smoker looked at the girl and sighed, knowing that her problem was being too emotional and easily influenced by others.

He didn't bother to explain his reasoning to her and merely said that it was "just a precautionary measure" in case he was somehow connected to the incident.

....

Midnight quickly arrived in Loguetown while the Marines continued tirelessly scouring the city for the thief. In a certain hotel room, a handsome man suddenly opened his eyes.

He looked at the clock hanging on the wall; it was 12:00 midnight.

Yawning slightly, he stretched his limbs as he got out of bed and headed to the bathroom to freshen up.

He quickly showered, dressed in black, and put his loaded gun in his jacket pocket.

As for bullets, he had been in a hurry and had forgotten to take more from the old owner's shop. He only had 70 bullets, most of which were stored in the system's 'infinity space.'

He opened the window to let fresh air into the room as he gazed at the empty streets at night. But what caught his attention were the Marines passing by now and then, looking as if they were searching for someone or something.

"Have they already begun securing the city? With Smoker's intelligence, he should be a bit suspicious of me. I introduced myself as a bounty hunter from nowhere, declined his invitation to join the Marines, and left a poor impression. On the same day, a high-grade sword gets stolen... it must look like I came here specifically for that sword. In short, I'm sure Smoker is starting to doubt me..."

Ren, of course, was the person in the hotel room. He calmly analyzed his situation, putting himself in Smoker's position as a Marine and mentally simulating Smoker's personality based

on the manga.

"But... all of this is useless."

Ren chuckled lightly, realizing that any attempt by Smoker to frame him would be futile. Even

if Smoker discovered his involvement, he would already be gone.

Yes, Ren had decided to leave Loguetown tonight!

....

In Loguetown's port, activity continued even at midnight.

Merchant ships were docking, goods were being loaded in preparation for sailing, and cruise

ships were receiving passengers.

More Marines than usual patrolled the area, scouring every inch for anyone matching the description provided by Commander Smoker.

Among the bustling activity, there were a few pirate ships, whose occupants behaved unusually calm out of fear of the Marines-especially Smoker. Knowing his reputation and power, the pirates dared not break the law.

"Guys! Are all the goods and supplies loaded? We need to set sail now and head to the Grand Line! Hurry!" The captain of one pirate ship called out, imagining himself as a king of the seas, sitting on a golden throne with beautiful women surrounding him.

"Yes, sir! We're ready to sail now."

"Hahaha, boss! We're going to conquer the seas!"

"Quickly, men! My sword thirsts for blood!"

"Hahahaha!"

"My name will be known across the seas! Everyone will fear me! I'm the new Whitebeard!"

....

Inside the ship, in the supply warehouse, a powerful hand emerged from a large wooden crate filled with food supplies, followed by another hand on the other side. The crate, unable to bear the pressure, broke into several pieces.

The drunken pirates on deck, engrossed in their celebrations, didn't notice this strange

occurrence.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 8: [8]Target:Sisters
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

Ren, emerging from the wooden box, looked around curiously. His face quickly lit up with a smile upon seeing several barrels and boxes filled with food-it reminded him of the first scene involving this world's "hero," Monkey D. Luffy.

Back then, Luffy had emerged from a barrel in the sea, surprising the crew of Alvida's ship, then met Coby, and so on.

Returning to the story, Ren recalled what had happened before leaving the hotel.

[10 minutes earlier.]

Ren quietly leapt from the room window, slipping into the dark streets undetected, thanks to the cover of night that obscured him from the Marines' sight.

At the port, he spotted several pirate ships and decided to sneak aboard one of them to escape Loguetown. He identified his target, rushed to a crate of goods, and began clearing it of food to make space inside.

"Who are you, bastard?" A voice shouted in annoyance.

Realizing he'd been discovered, Ren turned towards the source-a bulky pirate holding a wine bottle, his eyes barely open.

"He's alone... everything's fine," Ren muttered to himself. He advanced swiftly, silencing the pirate by covering his mouth and choking him with a powerful grip.

The darkness concealed Ren's actions, allowing him to carry out the assassination perfectly. The pirate couldn't cry for help, silenced by Ren's firm hand that wouldn't relent.

"Umgh... Ahhh... Aghhh!" Weak sounds escaped the pirate's mouth before his body gradually stilled from lack of air.

Once Ren confirmed the pirate was dead, he hoisted the bulky body and dumped it into the sea, letting the waves swallow it.

Afterward, he quickly returned to the crate, climbed inside, and covered it.

"I need to learn Observation Haki as soon as possible..." Ren thought, as he waited for someone to load the crate onto the ship. The close call had heightened his urgency-he hadn't noticed the pirate until he'd heard him. If it had been a sober and determined opponent, they might've attacked him outright.

After a few more minutes of waiting, two more pirates arrived.

"Damn, where did that idiot Karenbo go?"

"Maybe he got lost taking a piss and forgot his way back to the ship?"

"Or passed out somewhere around here."

"Hahaha!"

They laughed, joking about their comrade who, unbeknownst to them, was already dead. They lifted the crate and carried it aboard.

----

Currently, in the ship's food hold.

"It seems this crew is heading for the Grand Line. I need to act before they stray too far from my destination."

Ren cautiously opened the food hold's door and moved towards the deck, finding the corridor empty-everyone seemed to be celebrating topside.

Once he reached the deck, he silently unleashed his attack.

"Tempest Kick!" He kicked out powerfully, generating a blue energy blade almost two meters long. The blade slashed into two unfortunate pirates, leaving deep wounds across their bodies.

They had no chance to scream; their consciousness faded as they collapsed in their own blood, faces frozen in shock.

"You bastard!"

"Where did he come from?!"

"Kill him! Rip him to pieces!"

The pirates rushed to Ren with swords and rifles. One fired, but Ren vanished and reappeared behind a large barrel.

*Bang! Bang! Bng!*

A flurry of gunfire tore into the barrel, but Ren remained unscathed. When the shooting paused, the pirates with swords charged with vengeful expressions. Ren launched a series of swift, slicing kicks, sending multiple energy blades through his attackers.

*Whoosh!*

*Whoosh!*

*Whoosh!*

The blades severed limbs, leaving most of the attackers dead and a few injured. Yet the pirates kept advancing. As another wave of gunfire erupted, Ren grabbed a wooden box, flinging it towards an attacker.

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

The box shattered under fire. The pirate captain shouted in fury.

"Stop running, you motherf-!" The captain cursed, enraged by the stranger who had appeared out of nowhere and slaughtered his crew with ease.

Ignoring the captain's outburst, Ren unleashed another Tempest Kick, cutting through the charging pirates. Their screams filled the air as they fell.

The captain momentarily lost sight of Ren, and when he looked again, Ren was gone. "Looking for me?" Ren's voice, calm and almost friendly, sent chills down the captain's spine

as he felt the cold muzzle of a gun pressed against his head.

Heart pounding, the captain froze, his mind filled with terror as Ren's words echoed.

"Drop your weapon, raise your hands, and tell your men to do the same."

Ren held the captain by the neck, his gun poised to fire. Silence fell over the deck as the crew watched, fear evident in their faces.

"Do as he says!" the captain shouted desperately, abandoning his pride to survive.

The pirates hesitated but eventually complied, throwing their weapons down.

"Good. Now, who here is the navigator?" Ren's gaze swept over them.

After a moment, a muscular man stepped forward, visibly nervous.

"Do you know how to reach Cocoyasi Village?" Ren asked.

"Yes, sir... it's a day's journey at top speed," the navigator stammered.

"Perfect. I'll need your help."

Ren looked around, tightening his grip on the captain.

"Now... I want you to...die for me!"

*Bang!*

The captain's head exploded as Ren fired, leaving the pirates in stunned horror. Taking advantage of the chaos, Ren unleashed a series of Tempest Kicks, mowing down those who

tried to flee.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 9: [9]God Of Weapons
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

After eliminating the pirate crew, leaving only the navigator alive, Ren ordered the trembling Mayn to clean the deck, now littered with the bodies of the slain pirates. Fear gripped Mayn as he realized he was in the presence of a man capable of single-handedly decimating his entire crew. With no courage to refuse, he began the grim task of tossing the corpses into the sea, where the sharks eagerly awaited their next meal.

Ren settled into a wooden chair on the deck, gazing out into the dark expanse of the night. The only illumination in the vast ocean came from his ship, which felt like a beacon in solitude.

As Mayn steered the ship towards Cocoyasi Village, Ren felt a buzz in his mind—a familiar voice echoed once again:

[Ding! Congratulations, host! You have completed the hidden mission "Eliminating the First Pirate Crew" and obtained the combat skill!]

"!!!"

Ren's heart raced. "Did I just complete a hidden mission? This skill should be something extraordinary... Let's see what I've received."

He commanded the system to display his status.

Name: Ren Konabar.

Age: 27.

Physical Strength Level: 4500 (E)

Skills: Weapons Knowledge (Level A)

Combat Skills: [Tempest Kick (Low)] | [God of Weapons (Low)]

Devil Fruit: None.

Girlfriends: None.

Crew Members: None.

Bloodline: None.

Ren's gaze fell on the new skill and he eagerly read its description.

[God of Weapons: A skill that enables the user to create any weapon existing in this world or born from the user's imagination. As long as the user possesses sufficient resources, any weapon can be created. Once this skill reaches its maximum level, the user can forge powerful weapons like Ancient Weapons, Shichibukai Seraphim clones, Germa 66's body, and various advanced weapons that predate this era.]

"!!!"

The power behind such a skill left Ren awestruck. 'If I can maximize this ability, I could create an infinite army of robots as long as I have the materials!'

He contemplated the implications of wielding Ancient Weapons, recognizing the strategic advantage they would offer in battle. "Imagine the surprise on my enemies' faces... With such power at my disposal, I'd be unstoppable!"

Recalling past events, he thought, "The Pacifista are formidable foes, capable of easily dispatching weaker opponents. I remember the Straw Hat Pirates struggling against one; they would have perished if not for Kuma's timely intervention... but that's an impossibility for me."

Coughing lightly to clear his thoughts, Ren reflected on the rewards of his hidden quest. "Eliminating this pirate crew has yielded such a remarkable skill. It seems hidden missions reward generously."

He soon noticed another skill had been added to his status.

"Weapons Knowledge...Acquired due to my extensive understanding of advanced weaponry from my past life. These technologies are far beyond the primitive designs of this world... save for Vegapunk's inventions."

He also noted an increase in his physical strength, now up by 1,500 points, although his overall level remained at E.

After processing the changes, Ren glanced at the new categories in his status.

"Girlfriends... cough, this system is oddly meticulous about perverted details... but why is there no 'sex slave' category?"

The system, maintaining a stoic front, seemed to want to retort: "Who here is the pervert?!"

Shaking off the strange interaction, Ren rose from his seat and left the deck, making his way toward the ship's warehouse.

Standing among the stacked boxes filled with various foods, including apples, meat, and barrels of wine, Ren noted the bounty available. This stockpile would sustain him for several years, even for someone with a hearty appetite.

He stored the supplies in the system space, knowing that time paused for anything kept there, eliminating worries about spoilage.

.....

As Ren continued exploring the ship, he checked different rooms, gathering anything he deemed useful.

In the treasure room, he stood amidst piles of gold, jewels, and neatly arranged money. His eyes gleamed with excitement, remembering someone who would surely envy such a sight. "Ah, Nami would lose her mind over this... and maybe her childhood friend, Carina, would join her," he mused, a smirk forming on his lips.

After securing the wealth, he exited the room to continue his collection.

....

"A treasure trove of weapons and artillery... This reminds me of my previous life," Ren sighed, nostalgia washing over him as he recalled his days as a Special Forces soldier.

Once he had cleared the weaponry from the room, he ascended to the deck, where the night slowly yielded to dawn, bathing the Sea World in light.

Although fatigue weighed heavily on him, Ren knew rest would have to wait. Mayn was still aboard, and he couldn't lower his guard until they reached their destination.

As the ship approached Arlong Park, Ren braced himself for whatever challenges lay ahead.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 10: [10]Nojiko & Nami First apparence
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Edited.]

Ren's journey towards Cocoyashi Village continued without any problems, and after more than a day of sailing at top speed, Mayn came running to him.

"S-Sir, we have arrived..." he said in a trembling voice as he bowed his head; he didn't dare to look at Ren.

Ren raised his head and looked at Mayn without expression. He had just gone up to the deck and taken a rest after finding one of the random rooms and closing the door to it as a precaution.

"We've already arrived? I see..." he said boredly and began to yawn while stretching his limbs.

He raised his head and looked at the island in front of him, which was the Conomi Islands archipelago where Cocoyashi Village was located. In fact, Arlong not only controlled Cocoyashi Village, but also many similar villages and collected money from them every month.

100,000 Berries for adults and 50,000 for children.

"Arlong, that Fish-Man must be very rich now after all these years of robbing citizens... Right?" Ren thought internally, and a plan began to form in his mind on how to get Arlong's

money.

"So... sir... C-can I go??" Mayn asked nervously and expectantly; he hated Ren so much, but he was more afraid of him.

"Yes... Take one of the boats on the ship, some food, and get out of here quickly."

"T-thank you, sir!"

Mayn turned and started running quickly to escape from this ship to freedom.

But he didn't know that as soon as he turned around, Ren had already taken out his gun while trying to hit him with a single bullet to the head.

*Bang!*

Ren fired, and the gullible pirate's body fell in front of the door that led inside.

"Nice shot!!" Ren laughed, then reached next to the corpse and saw a bullet-sized hole in the back of the pirate's head.

"I'm improving my skills day by day~"

"When will the system add the 'sniper' skill along with 'tempest kick' and 'God of weapons"?"

[Ding! Congratulations, host, for regaining your shooting skill. Congratulations for obtaining the 'sniping' skill!]

"Is this system reading my mind or something?" Ren said suspiciously.

[...]

The system didn't answer and remained silent. Ren didn't care about the matter anymore and opened the Status.

"Status," Ren said.

Name: Ren Konabar.

Age: 27.

Physical Strength Level: 5300 (E).

Combat Skills: [Tempest Kick (Low)], [God of Weapons (Low)], [Sniping (High).]

Skills: Weapon Knowledge (High), Sniping (High).

Devil Fruit: None.

Girlfriends: None.

Crew Members: None.

Bloodline: None.

Not many things had changed except for a slight increase in physical strength due to Ren constantly training his body while waiting to reach Cocoyashi Village.

The sniping skill was also added, and his level was already high from the beginning. This is the first high-level skill that Ren obtained!

"Now I'm more confident to kill Arlong and his crew without help. All that remains is to sneak onto the island."

He wasn't stupid enough to try entering the village with the ship. Once his ship was spotted by the enemies, the Fish-Men would rush towards it and sink it. Then it was known that the Fish-Men are a race that doubles in strength in water; he would be in a very hard position. In addition, he was human and needed to breathe to live.

Ren entered the ship, changed his clothes, and put the clothes he bought from Loguetown into the infinity space.

A blue light came out of his hand, and this light quickly covered the entire ship. In the next moment, the ship disappeared, and there was no longer a place for Ren to set foot.

*Splash!*

He fell into the sea and started swimming towards a hidden corner of the island without attracting attention.

He was going to hunt the sisters... cough, to help the village... for humanity!

...

Cocoyashi Village, Tangerine Farm.

A medium-sized girl with light blue hair, beautiful black eyes, and tan skin can be seen.

A large heart tattoo was inked on her right arm and chest. She was wearing a beige sleeveless shirt, blue pants, and purple sandals. Moreover, she appeared to be wearing pink lip gloss.

This beautiful girl, who appeared to be in her twenties, was Nojiko. As usual every morning, she went out to take care of the tangerine farm that she inherited from her adoptive mom,

Bell-mère.

Although she was smiling while watering the lemon trees, behind that smile was deep sadness and hatred.

She hated the Fish-Men, and she hated herself for being weak and allowing her little sister to go through suffering that a little girl couldn't bear. This suffering continued to this day. She also hated the Marines who neglected her village and allowed the Fish-Men to do whatever they wanted on this island, enslaving humans as if they were gods.

If it weren't for her little sister who worried so much about her and didn't want to leave her alone in this cruel world, she would have left this world a while ago.

"Hi, miss... Do you have anywhere I can get some warmth..." Nojiko suddenly heard a male voice and looked in the direction of the voice.

There was a handsome man standing there. Although she was surprised at how handsome he was, she looked at the state he was in.

His clothes were completely wet, and drops of water were dripping from them every second.

He looked as if he had just emerged from the bottom of the sea.

This person was Ren, who swam to the island and came to Cocoyashi Village while avoiding

the eyes of the Fish-Men.

He wanted to surprise Arlong and his crew.

"You... I've never seen you here before... You're not a Citizen from the island, right?" Nojiko

stopped what she was doing and then looked at the handsome man in front of her, trying to remember his face, but it was useless.

"Yes, I'm not from this island..." Ren was about to make up a story about how his boat sank and he had to swim to this island, but Nojiko's voice quickly interrupted him. "Anyway, you have to dry your clothes before you get sick. Come on in, and you also have to leave the island before Arlong's men see you." She motioned for him to enter the house. Ren didn't refuse the invitation of this beautiful girl, whose identity he naturally knew. He quickly entered the house, and Nojiko told him to use the bathroom to dry and change his

clothes.

"I don't have any male clothes... don't you mind wearing girls' clothes?" Nojiko said while

laughing a little.

Ren's lips trembled slightly, and he quickly told her that he had spare clothes. Nojiko was confused but allowed him to do whatever he wanted while she went to prepare some tea.

....

Ren was now sitting opposite Nojiko, who was looking curiously at his beautiful black clothes. She didn't remember that he was carrying anything in his hands, but she didn't ask him. "Drink this tea and leave the island then. Do you have a boat?" Nojiko said and then took a sip

of tea.

"No, my boat was broken, so I swam to this island." Ren made up a story on the spot.

"That's a problem..." Nojiko frowned slightly.

"By the way, why do you keep insisting that I leave the island? Is there something going on

here?" Ren asked.

Nojiko sighed and then decided to explain to this person who seemed older than her but maintained the appearance of a twenty-year-old young man.

She told him that she was an orphan and that a woman had found her on the battlefield, along

with another girl who became her sister; the second girl was called Nami. That woman was a Marine who was wounded in a battle against pirates. Her name was Bell-mère.

She also told him about Bell-mère, who decided to adopt her and Nami, then returned to her hometown, Cocoyashi Village, and lived her days happily with the two children, watching them grow year after year.

"Even though Bell-mère wasn't rich, she supported us with the little money she earned from working in tangerine farming. She always tried to meet all our needs..." Nojiko began to remember those beautiful days, which she considered the best days of her life with her sister

and her sweet mother.

"But... everything changed when Arlong and his crew of pirates arrived here. They imposed taxes on people for their lives, and whoever refused to pay died."

"Bell-mère sacrificed herself for me and Nami... she didn't have enough Berries left to pay for

her life, and Arlong killed her...!"

Nojiko had already begun to cry. She usually wasn't like this, but upon remembering her tragedies, she could no longer contain her feelings.

Ren looked at the beautiful girl in front of him with pity. He wasn't a cold-blooded killer, although he could be cruel to people who deserved it, like pirates.

He was someone who valued relationships and family love if it was between people who deserved it, like Nojiko, Nami, and Bell-mère. Those three were a happy family before Arlong and his men arrived. They destroyed this happy family just because of their hatred for

humans.

Ren didn't care about Arlong's past or his deep enmity with humans. He decided that he would make that fish and his crew regret being born in this world.

"And it didn't just end there... Arlong, that son of a bitch, forced Nami to join his pirate crew

and threatened her that he would kill all the villagers if she didn't draw the maps he wanted.

He promised her that if she brought him 100 million Berries, she could buy the freedom of

Cocoyashi Village."

"Because of this, Nami became a member of the Arlong Pirates and had the Jolly Roger of the Arlong Pirates tattooed on her shoulder."

Thinking of the suffering her little sister was still enduring, tears welled up in Nojiko's eyes,

and she could no longer continue. She felt embarrassed to be crying in front of someone she

Night quickly arrived in Syrup village, and villagers began to return to their homes to sleep and rest after a tiring day of work, but they didn't know that an unknown pirate ship was docked on the shore.

The pirate ship was carrying the flag of the Black Cat Pirates and had a prominent mast in the shape of a cat's head. Many fierce and evil pirates could be seen polishing their swords and checking their guns.

It's a hunt night after all, and today they will make a great fortune according to what Captain Jango, who serves as captain of the Black Cat Pirates in Kuro's absence, said.

When the huge pirate ship reached the shore, a loud wave rose, carrying with it the melodies of the wind and the waves of the sails. The beach was empty, except for scattered rocks and calm waves.

The pirates began to disembark from their dark ship, their faces carrying a ferocious smile, sneaking onto the shore like dark ghosts, each step vibrating with tension and anticipation on the shore.

Aside from the pirates excited about the massacre, four men and three children could be seen looking at this with different expressions.

Luffy and his crew had come here when one of the three children saw a pirate ship secretly coming to this village at night and told Usopp.

Usopp was so afraid that he could only ask Luffy and the rest for help, who immediately accepted that.

"There have been many changes in the plot due to my intervention, but this world still wants to include Usopp in Luffy's crew in any way possible, so that Usopp will remain indebted to him forever."

Ren thought internally, looking from afar at this scene while there were three girls with him, Nojiko, Nami and the last one was Kaya, who agreed to join his crew after he saved her and treated her, then promised her to save this village as well.

After Kaya learned that the village was still targeted by the remnants of the Black Cat Pirates, she couldn't help but feel panic and fear, but Ren quickly reassured her and told her that he would take care of them.

"Nojiko and Nami, you are the ones who will fight in this battle. I'll not intervene unless something goes wrong." Ren looked at Nojiko and Nami.

"I will not disappoint you, Captain!"

"Me too!"

The two girls were excited for the battle, although they were a little nervous, but they had more self-confidence after receiving the latest rewards.

Especially Nami, she felt that her physical strength had increased a lot after she ate the Armament Haki cake. Nojiko was also excited to try out her new power.

Back to the pirates, Jango was now talking to them about how Captain Kuro was waiting for them to act as quickly as possible. After the pirates heard Kuro's name, they were very terrified because they still remembered his methods, strength, and cunning, as well as his bloodlust.

On the side of Luffy and the rest, some things happened, Luffy and Zoro were able to reach the slope without losing their way like the original work. They were about to start the battle when they looked at the pirates who started to climb the slope, but they were soon interrupted by a voice from behind them.

"You're not going anywhere."

Ren said to them, using Soru who arrived in a brief moment behind them and they never noticed it.

"What?!"

"Where did he come from?!"

Luffy jumped in panic when he heard a familiar voice coming from behind him. After recognizing it, he didn't relax. He was still alert, unlike his usual stupid personality.

Zoro also nervously gripped the sword hanging at his waist. He didn't know how this man appeared again in front of them. Was it the same speed we saw before?

Maron, Usopp, and the rest of the children remained frozen in place. Naturally, they didn't know how the man they met in Kaya's palace appeared right behind them and what he wanted, so they were naturally afraid.

Especially Usopp, he felt that this handsome man in front of him had taken what was most precious to him. He didn't know how, but he felt that he would be an enemy of this man to the death.

Ren didn't care about their different thoughts and spoke again in a serious tone: "No one will move from here. As for the pirates, they will be dealt by my crew members, is that clear?"

Ren's serious tone made Usopp afraid. He was about to open his mouth and accept but Luffy's angry voice ruined his plans:

"What the hell?! Who are you to order me? You bastard fight me!"

Luffy screamed angrily. He couldn't accept the idea of someone controlling him and telling him what to do. He had always longed for freedom, which is why he didn't listen to his grandfather's words when he urged him to join the Marines, and now a stranger comes and tells him this and tries to Control him, how can he not get angry?

Zoro didn't mind his captain's actions, he was also annoyed by Ren's arrogant attitude, even though Zoro knows that the man called Ren is strong and perhaps stronger than he imagines but he believes that with enough training and determination he will reach him and perhaps surpass him.

In addition, Zoro always felt that Ren gave him a bad feeling...

"Gomu Gomu no, pistol!!"

Luffy quickly punched Ren, his rubber hand extended and reached in front of Ren in a few moments and was about to hit him.

Usopp and the three kids looked at this scene with horror.This was normal because it's the first time they had seen Luffy's Devil Fruit ability, but their expressions quickly changed again when they saw the next scene!

*Swoosh!*

With the sound of the wind rustling, Ren disappeared from his place in an instant, then appeared behind Luffy, whose smiling expression froze and was replaced by a look of shock and disbelief. His body froze and his scalp felt numb. An inexplicable shiver ran up his spine and spread to every place in his body.

Zoro froze in place in horror. He couldn't see the opponent's movement at all, and it seemed as if he had disappeared and reappeared behind his captain in moments! This level of speed was terrifying to him.

"how..."

Luffy turned his face and looked at Ren's strong figure behind him, looking at him with a cold smile, like a lion looking at a weak rabbit.

At this moment, Luffy felt that the person in front of him was as terrifying as his old grandfather!

But things were much different in this situation, even if Luffy was afraid of his grandfather but he knew that he wouldn't kill him in the end, but there is no evidence that Ren wouldn't!

In fact, all these thoughts passed through Luffy's mind quickly and it was only a moment in the real world. Zoro wanted to respond quickly and tried to pull his sword from its hilt, but

before that Ren was moved!

*Boom!!*

He kicked his foot towards the shocked Luffy, the kick was so fast that only a small explosion was heard and then Luffy was thrown a few meters and fell to the ground in pain. Although Ren had controlled his strength well so as not to kill Luffy with one hit, this kick was still very painful for him.

Luffy tried to get up, but the pain he felt didn't allow him to do so. He remained holding his stomach while he was kneeling, moaning and spitting blood from his mouth.

Ren didn't stop, he arrived in front of Zoro, who took out his sword and tried to protect his captain, imbuing his hand with Armament Haki and then punching straight at Zoro's sword.

*Crack!*

sword and reached his stomach.

with him.

[The illustration pic here]

The Log Pose syncs with the magnetic field of the island they are on and the nearby islands, and based on that, it determines the path forward in the Grand Line.

[As the host wishes, an explanation of the new feature will be sent.]

After a moment, a new notification arrived.

[Pirate Hunting Feature: Enables the host to obtain random rewards by hunting specific pirates, The host will always receive half of the bounty placed on the pirate's head, and as for the remaining rewards, they depend on the host's luck.]

Miss System said in a teasing voice.

This feature was extremely useful; firstly, he would get a certain amount of money after hunting pirates, and there was a good chance he would receive additional rewards along with the money.

[Shigan: It's a close combat technique, one of the Six Powers, allowing the user to pierce the target's flesh with their finger at extremely high speed. The resulting wound is approximately the size of a bullet hole. It's a highly useful technique that can be executed quickly and accurately. It synergizes well with remarkable speed, and its impact is multiplied several times when combined with Armament Haki.]"

.

"Shigan: Golden Lotus!"

After announcing the name of the technique, she thrust her finger into the rock; normally, her finger would break because of this step, but it went all the way into the rock and smashed it to

pieces this time!

Shigan has many variants.

"The Golden Lotus" is where the user directly attacks with their finger in close combat.

"Fling Finger pistol Pick" is sending a shockwave similar to a bullet; the effect is stronger than a regular bullet shot.

"Fling Finger pistol Sting" is an air bullet fired from the user's finger, and this bullet can penetrate Devil Fruit defenses, as Rob Lucci did with Luffy in the canon.

Mikita nodded to him and approached another rock, All these rocks were brought out from Ren's Infinity Space specifically for Mikita's training.

The Grand Line, Paradise.

In an unknown sea, aboard a certain pirate ship.

Kaya was shocked after hearing Ren's words, and so were the rest of the girls.

The answer, of course, was no.

So, they didn't hold much hope for Vivi, They looked at the girl who wore a grim expression as she pondered over Ren's previous words.

Vivi was conflicted; she wanted to save her country, but the price was becoming a criminal. She thought of her father and everyone she loved in the Alabasta Kingdom, the people dying of thirst, seeking revenge against the king, believing him to be the cause...

She wanted to go there and tell them that all of this was Crocodile's plan!

But she couldn't. Luckily, after facing the disaster that was a powerful pirate, it turned out he had the same goal as her: to overthrow Crocodile. Before he helped her, though, she had to pay the price.

She was on edge; she didn't know how her father would react when he found out she might become a pirate.

In the end, Vivi chose to "sacrifice" herself for the sake of the country.

No one could solve her problem except Ren; she feared if she refused, everything would be ruined.

Ren laughed when he saw Vivi's exaggerated expression, so he decided to explain to her what he planned for her.

"R-really?!" She exclaimed with joy, seeking confirmation from Ren. Did this mean she would be the crew's joker or what?

Even the girls were surprised by Ren's words; they thought Vivi would sail with them and become wanted by justice.

"I mean what I say, but you must obey my orders as a member of the crew, even if you're not with me on the ship." Ren suddenly became serious.

"I understand! Captain." Ren chuckled when he heard her call him captain.

A notification arrived from the system; Ren looked at the blue interface and smiled.

[Ding! Congratulations host, for completing the main mission "Recruit Vivi into the crew" and obtaining:"Haki Training Guide"]

[Haki Training Guide: An instructional book that allows you to learn about Haki and how to train it, providing tips for getting the best results in the shortest time, it contains all information about types of Haki as well as theories on new ways to use Haki]

"Interesting" Ren read the description and became interested in this mysterious book that appeared in The infinity space without warning.

"You're officially a member of our crew now, Vivi" he said, looking at the princess and smiling faintly.

"Thank you, Captain."

Ren stopped talking to Vivi and opened her data.

¶- Name: Vivi.

¶- Age: 17 years.

- Occupation: Not specified yet.

¶- Affiliation: #### Pirates.

¶- Status: Stable.

- Title: None.

1- Bounty: None.

¶- Physical Power Level: 2,400 (E).

- Loyalty: 55%.

¶- Devil Fruit Rating: None.

¶- Skills: None

- Haki: None.

There were no surprises; she was weak and pitiful, but he would gradually make her stronger over time.
