She noticed Ren didn't like this clear division among them; Nojiko didn't want to see her boyfriend upset, so she took it upon herself to improve the relationship between all the girls.

In Ren's room.

Ren sat in his room, his face illuminated by a faint light emanating from a single candle placed on a small table in front of him, He held a book with a black cover and golden

inscriptions.

The golden inscriptions formed a single sentence that seemed to be the title of the book:

{Secrets of Haki: The Hidden Spiritual Power.}

He was contemplating the pages of the book, while the illuminated candle cast a long shadow on the wall, adding a mysterious and sacred atmosphere to the place.

Amidst the deadly silence, he immersed himself in his reading, and as he pondered the pages, The noticed small details related to Haki techniques.

The book discussed the types and levels of Haki, then moved on to explain the characteristics

of each.

- Armament Haki: Where the user focuses their spiritual power through a part or all of their body, creating a magical shield of black color called Armament Haki, which can be used for both defense and offense, making attacks several times stronger when combined with Armament Haki.

- Observation Haki: It's a power present in all creatures, but often remains dormant in many, The essence of Observation Haki is to use that mysterious spiritual power to awaken the sixth sense, which achieves miracles and reaches a stage where you can see the future.

As Ren read more and flipped through the pages of this mysterious black book, his

understanding of Haki deepened.

He knew relying solely on the instant power of the system was a mistake; Ren understood this, so he always trained hard to avoid falling into the trap of quick benefits.

"To become a master of Haki, one must feel Haki, sense Haki, live with Haki, and understand

the essence of Haki"

When he read this sentence from the book, Ren didn't quite understand what it meant.

After several attempts, he gave up and decided to continue reading the book.

- Conqueror's Haki: It's a power present in a few creatures, they are born with it, but it remains dormant, It can be awakened in life and death situations or when exposed to a strong

mental shock.

Those who possess Conqueror's Haki have the ambition to rule everything, the ambition to rule the world and the seas; these people were born to become kings.

Ren's heart raced when he read this page; he had the desire to rule everything, Does this mean

*Boom!*

Compared to being upset with Ren, he was now even more afraid.

Not only him, but even the other agents, even those who had lost consciousness, miraculously woke up with vacant eyes.

*Pop!*

Ren's voice at that moment invaded the minds of the agents, imprinting commands that couldn't be disobeyed. Memories of the previous incident began to fade from their minds, leaving their memories incomplete. Their minds then invented complex scenarios to explain this strange situation.

The effect of Ren's mesmerizing magnetic skill was extremely powerful. All the agents were much weaker than Ren, so there was no problem in hypnotizing them and making them forget the previous wave of Haki.

But the fear of Ren remained deeply rooted in their hearts, and this was something that couldn't be erased.

Simply put, everything was extremely bizarre.

speaking to his superiors.

Chapter 78: [78] The Poneglyph

The Grand Line, Alabasta.

-[Cobra POV]-

"H-how do you know about this?!" I asked, my voice trembling, shock filling my heart.

Standing before me was Alabasta's savior, not alone, accompanied by a member of his crew whom I hadn't met before.

The shock hadn't left me yet. Just minutes ago, he had come to me, stating he wanted to speak with me alone, but now this woman was with him, and it seemed he didn't mind.

Then suddenly he dropped a bombshell. How did he know about the Poneglyph in the tomb of the kings?

If this information were to be leaked...

My body trembled as I thought of the World Government's ruthlessness; they wouldn't hesitate to erase Alabasta from history!

"Do you know what happened to those who searched for these things, Mr. Ren? Like...Ohara?" I asked, surprised to see the woman with him trembling.

He calmed her down, then looked at me, a meaningful smile on his face before saying, "It seems I need to convince you."

-[General POV]-

After 10 minutes of conversation with King Cobra, Ren convinced him that he harbored no ill intentions towards Alabasta.

Cobra already knew this, but what king wouldn't feel apprehensive when a pirate knows a secret of his kingdom, especially if that secret is a hidden Poneglyph in the royal tomb.

And so, Ren, accompanied by Robin, headed to the tomb of the kings under Cobra's directive. They descended a secret staircase and reached their destination swiftly.

Along the way, Ren noticed Robin's continuous trembling. Though she maintained a calm and calm façade, he knew her current emotions better than anyone else.

20 years of pursuit couldn't be hidden.

It had been her life's dream, even if the Poneglyph she was about to read didn't contain information about the Void Century, it was still incredibly enticing.

The first Poneglyph Robin discovered when she was 8 years old was in a secret chamber in the Tree of Knowledge in her hometown, Ohara, containing partial information about the ancient kingdom and the Void Century.

Robin followed the will of Ohara's scholars. In fact, they didn't leave her any instructions regarding discovering the Void Century and revealing the truth to the world, but Robin took on this seemingly impossible mission to honor her mother's memory and the rest of the scholars.

"Are you alright?" Robin heard her captain's inquiring voice. She knew he noticed her emotional state beneath her cool façade but didn't feel embarrassed. She answered with a slightly trembling voice, "Yes...a little."

He patted her shoulder; Robin didn't mind the close contact. She wasn't in a position to care about such small things; they had reached the royal tomb after all!

"We're here," Cobra said, then looked at the two individuals following him.

Ren observed the scene before him calmly; he didn't react much because he had seen it in his past life, but seeing it personally was still an amazing experience.

There, in the center, above the solid ground, lay a carved stone, appearing as if it had been there since the creation of this world, though it had only been there for a few centuries.

It exuded an aura of mystery and reverence. Yes, this was the indestructible stone, holding the secrets of the world and the ancient weapons in its inscriptions.

[Image here.]

Then he turned his gaze to Robin; the woman was trembling with excitement. If it weren't for her attempt to maintain her composure in front of the king, she would have already rushed to the Poneglyph and begun studying it.

"King Cobra, some time please," Ren said calmly, indicating that he needed some personal time with Robin. He didn't want him to overhear their next conversation.

"Take your time," Cobra nodded and left. After making sure Cobra wasn't eavesdropping using Observation Haki, Ren nodded to Robin.

Robin rushed to the Poneglyph and began examining it with great interest and curiosity. To her, this stone was the culmination of 20 years of her hard work.

Her trembling hand touched the Poneglyph, ensuring her imprint in those distinctive inscriptions, inscriptions she could read.

"Mom... I've started to approach my goal..." A sad and melancholic voice escaped her; tears gathered in the corners of her eyes.

Ren looked at the scene with a slight sigh. Robin's reaction was reasonable and natural.

She had been searching for this for decades, and now she has reached it "easily." All she had to do was join Ren. Thinking about it, it was surreal for Robin.

The innocent hadn't died, and she managed to find the Poneglyph. This was the best possible

outcome.

Quickly, Robin reverted to her archaeologist persona, ignoring her sadness, painful memories, and the moment of parting with her mother. She began reading the inscriptions in her mind.

Minutes passed, and neither Robin nor Ren moved from their spot, as if they were frozen in place.

For Robin, it was evident that her mind was fully focused on the complex symbols before her, trying to unravel the mystery surrounding them and uncover the secrets of this world.

With each symbol she deciphered and translated, her eyes widened gradually!

"Have you discovered what this Poneglyph is talking about, Robin?" Ren's voice came to snap her out of her dreams.

She turned towards him and saw his gaze. There wasn't any curiosity as she expected. Why? Don't tell me he... Does he know how to read Poneglyphs?!

The sudden realization struck Robin's mind. It was a bold guess, but she expected anything from the person in front of her.

No, he couldn't possibly learn how to read Poneglyphs. No one can teach it in this world except for me, and he's clearly not from Ohara. So it's impossible--

But Ren's voice interrupted her thoughts, shocking her: "The stone, it talks about Pluton,

right? One of the ancient weapons."

"!!!"

'He really knows!!' Robin thought in her mind as her eyes widened intensely while she stared at him. After several attempts to calm her emotions, she asked with a slightly trembling voice, "H-How can you read them?"

"Heh, you have to discover that for yourself." He smiled and then approached her, looking at

her disappointed expression, finding it somewhat cute.

Ren wasn't lying. He received a notification from the system a while ago:

¶[-Ding! Congratulations host, for completing the hidden mission "Interact with the First Poneglyph" and acquiring: "All Languages Skill".]

¶[-All Languages: A skill that allows the user to understand all languages in this world once activated, with no side effects.]

The description was short and simple. This skill was extremely useful. Although Robin was with him and could read the Poneglyph for him, reading it himself would be good too, right?

'He can really read Poneglyphs... That's unbelievable!' Robin murmured in astonishment. The shock wasn't from disbelief or anything like that but...

To understand, Robin had been carrying a heavy burden and great responsibility since she was

8 years old.

She was the only one who could read Poneglyph inscriptions thanks to the education she received from Ohara scholars, so if she died, this legacy would disappear.

Such information was mentioned in the Knowledge Tree books that were burned decades ago, so few people know about this.

Aside from its historical value, there's also something unique about this island.

It was lawless, a haven for pirates, untouched by the Marines or the World Government, making it a refuge for seafaring pirates with their dreams and ambitions.

The island was a lush green paradise, hit by the Knock Up Stream, and over time, it shrank significantly, taking the shape of a "U."

Mock Town is the only urban area throughout the island. Despite its resort-like appearance, it's considered a lawless city where all kinds of pirates gather and do as they please, far from the reach of the Marines and the World Government.

Another famous place in Jaya is South Grave.

South Grave was a rainforest located in the southeast of Jaya, inhabited by giant insects and southern birds, capable of controlling the former to protect the forest from invaders.

Except for Mouth Bay, which was a part of the ocean connecting the two ends of Jaya Island, there were no other places. So, it's safe to say it's just a small island with some historical value.

The Grand Line, Paradise.

On Skypiea, an intriguing scene unfolded.

They were Skypiea Police, personally tasked with delivering the ship to the priest!

Suddenly, a significant change occurred, altering the expressions of Skypiea Police.

The ship began flying on its own as if possessed by a ghost, instilling fear and horror in the faces of those aboard Elsama.

Elsama remained in the air for several seconds as if controlled by someone, then turned around and sped off towards its owner.

No one was around to witness the earlier massacre, but Enel managed to witness it, further fueling his interest in the ship!

This ship deserves to be a godly vessel like mine!

Enel widened his eyes in shock at the scene before him.

His body turned completely black thanks to Armament Haki, and his defensive clothes didn't suffer any scratches, so he looked as he was before.

Ohm, the priest who was about to be obliterated by Enel's lightning, looked at Ren with deep fear and astonishment, as Gan Fall did, who fell to the ground in a comedic scene.

"You!! You ant who doesn't know the greatness of this god!!" Enel shouted some cliché phrases, but in reality, he felt extreme shock.

Why didn't his divine powers affect him?

He didn't have a chance to think about it further because he sensed a severe danger. His eyes widened to the size of pins, and he turned into lightning before disappearing from his place. But before he disappeared in a second, Ren appeared again and waved his sword.

.

•

"One Sword Style: Death Blade!"

*Whoosh!*

*Boom!*

Ren had turned Versa into a sword a while ago, so a 10-meter-long blade shot towards Enel. Ren narrowed his eyes slightly when he saw Enel flicker and disappear from his place. *Boom!*

The blade, saturated with Armament Haki and the strength of Blood Metal, didn't cut Enel, but it cut the ground and a few trees in the forest, creating an empty square.

"Nice reaction," Ren suddenly became excited about this battle.

Since he wasn't Luffy, he couldn't be immune to Enel's Devil Fruit, but his Rubber resilience could be compensated with the Armament Haki he developed day by day with the help of Haki Guide.

Ren had a lot of information about Enel's power. He had read several articles about his strength, and all that information was stored in his photographic memory.

For example, Enel had strong Observation Haki, stronger than his own. He could predict the opponent's movements before they made them.

It could be considered a weaker version of Future Sight.

When he mentioned Future Sight, Ren suddenly remembered a character with a large mouth.

In addition to the strong Observation Haki, Enel could manipulate metals, especially gold. He could reshape his golden staff into anything he wanted thanks to the power of his Devil Fruit. His Lightning Fruit also gave him incredible speed, faster than Ren.

But Ren relied on endurance. Enel wouldn't be able to avoid his attacks, and he would eventually tire out.

He didn't really move at the speed of lightning in the literal sense, or else Ren would have been killed long ago.

Moving at lightning speed was theoretically possible for a user of the Lightning Fruit, but the human brain couldn't think at lightning speed and transmit orders to the limbs. As long as a Lightning Fruit user didn't find a way to develop his brain to this level, traveling at lightning speed would be just a dream.

Ren refocused on the battlefield, which had now become wider thanks to his attack, and looked at Enel, who was staring at him in shock and disdain.

"This attack... would have killed me!" Enel thought mentally. If he hadn't been fast enough, his body would have been cut into pieces!

Supposedly, he was immune to swords and guns, but his mantra demanded an immediate escape! Even his immortality wouldn't be useful!

[A/N: Enel believes that Elemental feature of his Devil Fruit, Logia, is some kind of immortality.]

Gan Fall looked at all this with increasing shock. First, this person managed to emerge unscathed against Enel's divine lightning, then he moved so quickly that he couldn't catch up and attacked Enel!

When he saw dozens of trees being cut down easily, and Enel's serious expression, hope grew stronger in Gan Fall's heart!

Conis watched the battle with concentration. She hated Enel for killing her father and oppressing her people. She wanted Ren to kill him and rid them of him!

Returning to Enel, he quickly regained his arrogant behavior, but there was a hint of caution

as he looked at Ren.

"Even if you're stronger than other ants, you'll still Just an ant!" He shouted loudly, then in the next moment, he grabbed his golden staff and attacked Ren.

*Screech!*

*Boom!*

*Screech!*

*Clang!*

The clang of iron collision echoing around could heared. Conis stared at the two figures in awe. Ren covered his hand with armament haki as he countered Enel's staff.

Enel hissed, then in the next moment, utilized his Devil Fruit power to increase the heat of the golden staff, smoke emanating from it, but Ren remained unfazed by the heat.

"Child's play," Ren sneered mockingly, intensifying the Armament haki on his hand as he glanced at the hot golden rod aimed at his face.

"I've got you!" Enel smirked wickedly, striking his staff forcefully at his opponent's face, but his expression shifted the next moment.

"Release my staff!" he yelled, trying to pry his weapon from Ren's grip, but to no avail. The physical strength difference between them wasn't significant, yet it wasn't negligible either. "Of course, I'll return it as long as you wish," Ren calmly replied, tightening his grip on the staff and lifting it in the air, Enel with it.

"Damn it!" Enel cursed, contemplating letting go of the staff but quickly retracted the thought; this was his powerful weapon, and he couldn't relinquish it just like that!

*Boom!*

*Boom!*

He didn't have a chance to think of a way to escape the situation. He was thrown to the ground while Ren still held the staff, slamming it into the ground alongside Enel.

"Argh!" an angry cry erupted from him. He didn't feel pain as he was a Logia user, but he felt humiliated being manipulated like this in front of everyone!

Ren released the staff, then grabbed Versa and was about to attack again, but Enel's enraged

voice interrupted him.

.

.

*Zap!*

*Boom!*

only a second before the attack struck Ren's body.

Zala fell silent, not daring to reply to Nami, realizing she was a newcomer to the crew, and Nami had seniority over her, not to mention, she was the strongest after Ren.

It seemed Conis and Gan Fall's expressions relaxed upon hearing Nami's words, then they quickly shifted their gaze back to the battlefield.

There, a dome of blue lightning and fire energy formed, shattering the ground beneath where Ren stood. The lightning energy disappeared quickly, replaced by a cloud of dust. Enel laughed maniacally as he looked at the scene in front him. His arrogant smirk returned to his face, but it froze in place when he used his mantra to see what happened to his enemy. "Stubborn!" A shiny black hand emerged from the dust pile, not empty but holding a red gun with numerous dragon patterns. One of the hand's fingers quickly pressed the trigger.

"What are these explosions, Aisa?!" Raki exclaimed in panic as she listened to the numerous explosions happening nearby.

They were heading towards the battle between Enel and Ren. Aisa had spotted them about to enter the fray earlier and informed the Shandians.

The mood in Shandia was somber due to Wyper's death; many strong warriors shed tears,

though Raki and Aisa didn't.

But they had to fight to reclaim their land. So they suppressed their grief and headed towards

Wyper! Shandian warriors were led by Braham and Kamakiri, while Rakki was in the lead carrying Aisa, leaping from tree to tree.

and they increased their speed. They had to cooperate with the man fighting Enel and assist

him!

Unfortunately, they didn't know their foolish thoughts might lead to their demise. They would only be a burden...

Returning to the devastated battlefield, several holes could be seen in the cracked ground,

and many Upper Yard trees were destroyed.

"Pant... Pant... bastard" A weak curse escaped Enel's tired mouth. He looked at Ren, who fired another red bullet and transformed into lightning to evade it. *Boom!* Taking advantage of the explosion's momentum, Enel decided to launch a counterattack. He transformed his golden staff into a serrated spear with three sharp blades

on its head. This was Enel's deadly weapon-Nonosama Bō!

"I can do that too," Ren said coolly, then transformed Versa from a pistol into a spear

resembling Enel's.

*Clang!* The weapons clashed, then Ren's physical strength prevailed over Enel's, forcing him to retreat after being kicked.

crashing to the ground. Before he could suppress the pain, he felt his mantra warning him of imminent danger.

"Shit!!" Enel roared loudly as he found himself in an unfavorable position. A shiny black foot

descended upon him, about to strike his skull and crush his face!

*buzz!*

Enel transformed back into lightning and escaped from Ren's foot. *Boom!!* Ren's foot smashed the ground where Enel had been standing just a second ago. He grimaced when he realized his attack had missed once again.

'His speed in escaping is beyond my Observation Haki. He has a stronger Haki than mine, so he has an advantage in this regard... Strange, Enel wasn't this powerful in the Canon, was he?', Ren thought in his mind as he held Versa, still in its serrated spear form, and pursued Enel.

"Enel's strengths are clear, Observation Haki and Devil Fruit. The Devil Fruit complements his Observation Haki, making him adept at escaping danger... But how did Luffy manage to defeat him?" Ren was lost in thought, but his body didn't stop attacking Enel and putting pressure on him.

He hadn't been serious until now; he wanted to experience fighting a user of a stronger Devil

Fruit to better understand the power of the Goro Goro no Mi. This fruit would soon belong to him or one of his girls after all.

His spear clashed with Enel's, pushing him back again. Ren found a gap in Enel's defense, and

it seemed the latter was not in a position to escape. He sighed and didn't use Armament Haki on his spear, cutting through Enel's body.

The spear passed through his body without causing any damage. Enel, who had previously

thought he was captured, was now ecstatic and thought to himself, "He can't hurt me!"

'Luffy couldn't defeat someone as powerful as Enel without learning any type of Haki. Hell,

he hadn't even learned Gear Second back then. Could it be that 'the world' itself helped him?',

Ren pondered.

As he was in the air, preparing to strike Enel, he thought to himself, 'I've read about such

plots in my previous life, 'the will of the world' choosing the destined child to control this world. And undoubtedly, that hero in this case is Luffy, but he's currently imprisoned. Could an incident occur that would lead to Luffy's liberation? Such plots were common in the stories I've read.' Ren furrowed his brows when thinking about this, realizing he needed to wrap

things up in Skypiea quickly and return to Luffy's side to prevent any unexpected incidents from happening.

He also guessed that Garp was about to arrive in Alabasta. The old man loved his grandson

and would comply with his conditions; he wouldn't dare do anything to him because he's a Warlord.

'In a world like this, absurd laws can restrict a powerful person like him, pathetic.' "Aaagh!! Damn it!!" Enel's painful scream snapped him out of his thoughts. He looked at his

foot covered in Armament Haki, which had struck Enel's abdominal muscles.

"It seems you couldn't escape this time." Ren lifted his foot off Enel's body and allowed him

to transform into lightning and appear several meters away. Enel's eyes were red with anger and pain as he held his abdomen, now marked with a blue bruise.

"Dare to hurt me, I am the god!" he roared in anger, then struck the drums behind his back

.

angrily.

its staff form, towards Ren.

The Grand Line, Paradise.

Skypiea, In the upper Yard.

*Boom!*

The lightning dragon struck Ren, causing the surrounding areas to explode and spreading fires amidst the burning trees.

Enel smirked wickedly as he witnessed the scene, exhausted to the point of forgetting to activate his own mantra. He had used his demonic fruit power excessively today, making it difficult for him to read the electrical waves in the air.

"Why are you smiling?" A cold voice emerged from the smoke as Enel gazed at Ren, whose clothes were slightly burnt, the upper part of his attire spoiled, but quickly fixed by Ren himself.

Enel could see Ren's blackened body returning to its normal state, while his burnt clothes reverted to their original condition.

"Shit!" Enel cursed loudly, on the verge of fleeing. Escaping this monster, he would take his ship and leave this place!

But to his misfortune, he wasn't given that chance. Ren appeared before him swiftly, using Shave to transform Versa into an iron club.

*Boom!*

Enel's body was thrown backward several meters, the ground shattered from the impact as he fell, coughing up blood.

All the spectators watched with various expressions. Among Ren's crew, the girls looked at him with confidence, pride, and admiration.

Conis sighed with relief, suppressing the shock in her eyes. She had witnessed the supposedly invincible god being struck down to the point of displaying expressions of horror.

"W-wow!" Gan Fall exclaimed with shock and joy. Could this be the end of the tyrannical god's reign that lasted for six years finally?

But what if the one who defeated him is worse...

What will they do? Resist? They didn't even dare to resist Enel, let alone the one who struck him down like a child.

Gan Fall shook his head, trying not to be pessimistic.

"Too powerful!" Raki looked at the battle, or rather, the "bullying," with wide eyes. Was this person playing with Enel like this? She swore she saw the terrifying god's expressions turn to horror and shock before being pitifully struck down.

"Blue-haired oni-tchan is amazing!" Aisa cheered, standing naturally among Ren's crew. No one on this island liked Enel, except for the army of God led by the four priests, who had already been dealt with.

Braham, Kamakiri, and Genbo watched the battle in shock. At this rate, this person didn't need their help, right? So they could only watch from afar as he defeated him, then demand their land back afterward, right?

'I just hope he isn't like Enel,'

Kamakiri thought in his mind, but in the next moment, he saw the blue-haired man turn towards him, look at him briefly, and then... smile? Why?

But immediately after... his eyes went blank.

"Attack Enel with me! Kill him to reclaim Shandora's light!"

"Roar!!"

The warriors of Shandia were confused by Kamakiri's sudden outburst, but the next moment, they attacked with him without hesitation, all aiming their weapons at Enel.

He had already risen and escaped from Ren to a safe distance, or rather, Ren allowed him to escape. He still had his role to play.

"Fire!!" Kamakiri commanded, but the stiffness in his eyes didn't change. No one noticed his strange condition; his voice remained normal, seeming excited to kill the enemy.

*Boom!*

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

*Crash!*

Dozens of bullets and shells pierced Enel's body, but they passed through him without causing harm. A long spear even pierced his head, but Enel just smiled, unharmed.

But he was annoyed, extremely annoyed. Ren had already angered him by striking him badly and not even allowing him to rest. And now, these ants came out of nowhere and dared to attack him?

He gritted his teeth in anger, then the drums sounded behind him.

"Wait, what are you doing?!" Raki screamed, not understanding what these men were doing. Eneel was already on the verge of defeat, why risk their lives at this time? It was completely meaningless!

She didn't attack with them because she was protecting Aisa and couldn't approach the enemy during the process as a little girl. Nor could she leave her alone because Aisa said the remnants of God's army were coming.

"God's Lightning: God's Judgment!"

---

Enel screamed madly, while Ren watched the scene coldly. In the next moment, the Eastern Lightning Dragon emerged again, heading towards Kamakiri and the remaining warriors.

"Ahh!!"

"Save me!"

"Dammit!"

The warriors of Shandia screamed as the lightning dragon passed through them, burning and piercing their bodies. Several warriors were killed in an instant while the rest fled.

Braham looked at all this in horror, suddenly regretting obeying Kamakiri's order to attack. He thought Eneel no longer had the power for such an attack, but it turned out he was wrong.

Genbo felt the same way, but what was strange was that Kamakiri's expression remained unchanged; his eyes stayed fixed.

In the next moment, Kamakiri grabbed his dagger and stabbed towards Enel, but the latter scoffed and seized his golden staff, transforming it into a trident.

"Splash!"

"Crack!"

Enel sliced Kamakiri's body in half in an instant, the hot blades of the trident piercing his body and shattering his spine.

Enel didn't mind the blood on his face or his lethal trident; he swiftly pierced Brighum's head with elegance. He used every bit of his remaining physical strength to eliminate these ants.

Since I'm going to die, why not take some of them with me?

With these thoughts in his mind, Enel pulled the trident from Braham's head and turned to the other warrior, Genbo. In the next moment, he thrust the trident into Genbo's abdomen until it emerged from the other side.

Enel lifted Genbo's body suspended on the trident in the air, gazed at Genbo's pained expression, and grinned madly before slamming his body on the ground, turning his head

into a pile of flesh and blood.

These attacks happened in mere seconds; Raki couldn't even react to seeing the tribe's strongest members being slaughtered like sheep, while Aisa screamed and covered her eyes in

horror.

"Hahaha..." Enel laughed maniacally, seeming to have lost his mind. He glanced at Raki, who

wasn't far from him. The rest of Shandia's warriors had fled in fear upon witnessing Enel's madness, so Raki was his next target.

Quickly, he moved his exhausted body towards her while holding his blood-stained trident.

*Slash!*

He struck quickly towards her; Aisa cried as she watched the trident that was about to sever

become alone.

breathing his last breath.

Ren stared at this scene in silence, then approached Enel and sat in front of him. Eneel didn't react, no, he wasn't able to.

He stared at the sky as if he could recall a very old memory.

[30 years ago]

In an island in the sky called Berka, a typical scene took place in one of the narrow and hidden

streets.

"Hit him harder!"

"Why don't you have wings?!"

"He must be some kind of monster!"

"Did you bring the sewage water, Roksha? Pour it on him!"

Five young children could be seen surrounding a seven-year-old boy, who had blonde hair

and long pointed ears, much longer than an ordinary person's.

He suffered a brutal beating while receiving insults; he was always subjected to racism in this

place.

Just because he didn't have wings like everyone else, just because he didn't have normal ears like everyone else.

The child Enel closed his eyes and cried silently while enduring the pain and the dirty fluids he

was soaked in.

After a while, the children left after they got tired of beating him. Eneel left the narrow alley

saw him standing in the street.

"Someone throw him somewhere!"

In no time, Enel found himself being chased away from the street. "Dammit!" he cursed loudly, before deciding to go and clean himself in one of the rivers.

He headed to Berka Forest, and then found the great Monzõ River that spread throughout Berka and bathed in the cold water.

He gritted his teeth as he resisted the cold, removed his dirty clothes, and washed them to

wear them again.

"I need to find another set of clothes," Enel sighed when he saw the state of his clothes, torn

everywhere and with dirt stains that couldn't be washed out.

After cleaning himself, he didn't leave the forest. This was where he lived, after all.

He had no family; his father had died from a chronic illness when he was four, while his

mother left him to go with her new husband because he asked her to.

He walked a little into the forest, seeming to have a specific destination in mind. After a few

minutes, he arrived at a rundown wooden house.

"Oh! How are you, Maxim? Did you miss me?" he laughed as he looked at his pet.

"Meow~!" A meow came from the cat's mouth, indicating its longing for its owner.

Since Enel rescued this cat a year ago, it became his only family. He took care of it and provided it with food by fishing and hunting small animals like rabbits. He lived his life in the forest, so Enel naturally knew how to avoid dangerous areas.

Time passed, days turned into weeks, weeks into months and years.

.

.

13-year-old Einil looked at his cat's body, silent tears streaming down his cheeks.

"This Fucking creature deserves what happened to it. How dare it scratch my foot? I am a priest of the god!" A grown man wearing white clothes and holding a spear laughed. The spear was still dripping blood, indicating that he was the one who caused the hole in the

cat's body.

.

Enel carried his cat's body and left for the forest without uttering a word. "Someday, I'll leave for Fairy Vearth, Maxim! I promise!"

He buried his cat's body, tears still flowing from his eyes.

[After 24 years]

Enel grew up and became a man, but the coldness and madness in his eyes grew stronger.

"With this, I will change everything!"

He looked at the strange yellow fruit in his hand that he found in the forest.

'It's likely that this is the Devil Fruit that grants supernatural divine abilities!' Without

hesitation, he took a bite of it.

Chapter 91: [91] Light of Shandora [1]

[2400 Words]

The Grand Line, Skypiea.

In the upper Yard.

[Miss System, extract Enel's Devil Fruit.]

Ren stood in front of Enel's corpse and commanded.

[Goro Goro no Mi fruit has been placed in the Infinity Space, master.]

He glanced at Enel's body one last time, then bowed and touched the golden staff that disappeared the next moment.

"It's time to take control of Skypiea."

With this idea in mind, Ren approached the astonished Raki and glanced briefly at the girl, Aisa.

"Um...hi," Raki stammered, her heart pounding as she had just escaped death, and then things happened quickly, seeing Enel breathe his last.

"Thank you... for saving Raki, Oni-chan," Aisa suddenly said gratefully, wiping her eyes that had reddened from crying.

Ren blinked when he heard "Oni-chan," but then he returned to his calmness and said:

"It's good that you're okay. We'll talk later."

*Whoosh!*

In the next moment, Ren disappeared from in front of them and reappeared tens of meters away thanks to Shave.

"You're the former god, Gan Fall, right? I have something important to discuss with you," Ren said, looking at the old man who stood incredulous that Enel had indeed died.

"Y-yes... how did you know... never mind, what do you want, um...?"

"It's Ren," Ren told him his name and then said directly, "Let's be clear and tell you what I want."

He paused for a moment and continued:

"I will rule this place."

Conis, Gan Fall, Raki, and the rest of the Shandians froze upon hearing what he said, while his crew members were aware of his intentions.

Before Gan Fall could speak, Ren's voice came again: "I've heard that this land has witnessed a war that has not stopped for hundreds of years, claiming many lives. Then Enel came and ruled with an iron fist. I plan to put an end to this unnecessary cycle of violence."

Ren began delivering a speech that would move anyone to trust his words, claiming he would ensure stability in the land and stop the war, as well as guarantee the rights of all parties, whether Skypiéns or Shandiéns.

"So, I hope you will cooperate with me, Gan Fall-san, by conveying my words and goals to the people of Skypiea."

Ren looked at the tense old man with a friendly smile, but behind that smile, he was already prepared with an alternative plan if his offer was refused. It must be said that things wouldn't end well for Gan Fall if he refused...

He sought Gan Fall's help because he had been the former god of Skypiea for several years, so the Skypiéns trusted him greatly. Convincing them would be easier.

Of course, Ren wouldn't rely solely on this to win their loyalty; he would make some moves on his own. Enel's body and the unconscious priests would assist him in that.

Gan Fall looked at Ren with tension. Would he really keep his promise? Would he not become like Enel, or perhaps worse?

'But he's a pirate...' Gan Fall sighed inwardly with doubt. He knew pirates couldn't be trusted, but his sense and experience in judging people told him that this person wouldn't harm Skypiea without reason.

'But what if there is a reason?' Gan Fall trembled at the mere thought of that.

"What do you say, Gan Fall-san?" Ren's voice brought him out of his thoughts; he still maintained a friendly smile.

Gan Fall realized he had no choice but to comply with his words. He had killed Enel and freed them from his rule. As for whether the Skypiéns would accept this, he didn't know their reaction to it.

He only feared that Ren would impose himself if they opposed him, just as Enel did.

"I accept, Lord Ren."

Ren smiled. Wasn't this too quick?

But he was satisfied with the old man's flattery. He patted his shoulder and then turned to his crew members, looking at Conis.

"Yo~, I've avenged you." He patted her shoulder, seeing her quietly shed tears, and then hugged her without hesitation, starting to cry.

The girls stared in shock at Conis clinging to Ren, while the latter laughed and thought to himself, 'It seems that the mission related to her will be easy.'

"Nami, Nojiko, Mikita, the three of you are responsible for taking the priests to Angel Island and waiting for me there," he looked at the three girls and assigned them the task.

"Aye, Captain!" Mikita said with a smile, while Nami and Nojiko nodded.

"Robin, you'll come with me... but before that, I need to take care of some pending matters."

Without waiting for a response from Robin and the others, he disappeared from his place and reappeared tens of meters away.

The God's Army was Enel's main force.

It consisted of the four priests who had been defeated earlier and the Divine Soldiers led by Commander Yama.

In addition to Commander Yama, there were Lieutenants Kotori and Hotori, Satori's

brothers.

With the addition of the Divine Soldiers, there were fifty people in total.

They had heard the sounds of the previous battle for a long time, so they left the temple to

confront the enemy.

But they were shocked by the scene that met them there: there, their powerful, invincible god lay on the ground, dead...

The God's Army trembled and felt lost for a moment. What should they do now?

Should they escape? Where to? The person who killed their god is coming for their lives.

And he surely came.

Ren looked at the fifty soldiers in front of him with amusement on his face, transforming Versa into a black and red whip in his hand.

"Oh, killer of the god Enel!" Yama shouted, his eyes glowing red with fervor and worship, then ordered the soldiers to attack.

"Let's finish this quickly," Ren muttered, not wanting to waste any more time, and began striking the soldiers in front of him.

But he didn't kill them.

*Creek~*

The sound of the whip echoed through the air as Ren whipped the soldiers, causing them intense pain, forcing them to fall to the ground and not rise again.

*Slash!!*

"Ugh!" Yama cried out, attempting a counterattack, but was quickly subdued along with the

others.

"We're done here," Ren muttered, then turned to his crew members who had watched

everything calmly.

"Go, Nojiko, Nami, Mikita and the others, to Angel Island and wait for me there. Don't forget to take care of Conis too."

After seeing the girls assure him that they would do so, he turned his attention to Gan Fall and said, "I want you to enlist the White Berets and transport the remnants of the army to a safe place... a place where the angry people of Skypiea won't reach. Understand?"

"Yes, I'll make sure of that..." Gan Fall didn't understand Ren's reason for wanting these people, but he didn't argue. His concern was not to upset the new ruler who was even more powerful than Enel in a ridiculous way, or they might be doomed to failure.

Suna Suna no Mi is a Logia-type Devil Fruit that allows the user to create, control, and

transform into sand at will, turning the user into a sand-human.

This Devil Fruit resembles a spherical cactus with two short arms pointing upward, with a

swirling pattern on it. It has a green, T-shaped stem protruding from the top of the fruit...the

right side of the stem is longer and slightly curved.

[Image Here]

In fact, this Devil Fruit is powerful and has many abilities and uses. Besides forming and

controlling sand in large quantities, users of this Devil Fruit have immunity to ordinary attacks like all Logia-type Devil Fruit users.

Users can allow physical attacks and non-Seastone projectiles to pass through their bodies to

prevent damage, or fly by transforming the lower part of their bodies into jets of flowing

sand, providing them with a huge advantage in defense and mobility during combat or pursuit.

Additionally, creating sand grants the user diverse offensive capabilities; the user can create

blades of sand in various shapes and sizes, large sandstorms that can travel long distances, and even whirlpools that trap and pull opponents underground... These techniques are further enhanced in terms of power and range in a desert environment,

where sand is plentiful and can be mixed with the user's sand to increase the range of attacks.

In addition to basic elemental control, the Devil Fruit exhibits a unique additional ability: fluid absorption, allowing the user to dry out anything they touch and even extend the mentioned effect to their immediate surroundings, leading to drying out vast areas of land like drought. Usually, this ability poses a threat to the lives of any living beings, causing plants, animals, or people to suffer from dehydration, in addition to converting mineral matter into more sand particles for the user to manipulate.

By the way, this means that the user is also immune to caves, as they can easily escape from underground by turning themselves and the solid rocks around them into sand. Moreover, the user of this Devil Fruit can detect other substances within a mass of sand, such as underground rivers, and use them to their advantage by creating mobile sand traps. Suna Suna no Mi is one of the few Logia-type fruits that can naturally counter its weakness, and its power becomes stronger in desert areas where water is almost absent.

"But this Devil Fruit has a weakness..." Ren continued his explanation. Vivi was surprised to

learn that the Devil Fruit he planned to give her was the same one that belonged to Crocodile. Nevertheless, she easily agreed.

Especially when she heard about the power of this Devil Fruit from Ren's explanation.

No wonder Crocodile was so powerful! Especially in a desert country like Alabasta, which is

considered a favorite fighting ground for Sand Logia users.

"The main weakness of this Logia power lies in exposure to liquids: specifically, the user's sand turns into a solid substance upon contact with liquids, such as water or blood." "This prevents the sand from dispersing and thus temporarily cancels the user's intangibility (according to the concept of wet sand sticking together due to surface tension). However, this aspect of the Devil Fruit's power seems inconsistent regarding the type of liquid used and

how it comes into contact with the user."

Ren turned and began walking towards the palace, quickly followed by the girls. Garp looked at Ren's back with a dangerous glint in his eyes before calming down and ordering the

Marines to follow him.

....

Naturally, it was Ren and the others.

As soon as Garp saw Luffy, he felt relieved and rushed to the sleeping boy's side. After confirming that he wasn't injured, he sighed in relief and tried to wake him up.

But he couldn't. No matter how much he slapped or shook him, Luffy showed no signs of waking up. He even splashed water on him, but it had no effect.

If he couldn't see Luffy's chest rising and falling, showing he was breathing, he would have thought he was dead.

"What did you do to him?" He turned to Ren, who was sitting on the couch with Nami, Nojiko, Mikita, and Leila beside him.

After hearing Garp's question, Ren looked at him and replied in a bored tone, "Just a trick to make him sleep for a while. I can't be sure you Marines won't break your deal with me, so this

is just a small precaution, Marine Hero. Once we complete our deal and you leave this kingdom, your grandson will wake up naturally."

Ren spoke without stopping, explaining the reasons for Luffy's sedation. His words were very logical, so much so that Garp couldn't find anything to refute.

He wanted to talk to his grandson now and interrogate him about what Ren did to him,

whether it was psychological or physical torture. He would make sure Ren paid the price.

He wouldn't kill him, but he would make him experience the fist of pain. But... his little plan failed before it even started.

Garp thought for a moment and came up with an idea. He said calmly and seriously, "And what guarantees me that Luffy will wake up after I leave Alabasta? Let him wake up here once I hand over the goods, and the deal will be fair!"

"Sigh... I won't break my promise with the Marines if I want to keep my position as a Warlord.

Do you think I went through all this trouble to gain Warlord status only to lose it overnight?" Ren laughed at Garp's foolishness. He remembered that intelligence and planning were not among the old man's strengths.

Chapter 111: [110] Another Soul
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2600 Words]

In Alabasta Kingdom, the horizon was painted with the enchanting colors of the sunset. The sun, that great fiery disk, slowly sank into the sea of golden sands, creating a canvas of intermingling orange, red, and purple hues.

As the sun dipped lower, its light faded, casting long shadows over the meadows and sand dunes.

On the summit of the royal palace, Ren stood gazing at the horizon, contemplating the last moments of the day. The wind brushing against his face carried warm breezes, laden with the scent of dry earth and distant meadows. In the sky, stars began to twinkle one by one, heralding the arrival of night.

When the sun had completely disappeared, the sky transformed into a deep blue. The crescent moon slowly rose, casting a faint silver glow over Alabasta Kingdom.

The sounds of the night began to emerge: the whispers of the wind through the trees and the chirping of nocturnal birds awakening.

In the quiet streets of the kingdom, lanterns lit up with their warm glow, creating pathways of soft light that reflected off the white walls of the houses. People began to return home, leaving behind a day full of activity, noise, and hard work.

Thus, in complete tranquility, Alabasta Kingdom welcomed the night, embracing its calm and magic.

Ren looked at Leila. The two stood at the highest point of the royal palace, watching the star- studded sky.

Suddenly, Ren said, "I think the war announcement will come soon."

"Maybe in a few weeks at most, Ren... are you ready for it?" Leila looked at him with concern, thinking about the powerful individuals who might target her beloved brother, unable to help feeling anxious.

"Yes, I am ready, but there are always things I can't predict... I have to be wary of Whitebeard, that old monster might target me specifically." Ren's face showed no sign of worry; he was confident in his strength but knew there was a gap between him and Whitebeard that he couldn't bridge just yet.

Not to mention, the old, injured Sea Emperor. He wouldn't stand a chance against an admiral.

Whether in Haki or physical strength, he was still a few steps behind when compared to the admirals, let alone sea emperors.

Time passed quickly as Ren and his crew were deeply immersed in intense training. Under Ren's vigilant supervision, no one could escape the rigorous regimen, not even the former Baroque Works agents.

Initially, Ren planned to wait until after the Summit War, hoping for a year or more of stability to train his fleet leaders and recruit powerful individuals. However, he decided to accelerate the process.

Nami did not complain about the harsh training. Seeing her sword imbued with lightning and Haki was exhilarating for her. It signified her growing strength and usefulness to Ren, ensuring she would stay with him and keep pace, never falling behind.

Nojiko focused on improving her sniping and shooting skills. Recently, she experimented with new weapons, including rifles, small pistols, and small cannons resembling bazookas.

Her physical strength improved steadily. Nojiko never missed a training session and showed determination similar to Nami's.

The yandere's will was frighteningly strong.

Kaya spent her time in Alabasta's library studying medicine. Thanks to the medical talent scroll she consumed, she found herself on the path to creating medicines for previously incurable diseases. She also studied poisons and their types at Ren's request.

Vivi concentrated on her training to avoid feeling inadequate. Watching her crewmates grow stronger daily, she refused to stand still. Although her father seemed reluctant about her training, he remained silent, knowing she would ignore him anyway.

Zala, Mikita, and Robin trained in various aspects of their abilities. swordsmanship skills and physical strength.

There was nothing else of note, so Ren continued to await the summons.

With only one day left until the execution, the day passed. Meanwhile, in Alabasta, a Marine

ship could be seen docking at the port.

Several Marines disembarked from the ship, led by a woman in Marine attire.

Ren noticed them thanks to his Observation Haki from the moment they set foot in Alabasta.

He was mildly surprised upon seeing the identity of the woman leading the group but remained composed, waiting for them to approach.

After a few minutes, the Marines reached the royal palace and confronted Ren. "Warlord, Black Death Ren, you have been summoned to Marineford to contribute to the war

against the Whitebeard Pirates."

Hina delivered her message with coldness, waiting for Ren's response. Inside, she hoped Ren would reject the summons, which would mean revoking his Warlord status. However, Ren disappointed her, saying, "I accept the summons. May I bring two companions

with me?"

Hina suppressed her irritation and replied, "Yes, each Warlord may bring only two

companions to assist in this war as per higher orders."

Ren smiled with relief and said, "Then it's settled. You may leave for the port first. I will join

you shortly."

Hina felt annoyed because Ren spoke to her as if they were old friends rather than enemies who sought each other's demise. Nonetheless, she nodded and withdrew obediently.

After Hina departed, Ren retrieved a Den Den Mushi and said, "Nami, Mikita, prepare to

depart!"

"Aye, Captain!" they responded in unison.

"Pirate Empress Boa Hancock, do not make me repeat myself. If you refuse this summons

from the Marines, your status as a Warlord will be revoked!" Momonga shouted, his leg bleeding. His face contorted in disgust as he glanced at the petrified statues, once his

subordinates.

"Hmph... even if I refuse this summons, they'll forgive me, won't they?"

"That's because... I am beautiful~"

The first volume titled <Destiny Breake> has ended.

The second volume titled <Rise Amidst Chaos> will begin.

This volume consists of 200,000 words. I will take a short break and come back with better chapters.

Chapter 113: [112] Arrival at Marineford
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Beginning of Volume Two: Rise Through Chaos.

[2500 Words]

Nami was overjoyed. Why? Because her beloved captain had decided to take her with him to Marineford to participate in the war against the Whitebeard Pirates.

In the past, she would have been terrified, confused, and might have even refused to go. But the current Nami was very different. She was strong; the power of her Devil Fruit alone made her stronger than everyone in the first half of the Grand Line.

Moreover, she had a medium mastery of the Thunder Fruit, which multiplied her power several times over.

Her physical strength and skill in swordsmanship were also impressive. If Nami claimed to be the strongest person in the Sin Pirates crew after Ren, no one would argue.

Now, she was determined to dedicate her strength to her beloved captain. She would protect him, even though she knew she was weaker. She would sacrifice herself for him in a moment of crisis. That was the extent of her resolve.

Similarly, Mikita shared Nami's determination. She had decided to be Ren's sword and shield, not just in this war but for her entire life.

Ren looked at Alabasta, gradually disappearing from his sight, and sighed softly. He was now on his way to Marineford. The live broadcast of Ace's execution would begin in just a few hours.

Ren wouldn't have made it on time if he had taken the regular route, but the World Government had its shortcuts that made sailing much easier.

He realized the benefits of having a powerful organization backing you. Difficult tasks became easier, and the precious time one would otherwise waste sailing across the vast ocean was significantly reduced.

If Ren hadn't become a Warlord of the Sea, he wouldn't have had the right to access the Gates of Justice.

The Gate of Justice is a massive structure spanning specific areas of the Grand Line, connecting the main Navy headquarters to important locations like Enies Lobby, Impel Down, and Marineford. The World Government has three known main gates:

First, the Gate of Justice at Enies Lobby, located at the entrance to Enies Lobby, an important Navy base. Ships pass through this gate to reach Enies Lobby Island without facing the dangerous currents surrounding it, ensuring the safe arrival of prisoners and resources. Second, the Gate of Justice at Impel Down, leading to Impel Down prison, one of the most heavily guarded prisons in the world. Ships passing through this gate avoid the strong currents and dangers surrounding the area, ensuring a smooth arrival.

Finally, the Gate of Justice at Marineford, which was Ren's current destination. This gate is located at the entrance to Marineford, the main Navy headquarters. Ships pass through it to reach the headquarters quickly and safely.

The Gates of Justice work in coordination with special sea currents to shorten the distances of sea journeys and expedite the safe arrival of ships to their destinations.

These special sea currents are well-known to the Navy. They travel at high speeds and provide shortcuts between important locations. The Navy controls these currents and uses them effectively to transport its ships at high speeds across the seas. There are several types of these currents:

Tidal currents, which rely on tidal movements and allow ships to move quickly in a specific direction during certain periods. Then there are deep ocean currents, which are stable and steady in the depths of the ocean and allow ships to travel at high speeds without being affected by surface waves that slow them down.

Additionally, there are marine wind currents. The Navy ingeniously exploits these currents to take advantage of strong winds that propel ships at high speed across the ocean.

By coordinating between the Gates of Justice and these special sea currents, the Navy maintains high speed and efficiency in its movements, ensuring a quick response to any sudden or planned event.

Ren analyzed the information stored in his photographic memory. After finishing, his mind drifted to the remaining crew members in Alabasta.

'I temporarily gave Robin the right to control Elsama so they could escape if something urgent happened, so there shouldn't be anything to worry about,' he thought aloud, then looked at Hina, who was staring at him with dagger-like eyes.

'It has been a while since I talked to Tashigi. Has it been five days?' He touched his chin in thought, then looked at Nami, who was watching the sea with a beautiful smile on her face. He couldn't help but feel curious and ask, "You seem to be in a good mood?"

"Yes... it's just that being almost alone with you feels like a date or something-" she said shyly. Ren faintly smiled while thinking, 'A date? It's a war against the strongest pirate group in the world...'

Seeing Nami's calm and indifferent attitude towards the war, Ren couldn't help but be moved by the character development she had undergone.

"What about you, Mikita? Are you nervous?" Ren asked, turning his gaze to the smiling blonde. She shook her head lightly and laughed, "Kyahahaha~ Throughout my career as a bounty hunter, I've heard about the strength and ruthlessness of the Whitebeard Pirates. To think I'll be fighting them in just a few hours... destiny is truly unpredictable!"

"You're right," Ren said, patting her head before shifting his attention back to the newspaper he had read several hours earlier.

'Moria vanishes under mysterious circumstances, and Blackbeard takes his place as a Warlord... so he's following the same plan as in the original storyline.'

When Ren first read the news, he was thrilled because it meant there wouldn't be any variables disrupting his advantage of knowing the future. Blackbeard in this timeline still had the same intentions as his previous self.

'Jinbe is likely being held in Impel Down. Given his character and admiration for Whitebeard, he will try to stop the war just like before... but it's useless,' Ren thought about the fish-man currently in Impel Down.

'But Luffy is the biggest wildcard in the upcoming events. Since Garp took him away, he won't meet Hancock and go to Impel Down to free powerful prisoners who would help him rescue his brother.' Ever since Ren decided to act against Luffy, he had stopped caring about him. The only thing still valuable to Ren was Luffy's Devil Fruit. Garp would likely suppress Luffy and put him under strict guard, preventing him from going to sea again.

Ren pondered all of this as time passed. Seconds turned into minutes, and minutes into hours. The atmosphere on the Navy ship was calm. Ren occasionally spoke with Nami and Mikita. Nami loved staying close to Ren, which made Mikita jealous, while Hina watched them with a red face. No one knew if her face was red from embarrassment or anger.

....

-Boa Hancock POV-

*Sigh...*

I have to leave the island and go to that filthy place filled with disgusting men.

I really didn't want to go, but the old woman Gloriosa kept urging me, saying that my participation in this war would save the Isle of Women.

I stood on the deck of my ship, the Kuja Pirates' vessel, looking at the calm sea filled with Sea Kings sleeping in the depths. Fortunately, my ship was pulled by Sea King serpents, so we wouldn't encounter any incidents.

We had set sail a few hours ago and were about to reach our destination: Marineford, the headquarters of the Navy, the World Government's dogs.

Tch! How I wish this war would destroy Marineford and everyone in it.

"Hibihime-sama! We've arrived!" I heard my subordinate's voice. I nodded calmly and looked coldly at the Gate of Justice opening before me.

"You must continue alone from here, Warlord Hancock."

I looked at the source of the voice. It was the disgusting Vice Admiral who had come to

summon me.

He was on his ship, sailing alongside my Kuja Pirates' vessel. Naturally, I wouldn't board a

ship full of filthy men.

"Hmph, I know."

I glared at him coldly and glanced at my subordinates, who started to head back to the Isle of Women after bidding me farewell.

In a few minutes, I was at Marineford, and the scene I witnessed was truly astonishing.

...

-General POV-

"We've arrived!" Ren heard Hina's stern voice and looked at the Gate of Justice slowly opening. The Navy ship passed through the gate and entered Marineford.

He had finally set foot in the Navy headquarters, Marineford!

Ren looked at the soldiers filling Marineford's plaza. Their numbers were increasing every

second, showing how prepared the Navy was for this war.

One hundred thousand Navy soldiers in total!

The Navy's formation consisted of elite soldiers from the Grand Line and the four seas, along with Rear Admirals and Vice Admirals, and finally, the highest combat power of the Navy: the three Admirals and the Fleet Admiral!

Of course, the Navy hero Garp, who was on par with an Admiral, could not be ignored. The Seven Warlords of the Sea were also a strong and important addition to the Navy. The

Navy had chosen to fight pirates with pirates. Even if one of the Warlords died in this battle, they wouldn't feel any remorse or sadness-they would simply find someone else. "Wow..." Mikita gazed in awe at the massive crowd of Navy soldiers.

"So many..." Nami suppressed the tension in her body and gripped her sword tightly, determined not to let herself become overwhelmed.

At that moment, Hina looked at Ren, Nami, and Mikita and said, "Hina informed the Fleet Admiral of your arrival. He says your subordinates should stand with the rest of the soldiers while you stand alongside the other Warlords." Hina then turned to leave.

Upon hearing this, Ren understood why all these troublesome preparations were necessary. The execution scene would be broadcast worldwide, so the navy had to appear organized and strong to the public. As one of the warlords, he was responsible for this to some extent.

"I understand," he said calmly, then looked at Nami and Mikita, who jumped off the navy ship that continued sailing to dock at a distant port.

"We will part ways here and regroup when the war begins, girls." Ren held them tightly and

kissed them quickly, not caring about the embarrassed expression on Mikita's face, then turned to find his place.

It didn't take long to find four people lined up. Although the line didn't have many people, it

was clearly special.

After all, the warlords were gathered here: Pirate Empress Boa Hancock, Heavenly Demon Donquixote Doflamingo, Hawk-Eye Dracule Mihawk, and Tyrant Bartholomew Kuma. Including Ren, there were a total of five warlords at Marineford, with only Jinbei and the newly appointed Teach missing.

On the high execution platform, Sengoku frowned when he saw that Teach had not yet arrived.

Was he ignoring the summons, or did he no longer want his title as a warlord? This was

illogical since he had only just received it a day ago.

Sengoku looked at Ren, finally meeting this mysterious pirate face to face. He glanced at his friend Garp, whose expression was grim as he stared at Ren...

Sengoku sighed slightly in worry. He had to prevent Garp from doing anything reckless.

With this thought in mind, he went to talk to his subordinates to coordinate the war.

Back to Ren, when he saw the four warlords lined up in front of him, he didn't lose his composure and calmly walked past them.

'Seeing the characters I watched and loved in my past life standing in front of me is still a bit strange... hehe.' Ren smiled inwardly and walked silently. He could see Doflamingo looking at him with a malicious smile and a gaze filled with hostility and resentment.

When he looked at Hancock, he was internally shocked. Staring at the beautiful woman in front of him for a long time made him feel as if he was unconsciously attracted to her. 'Is this the power of the Love-Love Fruit?' he thought to himself, becoming fully alert inside.

In the next moment, he activated the < Cold Heart > skill, and the coldness on his face intensified, causing the warlords, except for Kuma, to look at him with a hint of surprise. When Ren is in the < Cold Heart > state, he doesn't care about the expressions and feelings of

the people in front of him. He quickly found his place in the line and stood quietly, the third tallest person in the group after Kuma and Doflamingo.

His weapon < Versa>, in sword form, was hanging at his waist, emanating a demonic aura.

Mihawk looked at Ren's sword with interest and then said in a powerful voice, "Are you a swordsman?"

Ren's expression didn't change, but he replied in a cold voice, "Yes."

"I See."

Mihawk nodded and stopped talking.

'What the hell is wrong with this man? Why doesn't he show any sign of amazement upon

seeing my beauty?' Hancock screamed in her heart. The Love-Love Fruit she had eaten enhanced her attractiveness, making people who saw her for the first time unable to resist looking again. But this person gave her one look before his expression turned cold and he

ignored her.

'Is he perhaps using Haki to cancel the effect of my Devil Fruit's power? No, that's not possible... It requires a terrifying level of Haki mastery.'

While Hancock was engrossed in her thoughts, Doflamingo laughed maliciously and looked at

Ren, then said in a voice carrying a hint of hostility, "Did you kill my toys that were on Jaya

Island?"

Ren looked at him with the same cold expression, knowing he was talking about Bellamy the

Hyena. He said, "So what if I did?"

The cold tone made Doflamingo narrow his eyes under his glasses and say, "I'm afraid you'll

have to compensate me for that, fufufu~."

"Make me."

Ren's statement made Doflamingo's veins bulge on his forehead in anger. Hancock looked at

the two with an arrogant expression, not bothering to watch their dispute, while Mihawk seemed interested in seeing Ren's swordsmanship.

"Fufufu... the newcomers are fierce these days." Doflamingo smiled but wasn't happy at all.

He raised his hand, looking ready.

"You can confirm it yourself." Under Doflamingo's aggressive movement, Ren's expression

didn't change, making the Heavenly Demon's face even uglier. He gritted his teeth and... "Fufufu, forget it." Doflamingo laughed and relaxed his hands, looking at Ren and thinking to himself, 'We'll see what happens after this war!'

Sengoku, who was about to intervene and reprimand the two, was speechless. These two were

about to fight just a moment ago, so what the hell happened? What about his plan to display his authority in front of 100,000 soldiers?

Chapter 114: [113] War's Begun
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2000 Words]

As Ace's execution approached, a strange calm descended upon all the islands of the world, as if the world had suddenly become deserted.

Everyone, whether pirate or civilian, halted their daily activities, their attention solely fixed on the Den Den Mushi broadcasting the scene from Marineford.

Children sang songs about the terror and evil of Whitebeard, while some civilians doubted his resolve to save Ace, especially upon seeing the formidable marine formation.

In the Sabaody Archipelago, giant display screens hung around the island, attracting a diverse crowd. Journalists and civilians watched intently, while some infamous pirates observed from a distance, their expressions varied.

Among these pirates were members of the Worst Generation, who scrutinized the naval forces with serious expressions, particularly after seeing the gathering of the Warlords.

"So this is the Black Death Ren..." Trafalgar D. Water Law muttered, watching the giant screen from afar with his sword resting on his shoulder, a look of anticipation in his eyes.

"Captain, do you think Whitebeard will appear?" one of his subordinates asked.

"I think he will. Let's just keep watching," Law replied.

"Yes, Captain!"

Around the world, many shared the same question: Would the aging and ill Whitebeard dare to appear at Marine Headquarters?

In Totto Land.

"Mama Mama~! I hope you won't disappoint me, Whitebeard. Show up and die there because your era has ended! Mama Mama~"

Big Mom laughed wickedly as she watched the screen showing Marineford.

Big Mom turned to her daughter Smoothie and asked, "Is this the new Warlord? What do you think of him, Smoothie?"

Smoothie, a tall woman with dark skin, wavy white hair, and blue eyes, wore a pink striped dress and a large yellow scarf. Her expression remained unchanged as she answered, "He looks decent. We will see his strength soon."

Big Mom nodded reluctantly. "You're right. I don't think someone who defeated the second division commander in that old fool Newgate's 'family' will be weak, but let's watch his performance in this war. If he's good, I'll invite him to the tea party and offer him your hand in marriage. Mama Mama~"

The thought of gaining such a strong ally lifted Big Mom's spirits.

Back at Marineford.

A Marine soldier hurried to Fleet Admiral Sengoku.

"Sir! We've lost contact with the ships sent to deal with Whitebeard's forces!"

"What?!" Sengoku shouted, then frowned as he looked out over the vast blue sea. "Bring the prisoner!"

The soldier quickly saluted and rushed to carry out the order.

Moments later, Ace appeared, shackled and one-armed.

All eyes on the Den Den Mushi live broadcast watched as Ace was led to the execution platform where Sengoku stood.

"This is Fire Fist Ace!" a journalist in the Sabaody Archipelago exclaimed, raising his camera.

"His hand was cut off by the Black Death Ren, standing among the Warlords!"

"Look, they've seen each other!"

On the display screen, Ren turned to look at Ace. Their gazes met for a few seconds before Ace lowered his head, his expression gloomy and unwilling.

'Oyaji... please don't come...' Ace prayed silently, not wanting his father and the crew to be doomed.

"Give me the loudspeaker," Sengoku commanded, taking it from a subordinate.

Garp's frown deepened. He knew a secret was about to be revealed.

Sengoku had informed him of this public announcement, and all Garp could do was nod reluctantly, feeling helpless.

Sengoku stepped forward, facing Ace on the execution platform. Ordering the executioners to step back, he began to speak.

"Before the execution begins, I have something important to say..."

Sengoku's voice echoed, ensuring the 100,000 people below could hear him clearly. The Warlords and the three Admirals also paid close attention, as did everyone watching the

screens.

Sengoku looked at Ace and shouted, "Tell me, who is your father?"

"My father is Whitebeard!" Ace replied firmly, his voice unwavering despite his current weakened state.

Sengoku shook his head and continued, "The Marines searched a certain island in the East Blue, looking for a specific child that was rumored to have been born there..." Ace's eyes widened, his expression turning grim.

Sengoku went on, "But we couldn't find that newborn. We searched all the newborns but to no avail, all because of a woman named Portgas D. Rouge! Ace's biological mother."

People around the world widened their eyes in shock. This was information that had never been revealed before. Journalists everywhere started taking notes frantically.

"Big news! A major scoop!"

These words echoed throughout the Sabaody Archipelago, but to everyone's surprise, Sengoku continued speaking.

"She carried Ace in her womb for 20 months, far longer than a normal pregnancy. After Ace was born, Rouge died... This happened exactly a year and three months after the death of Ace's real father!"

Sengoku took a deep breath and announced, "We discovered that Ace's real father is the Pirate King, Gol D. Roger!"

"!!!"

In Marineford, everyone except those already in the know was stunned. Did the Pirate King have a surviving descendant? And was that descendant the soon-to-be-executed Fire Fist

Ace?

Civilians and pirates watching the broadcast were immediately struck with both shock and excitement.

"So, that scourge left a child behind? Kill him, Marines!" some civilians shouted eagerly. They had suffered because of the Great Pirate Era that Roger started, making him the most hated figure in their lives.

Roger's former enemies laughed at his son's predicament, eager to see the Pirate King's reaction if he knew his son's death was imminent.

In Totto Land.

"Mama Mama~! So that child is Roger's brat!" Big Mom laughed gleefully. Roger had surpassed her to become the Pirate King, making her hate him naturally, not to mention they

had been enemies for decades.

She was quickly drawn back to Sengoku's voice coming from the screen.

"So, brave soldiers, this execution is not just a regular event. This is about ending the bloodline of the Pirate King who brought you and your families so much suffering. Fight with

all your might!"

Sengoku roared, hoping his words would ignite their fighting spirit.

"Yes!!"

"Death to Roger's child!"

"Slaughter the pirates!"

The roar of 100,000 voices shook Marineford. Doflamingo laughed upon seeing this and said, "Fufufufu- So he's the Pirate King's son... This war will be interesting."

Mihawk: "..."

Kuma: "..."

"Hmph, annoying men," Hancock said disdainfully, seeming indifferent to Ace's identity.

Ren had emerged from his "Cold-Hearted" state, having begun to acclimate to Hancock's allure, but he maintained a calm expression like the other Warlords.

Meanwhile, Sengoku continued, pointing out that two years ago, Ace went to sea as the captain of the Spade Pirates. His power and rapid rise made the World Government realize that Roger's lineage hadn't ended. However, Whitebeard noticed this too and recruited Ace before the government could reach him.

Reaching this point, Sengoku said in a grave voice, "Whitebeard did this to make him the next

Pirate King!"

"You're talking nonsense! Oyaji will be the Pirate King!" Ace shouted, waving his remaining

arm furiously at Sengoku.

Sengoku didn't even look at him and continued, "If we let Roger's lineage roam free, he will end up surpassing all pirates just like his father, becoming the next Pirate King! Therefore, this execution is crucial for us and the world! Even if it means a full-scale war with

Whitebeard's pirates!"

*Roar!!*

"Death to Roger's lineage!"

"Bring it on, pirates!" Sengoku's words acted like a magic tonic, causing 100,000 Marine soldiers to roar. Koby and Helmeppo, standing among the Marines, looked around nervously.

"Sir! Pirate ships have been spotted!"

An officer hurried to Sengoku with a serious expression.

"Let me see." Sengoku took the binoculars, and after several seconds, said, "Whitebeard is

not with them... These are the allied pirate ships of the Whitebeard Pirates!"

After confirming this, Sengoku grabbed the loudspeaker and commanded, "Prepare for

battle!"

Immediately after his words, several shadows began to emerge from underwater. Within moments, numerous ships surfaced from beneath the water!

Among these ships was one known throughout the world: Moby Dick, the main ship of the

Whitebeard Pirates!

Everyone watching the live broadcast turned their eyes to Whitebeard, their expressions

ranging from awe to schadenfreude. It was their greatest dream to see him fall here.

"He's arrived!" Sengoku shouted, ordering the troops not to fire yet.

On the Moby Dick, Whitebeard sat on his chair, then lifted his head to look at Ace, who stared

back in shock. "Oyaji..! Why did you come..!" Ace shouted, but he was met with only Whitebeard's gentle

expression as he spoke in a deep voice, "Wait for me, Ace. I will save you and avenge you. Tell me, Sengoku, you haven't hurt my son, have you?" Whitebeard slowly stood up from his chair and walked to the front of the ship. His voice was clearly audible to everyone in Marineford and those watching the live broadcast. 'That traitor didn't come...' Whitebeard thought silently as he scanned the Warlords' line and didn't find Teach. He naturally knew the news that Teach had become a Warlord.

But then his gaze fell on Ren, and he scowled. Remembering Ace's severed hand, he couldn't help but feel a surge of anger. However, he calmed himself and said, "Come on, my sons, fight

with all your might!"

"Yes!!"

Shouted the 14 commanders of Whitebeard's crew who were present on the three ships surrounding the Moby Dick. In the next moment, they saw Whitebeard raise both his hands.

*Crack~!*

"Not good!"

Sengoku shouted as he saw Whitebeard's sudden movement. It seemed as if the air itself had shattered. Moments later, a massive tsunami appeared, aiming to flood Marineford!

"I-It's a tsunami!!"

"What power!"

"Can we even stop this?!"

Some Marines in Marineford were terrified, not to mention the civilians watching this scene

from the safety of their homes. It was shocking and confirmed Whitebeard's renowned

strength. "The previous earthquake caused this massive tsunami you see before you, and it's all because of one man: Whitebeard! Military power alone won't be enough to stop him. He possesses the power to destroy the world, thanks to his Devil Fruit!"

Sengoku shouted, then cast a glance at Aokiji, who quickly vanished from his seat.

"Ice Age."

Ice spread with his cold voice throughout Marineford. Within moments, the massive tsunami

was frozen in the air, becoming a giant block of ice!

"Amazing! This is the power of the Admirals!"

"The three Admirals are on our side, so why fear the pirates?!"

'Wow...'

Sighs of admiration, excitement, and praise came from the Marines' mouths. People around

the world watched the live broadcast in shock, most of them never having seen such

supernatural powers in their lives.

Are they still considered human?!

Can humans even do this?!

Back in Marineford, Aokiji attacked Whitebeard after freezing the tsunami, but he was

interrupted by a quake punch from Whitebeard.

Aokiji reformed himself after turning into an ice block, looking at the water beneath him.

"Arara~ This is a bit troublesome."

He placed his hand horizontally in the water and then:

"Ice Age."

The vast bay began to freeze rapidly, visible to the naked eye, causing Whitebeard's and his

allies' ships to become immobilized.

Seeing this, Sengoku's eyes brightened, and he ordered, "Fire!!"

*Bang!!*

*Boom!!*

*Bang!!*

*Boom!!*

The sound of cannons was deafening. Dozens of modern cannons started firing madly at every

pirate ship in sight. Smoke rose, and fires broke out on one unlucky ship, while some pirates were blown to pieces.

The survivors glared fiercely at the distant Marines. Soon, the first pirate stepped onto the ice,

followed by dozens, then hundreds of pirates.

"Attack!!" the fierce pirates from the New World roared, causing some Marines from the Four Seas to feel dizzy. Their hands shook with fear, but the Headquarters Marines, accustomed to battling New World pirates, stood firm, holding their weapons coldly and advancing fiercely

toward the pirates.

"The war has begun!!" Sengoku shouted, and at the same time, the two armies clashed. The battle that would determine the future of this sea, no, this world, had begun!
