Chapter 115: [114] A Chaotic Battlefield
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2200 Words]

*Boom!!*

*Bang!!*

*Explosion!!*

The battle erupted with a storm of attacks. Marineford's artillery relentlessly bombarded Whitebeard's allied ships, while the pirates retaliated with a barrage of cannon and bazooka fire. In mere seconds, the battlefield descended into unparalleled chaos.

"Die, you bastard!" A random pirate grinned wickedly as he decapitated a marine soldier, but his joy was short-lived as another marine blew his head off.

*Swish!* Another pirate appeared and killed the marine who had killed his comrade. This chain of killings continued across the brutal battlefield.

"It's my turn to make a move," The Vice Admiral Momonga Said with a cold expression, ready to slaughter as many pirates as possible.

He stepped forward and vanished using <Shave>, cutting down any pirate in his path like a machine.

"Hmph!" Suddenly, Momonga heard a cold snort and looked at the newcomer.

It was a tall man, around four meters, with messy black hair and a scarred face.

"The Devil's Scythe, leader of the Harvest Pirates. So, you chose to side with Whitebeard? Don't think you'll leave here alive!" Momonga recognized the pirate as one of Whitebeard's allies.

"Sihahaha! Enough talking, let our fists talk!" The Devil's Scythe laughed wickedly and advanced towards Momonga.

*Screech!!*

In the next second, the Devil's Scythe's hand transformed into a sharp scythe and clashed with Momonga's sword.

With the sound of screeching metal, the golden scythe was pushed back a few inches but retained its wicked smile.

Ren noticed this battle thanks to his observation Haki and decided to take action. He had spotted many Devil Fruit users just minutes into the war, and his greed grew; he wanted to kill them personally to claim their Devil Fruits.

Still aligned with the Warlords, and seeing no one else had joined the battle, he decided to be the first.

*Whoosh!*

With the sound of rustling wind, he disappeared using <Shave> and swiftly moved to where the Devil's Scythe was fighting Vice Admiral Momonga.

This move caught the attention of several people, including Mihawk and Doflamingo, as well as Sengoku, who observed everything using his observation Haki.

Whitebeard, standing on his ship's deck, frowned when he saw Ren taking action.

"Should I go deal with him, Pops?" Marco saw Whitebeard's expression and naturally knew what he wanted, so he suggested taking the initiative.

Whitebeard shook his head, rejecting his son's proposal, and quickly refocused on the battlefield while also keeping an eye on Ren's movements.

At the same time, the Devil's Scythe felt a bad feeling in his heart, an instinct telling him to flee. But he was stuck in a fight against Momonga and needed full concentration, or he would be defeated.

'Death Blade!!'

Ren suddenly appeared before the two fighters and internally called out the name of his skill. He had already drawn his sword and infused it with armament Haki.

*Swish!!*

A medium-sized blade slashed towards the Devil's Scythe, who widened his eyes and tried to retreat. Momonga looked at Ren in shock; he was within the attack range too!

Without hesitation, Momonga used <Shave> again and retreated several meters, not caring about the Devil's Scythe's horrified expression.

All this happened in just a few seconds. The Devil's Scythe found himself in a life-or-death crisis. A fierce expression appeared on his face as he turned his other hand into another scythe, then created two sharp air blades and slashed towards Ren's Death Blade, formed from the power of <Versa> and Ren's armament Haki.

"Arrgh!! Cut for me! Cut! Cut! Cut!" The Devil's Scythe screamed madly, slashing with all his might with the air blades towards Ren's attack.

*Swish!*

*Splash!*

*Flow!*

With a loud collision sound, Ren's attack struck the Devil's Scythe's body, slicing it in half. His terrified, unwilling, and hate-filled gaze remained fixed on his killer.

Those watching Ren's movements couldn't help but adopt serious expressions; the power of that blade was overwhelming!

The Devil's Scythe's strength was not to be underestimated. He could fight a Vice Admiral and might even win against an medium-level one. His bounty had reached 450 million berries.

'Miss System, store his Devil Fruit,' Ren commanded internally. He no longer cared about the dead body and, after ensuring the Devil Fruit was stored, used <Shave> again and disappeared, ignoring the terrified expressions of the pirates and marines.

Momonga looked at the Devil's Scythe's body, then at the spot where Ren had disappeared, and gritted his teeth. He didn't like this arrogant pirate's behavior!

As a follower of Akainu's path, Momonga believes that all pirates should die, even the Warlords are just pirates with the support of the World Government.

With an angry sigh, he drew his sword again and randomly cut down an unfortunate pirate.

Ren did not return to where the other Warlords were, but instead, wandered through the

battlefield, attacking anyone who crossed his path.

"Bastard!" a pirate roared fiercely and fired a cannonball at Ren.

*Whoosh!*

Ren slashed the cannonball with his sword, splitting it in two and causing it to explode away from him. He looked at the terrified pirate in front of him and smiled mockingly.

He was about to cut the pirate, but the latter fell on his own.

"Stupid fool-" a soft voice came from behind the pirate, and Mikita appeared, having used <Finger Gun> to kill the pirate. She greeted Ren.

"Hello, Captain~ This battlefield is lively, isn't it?" She walked towards him, held his hand with hers, and smiled brightly as if she didn't care about the fierce war around her.

Chapter 116: [115] Nami Vs Jozu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2000 Words]

In Marineford, everyone who was focused on Nami and Jozu was stunned.

They saw Nami draw her sword and transform into a bolt of lightning, which reached Jozu in the blink of an eye.

Many knowledgeable individuals, such as Sengoku, Garp, and Whitebeard, recognized the source of Nami's lightning power.

It was undoubtedly the Devil Fruit that had been missing for 200 years!

Two centuries ago, there was a Marine admiral named Onas, who was incredibly famous in his era for using the power of Goro Goro no Mi to subdue many powerful pirates. The sea in his time knew a kind of peace, and pirates called him the "Thunder Demon," a testament to their fear of him.

After Onas's death, this Devil Fruit disappeared, and despite the World Government's extensive search for many years, it could not be found, leading them to give up.

And now, this Devil Fruit had reappeared, and worse, its user was a pirate!

Sengoku's face turned extremely grim. His worst fears, and those of the elders of the World Government, had come true-this fruit had fallen into the wrong hands!

"The power of the Goro Goro no Mi should not be in the hands of pirates!" Sengoku gritted his teeth in anger and spoke in a low voice.

"Fufufufu... I didn't expect that girl to possess such amazing power. I wonder where she found that fruit?" Doflamingo felt a bit anxious. He had openly shown hostility towards Ren, so naturally, he wasn't pleased to see the Sinful Pirates grow stronger.

As the Underworld's Joker, he knew some secret information about this Devil Fruit and was well aware of its tremendous destructive power.

"So she's a swordsman who uses a Devil Fruit?" Mihawk, who had been silent, thought aloud as he focused on the battle.

Back to Nami, who had reached Jozu, she swiftly slashed her sword towards him.

Jozu was shocked by the sudden speed of Nami but managed to dodge the sword thanks to his Observation Haki and retaliated with a powerful punch, his hand turning to diamond and augmented with Armament Haki.

Even though his Devil Fruit's primary advantage was defense, he knew how to use it best. After years of combat experience, he had found a balance between his Devil Fruit powers and his immense physical strength.

But Nami's Observation Haki had been prepared long ago. She stepped back, dodging the punch that held tremendous power.

*Boom!*

Jozu's hand struck the frozen ground, digging deep into the solid ice, showcasing the punch's force.

Nami didn't give Jozu time to free his hand and sent a massive air blade slashing towards him. *Swish!*

Jozu narrowed his eyes and chose to take the blade head-on, covering his body in diamond and receiving the hit.

*Screech~!*

*Scratch~!*

With a grating noise, the air blade dissipated, revealing Jozu's unscathed body gleaming under a diamond coating.

"You're strong... but you're still far from defeating me!" Jozu shouted proudly.

Nami didn't respond, but her gaze held hidden contempt. She chose not to engage in conversation and resumed her attack.

This time, the weather played in her favor. The sky over the battlefield of Nami and Jozu began to fill with clouds, signaling the onset of a storm.

"Divine Retribution!" Nami shouted sharply, and simultaneously, a thunderbolt struck Marineford, specifically targeting the spot where Jozu stood.

All this happened within seconds. Jozu expected a close-range battle, not a long-range attack, and didn't foresee Nami's next move. His Observation Haki urged him to escape, but The gritted his teeth and faced the thunderbolt head-on.

In Sabaody Archipelago, people were astonished by Nami's display of power.

*Gulp*

He swallowed the remaining liquor in the jar, wiped his mouth, and said, "Is this the end, Whitebeard?"

The chaotic battlefield was drenched in blood seeping into the ground. The land of Marineford had turned into a realm of death and hell. Amid the screams of pirates and marines, madness brewed, and the killing intent rose with every life taken.

She vanished from her spot with incredible speed, faster than the lightning dragon, and reappeared behind the terrified Jozu.

'My Observation Haki couldn't detect her!!' Jozu thought in panic as he tried to jump into the air to escape. But at that moment, Nami tightened her grip on her sword, coating it in the distinctive black of Armament Haki. At the same time, the lightning dragon reached Jozu.

He was completely cornered!

Jozu roared in frustration and used his Devil Fruit power to its fullest extent, encasing himself

entirely in diamond.

"Let's see if your diamond can stop me."

As soon as she finished speaking, a massive explosion erupted, and those watching the battle saw figures emerge from the dissipating fog.

The entire world witnessed this outcome, and from now on, Nami's name would rise to

become one of the legends of the Sin Pirates!

Chapter 117: [116]The Clash of Wills: Fate at Marineford
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2200 words]

The giant monster, Oars jr, moved forward without stopping, trampling every Marine soldier that got in his way with his massive body.

Oars was not particularly strong; he lacked any effective skills and did not know how to use Haki. All he relied on was his enormous physique, which he used to approach Ace, who was on the execution platform.

To Oars' dismay, his massive body posed a problem only for cannon fodder. As long as the person was a little strong, they could take him down.

"Open fire on him! Don't let him get any closer to the platform!" Sengoku shouted to the nearby Marines, who quickly relayed his orders. In the next moment, Oars was hit by dozens of cannon shots.

*Boom!*

*Bang!*

*Boom!*

The terrifying sound of cannon fire did not stop Oars' advance. Even Ren, at that moment, was slightly impressed by the giant's determination and will.

But their positions did not change; they were still enemies.

'Some things never change, no matter what happens... Oars is still colliding with the Warlords...'

Ren, who had returned to his place beside the other Warlords, was muttering to himself as he watched Oars' giant legs nearly trample a line of Warlords, recalling Oars' unfortunate fate in the original work after this scene.

His weapon, "Versa," transformed into the shape of a sword and then disappeared from his location at an incredibly fast speed. When it reappeared, it was already above Oars' massive head.

"!!!"

Hancock, who had not made any movement since the beginning of the war, was planning to attack the giant who was about to step on her, but her beautiful eyes widened when she saw the black death, Ren, vanish from his spot and reappear above the giant.

Even she, known as the Pirate Empress, was surprised by the level of speed that was unlikely to be matched.

The Shave technique at the intermediate level that Ren mastered was no joke.

You must understand that the Six Powers of the Marines do not have levels as classified by the system. There is one level that the inhabitants of this world can practice.

Yes, the Shave used by an admiral is not the same as the Shave used by a vice admiral, but this difference mainly stems from their proficiency in the skill, along with physical strength and several other factors. However, essentially, the skill remains fixed at one level and does not change.

But in Ren's case, it was different because the system enhanced this skill and achieved a "breakthrough" in it. Thus, the Six Powers that Ren acquired from the system differ from the ordinary Six Powers invented by the World Government.

Returning to the events of the war, as soon as Ren appeared above Oars' head, he sheathed his sword, and coinciding with the sound of the sword being sheathed, a scene occurred that widened the eyes of the Whitebeard Pirates and their allies in shock.

No, it wasn't just them who were shocked, but everyone watching the war, whether on the battlefield or through the giant screens.

"Ugh!!"

With a sorrowful scream filled with pain and resentment, Oars' giant body split vertically in half.

His massive, split body fell to the hard ground of Marineford without the slightest trace of life.

Blood began to seep from his divided body, and soon the ground was covered with blood and entrails, while the dense metallic smell of blood spread throughout the area.

All this happened in mere moments, so fast that everyone did not know how to react, especially on the pirate side. Some were shocked, while others lost their morale, but the main members of Whitebeard's crew were sadder than anyone else.

"Oars!!" One of the Whitebeard Pirates' commanders shouted sorrowfully.

"Damn that bastard! I'll burn him!" Marco yelled through clenched teeth, glaring at Ren with anger and hatred.

In stark contrast, the Marine side was filled with morale and a thirst for victory. The sounds of battle and gunfire returned swiftly after this brief pause, and it was clear that the pirates' morale had been affected by the brutal death of Oars.

Another thing happened that drew everyone's attention again; one of the giant vice admirals tried to attack Whitebeard, who was still saddened by the death of a good son like Oars, and the vice admiral's skull was smashed, killing him instantly.

The death of the vice admiral restored the pirates' morale back to what it was or at least helped them recover from what had just happened with Oars, which only intensified the battlefield.

In Alabasta, specifically within the royal palace, the crew of the Sin Pirates was intently watching the broadcast showcasing the chaos unfolding at Marineford, their expressions varying in response to the mayhem.

"Fufufu- Captain Ren is as ruthless as ever toward his enemies." Nojiko's gaze was filled with warmth as she observed Ren standing atop the dismembered body of Oars Jr.

"Strong..." Kalifa inhaled a cold breath, realizing she hadn't noticed when Ren drew his sword and struck down the giant Oars, a testament to his incredible speed.

To defeat such a giant, one would typically need to collaborate with Rob Lucci to land a decisive blow. It wouldn't be her who would deliver the fatal strike, but rather Rob Lucci, utilizing the secret form of the Six Powers.

Yet, Ren, who had temporarily become part of her crew to spy on him, had killed Oars with minimal effort.

Califa began to open her eyes to a new realm of power-a realm that could challenge her convictions and alter her destiny.

...

Meanwhile, at Marineford.

Amidst the chaos and destruction surrounding the Summit War, the sounds of battle echoed through the air. Between the cries of fighters and the clashing of swords, another sound resonated clearly—the flapping of fiery wings. Those were the wings of Marco the Phoenix, descending toward the ground like a shooting star.

Ren stood tall over Oars Jr.'s lifeless body, staring into the sky with a cold smile. He felt the warmth of blue flames approaching but showed no sign of hesitation. "So, you've decided to be my next opponent?" Ren said calmly as Marco landed gracefully before him. "You killed Oars... now, you will pay the price!" Marco declared, his suppressed anger palpable, his eyes glowing with fierce blue flames.

Ren slowly raised his sword, enveloped in Haki, and added with a challenging tone, "Make me

do it."

With those simple words from both sides, a battle of extraordinary magnitude began, capturing everyone's attention.

Marco wasted no time. In an instant, he transformed into his Phoenix form, his body igniting with blue flames that surrounded him like an indestructible armor. He charged toward Ren at an incredible speed, using his wings to propel himself faster. Ren dodged the initial attack but felt the heat of the flames grazing his skin.

'What a terrifying fire ability!' he thought to himself and countered with an attack, utilizing Observation Haki to read Marco's movements and targeting his weaknesses. With each strike from Ren, his Haki-coated sword sliced through the air, yet Marco continually regenerated. With every blow Marco took, his body renewed itself with the immortal flames of the Phoenix. Even Ren's Armament Haki struggled to penetrate the remarkable healing abilities of the

Phoenix.

"Tsk..." Ren felt frustration welling up as he watched his opponent recover so quickly, as if all his efforts were futile. But he was not the type to give up easily.

"Your abilities are extremely annoying... as expected from the second-in-command of the Whitebeard Pirates!" Ren said, taking a small step back to catch his breath and reorganize his

strategy.

Marco, soaring through the sky again, replied coldly, "Did you really think that by hiding behind the Marines and the World Government, you could avoid Pops' wrath? Listen, you

bastard, we will free Ace!"

Hearing Marco's mocking voice filled with anger, Ren chose to remain silent.

"You talk too much." A cold smile appeared on Ren's face as he addressed Marco, causing the

latter to feel an unsettling premonition in his heart.

In the next moment, a dark aura erupted from Ren's body, spreading across the battlefield.

*Zzzzzz!!*

All fighting ceased as warriors from both sides felt an unknown pressure enveloping them,

with some weaker individuals beginning to collapse, losing consciousness.

"This..."

"The Conqueror's Haki!"

"It's the Black Death! He possesses the Conqueror's Haki!"

Some knowledgeable men from both sides recognized the source of this pressure, and soon

Users of Conqueror's Haki were rare.

"Fufu... So you also possess this will... the will to become a king, but it's still weak and

unrefined." Doflamingo smirked slyly, unfazed by Ren's aura. For someone like him, who awakened the Conqueror's Haki at the age of seven, this was hardly surprising.

Hancock, still standing motionless in her place, showed no signs of shock. After all, she possessed this ability as well, and hers was stronger and more refined than Ren's,

maintaining her sense of superiority.

Ren's Conqueror's Haki was still at the basic level; he had gained this ability only a few weeks

ago. Despite training to control it, he found it challenging, and it continued to affect his

Marine allies.

Not that he cared about that last point, but the Fleet Admiral certainly did.

"Black Death, did you do this on purpose?!" Sengoku shouted, his expression filled with

there was no joy in his laughter as there usually was. This was understandable given the calamity facing his crew, but Whitebeard was not afraid.

He looked at Marco, preparing to order him to retreat. The priority for the Whitebeard Pirates

was to rescue Ace, not to seek revenge on Ren. It was better not to waste their energy on a

futile battle.

But at that moment, he heard Ren's mocking voice: "Fleet Admiral Sengoku, look at the number of unconscious pirates and compare them to the number of Marines in the same state. I'm fulfilling my part in the agreement of the Warlords to the fullest, assisting the Marines in

this war, so you shouldn't doubt me. Furthermore, despite your rank as Fleet Admiral, you have no authority over me, so don't tell me what I should do!"

Ren's tone grew increasingly aggressive and fierce as he spoke. Sengoku had attempted to assert his authority to the world and use Ren as a scapegoat for that purpose. Given that this war was being broadcast live to the entire world, Sengoku intended to publicly reprimand Ren to demonstrate that no matter how powerful the pirates were, he still held authority over

them.

Sengoku truly lived up to his title as the "Wise General"!

However, Sengoku did not expect such a hostile response from Ren. For a moment, even he was taken aback, his expression freezing as he contemplated an appropriate reply.

No matter how hard he tried, he could find no flaws in Ren's words! Everything Ren said was true. He looked at the thousands of unconscious pirates; under normal circumstances, he would have rejoiced at this, but now he couldn't, for it was merely evidence supporting Ren's

claims.

As Ren stood firm, exchanging glances with Sengoku and the Marines, a powerful voice resonated across the battlefield, capturing everyone's attention.

"We're here to save Ace, not to waste time with this brat, Marco!" These words came from

Whitebeard, delivered in a deep, calm voice, yet filled with an aura of authority that silenced everyone.

"Marco, retreat!" Whitebeard commanded firmly without glancing at his first mate. Despite the anger blazing in Marco's eyes, he had no choice. Their primary goal was to rescue Ace, not

to avenge Oars. Reluctantly, Marco stepped back from confronting Ren, retreating lightly to his father's side.

Ren, sensing the shift in the atmosphere around him, smiled coldly as he regarded Whitebeard with caution, thinking to himself, 'Smart move... but let's see how long you can

endure.'

At that moment, despite the losses, the roar of battle rose once more. The cries of pirates surged toward the execution platform with renewed vigor, gathering all their strength to rescue Ace. The Marines, for their part, braced themselves to repel the pirates' fierce assaults. Explosions echoed in the distance, and the ground trembled beneath everyone's feet.

Yet, Ren showed no signs of concern. With a calm gaze, he observed the battle unfold from his position as if it did not affect him. This was merely the beginning... the real war had yet to

reach its climax.

I'am back.

Chapter 118: [117]Mikita's Showcase
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2000 Words]

The battlefield at Marineford can only be described in one word: Hell.

Yes, it resembled a living nightmare. Unlike the portrayal on TV, witnessing the grim reality of war firsthand was an entirely different experience. The cries of rage, hatred, and pain echoed continuously. Every minute, dozens of people from both sides fell, their blood and entrails saturating the hard ground, accompanied by severed limbs and lifeless bodies.

"Kill those damn pirates!"

"Annihilate the Marines!"

"Retrieve Ace! Cut off the heads of the dogs of the Celestial Dragons!"

Both sides hurled harsh insults while killing, tearing, and shooting at one another. Amidst this chaos, Nami surveyed the madness around her, her heart heavy with tension.

Just a few months ago, she had been an "ordinary" girl... well, not entirely ordinary, but still unaccustomed to the brutality of battle.

Yet she didn't hesitate to strike down anyone who attacked her. Her sword was stained with the blood of her victims, and it wouldn't dry anytime soon.

Swish!

"Ah!"

"My hand!"

"Curse you, woman!"

Nami sliced through a group of pirates attempting to assault her, hearing their curses before they succumbed.

"I could get used to this..." With each strike, the tension and hesitation in her heart faded. This was a battlefield, and hesitation only led to death.

She refused to disappoint her beloved Ren and would not let her former naivety cost her the happiness she had fought hard to achieve. Not after reuniting with the most important woman in her life; she had a family waiting for her and vowed not to fall in this battle.

Suddenly, as Nami was lost in thought, her Observation Haki warned her of imminent danger approaching.

She utilized Shave to dodge the attack aimed at her.

The attack was... rose petals!

It might seem absurd, but her Observation Haki had warned her about these rose petals! Nami sensed that if she were to take this blow, it would severely injure her.

The petals that missed their target struck a hapless Marine soldier, inflicting numerous wounds and claiming his life within seconds.

"So you can evade my 'Rose Rondo'... as expected from the little devil who defeated Brother Jozu." A deep voice emerged from a group of frozen Marines. In the next moment, several Marines fell, deep wounds evident on their bodies, quickly closing their eyes forever.

A tall man appeared elegantly, facing Nami, who quickly recognized him.

"The Fifth Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, Vista." Nami said, her voice revealing no fear, only pure killing intent.

Vista was a muscular man with a broad chest and dark skin. He sported a curly black mustache, a dark blue hat, and an earring in each ear. He wore a partially open dark blue shirt with a diamond pattern near the collar, revealing some of his hairy chest, along with a cape draped over his shirt and white gloves. He also wore light blue pants with a belt holding two sheaths for his swords, one on each side. He wielded a sword in each hand.

The intent to battle radiated from him as he cautiously regarded Nami.

"Who do you call a little devil?! You bastard!" Nami suddenly erupted in anger at Vista's words. She gripped her cursed sword tightly, coating it with Armament Haki, then slashed her sharp blade toward him.

Swish!!

Another bloody battle commenced.

...

Marineford Square was engulfed in chaos and destruction. Forces from both sides were falling, and flames rose into the sky. Amid this hell, Mikita moved gracefully among the rubble and bodies, keeping her focus on her target: Curiel, the Tenth Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, who relentlessly fired his massive shells, shattering Marine ranks. Previously, Mikita had been a professional killer, which allowed her to secure a solid position in the Baroque Works organization. Even though she had long abandoned that identity, her instincts as a killer remained active and had grown stronger.

Thus, Curiel, the Tenth Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates, was her target. He had caused significant problems for the Marines due to his extensive firing range, capable of annihilating large numbers in no time.

Curiel was a tall man with a muscular build, wearing ear coverings resembling headphones. Mikita didn't know if they were merely decorative or if these headphones helped him block out the noise of his explosives. He wore purple glasses and had a Whitebeard Jolly Roger tattoo on his chin.

He was wearing a large green sleeveless jacket over a purple shirt, along with camouflaged pants dotted with green and brown. A green hat with black stripes adorned his head. Two bazookas were strapped to his back, and he held two pistols in his hands, now using them to blow the Marines into pieces.

"Stay clean and quick, Mikita~," she whispered to herself, glancing briefly at Ren. When she saw him watching her with interest, her heart leaped with joy, but she tried to focus on the enemy in front of her.

Mikita paused for a moment, contemplating how to approach an opponent armed with two guns and skilled in their use. However, she was confident because she possessed a unique weapon: the Kiro Kiro no Mi Devil Fruit, which allowed her to change her weight at will.

"I don't see any signs of Haki on his weapons..." she murmured as she lightly descended to the ground. In an instant, she reduced her weight to one kilogram, enabling her to move with astonishing speed through the pirates and Marines, avoiding detection by her prey.

*Boom!*

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

Curiel was unaware of Mikita's presence; he was preoccupied with firing at groups of Marines. His massive projectiles exploded, shattering both the ground and the bodies of the Marines, while Mikita approached stealthily like an almost invisible bird. She planned to get close and launch a surprise attack. But suddenly, Curiel felt something behind him and turned around

quickly.

"Huh? Who's there?!" he shouted, firing a shell directly at Mikita's location.

"Observation Haki, huh..." Despite her surprise, Mikita was quick enough to dodge the

explosion, using her light weight to leap into the air like a feather. When she was directly

above Curiel, she made her decisive move.

"Have you ever had 10,000 kilograms of weight on you?!" Mikita shouted with a crazy grin on her face, suddenly increasing her weight to 10,000 kilograms.

"What?!" Curiel exclaimed in confusion as his Observation Haki warned him of the impending danger.

In that critical moment, Haki manifested in his hands, crossing them to form an X shape. His level of Haki didn't allow him to protect every part of his body, so he had no other option. All of this happened in just a few seconds; Mikita fell from the sky like a massive meteor, her body entirely covered in Armament Haki, directing her heavy attack toward Curiel. She struck the ground with tremendous force, causing a massive explosion of debris around them. Curiel felt immense pressure on his body as he struggled to push Mikita away, but the crushing

weight exceeded his strength.

"What is this madness?!" he yelled as he strained against the pressure.

Mikita, bearing down on him with her massive weight, seized the moment and suddenly reduced her weight back to one kilogram, allowing her to move unexpectedly fast. She jumped away from Curiel before he could react, rolling lightly across the ground.

"Ugh!! My hands!!" Curiel's desperate scream caught the attention of Whitebeard and many others. Even Vista, who was fiercely battling Nami, couldn't help but lose focus upon hearing

Curiel's piteous cry.

Curiel's crew.

injury-" "Don't worry about me, brat. Now go kick some Marine butts!"

Shabondi screens.

for publication!

fruit.

Nami was using the Goro Goro no Mi in her fight once again!

Chapter 119: [118] Whitebeard's Anger
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2500 Words]

In Marineford.

The dust still enveloped the area where Vista was located, obscuring everyone's vision. However, the sounds alone were enough for those who witnessed the battle between Nami and Vista to understand what had transpired. The lightning struck before the sound of thunder, tearing through the sky, its reflection visible in the fighters' eyes. Vista, who did not easily accept defeat, was kneeling on one knee, clutching his cracked swords!

Blood dripped from his lips, and his eyes narrowed in pain, while Nami stood before him, her sword wrapped in blue lightning as she glared at him with a chilling look. The cruelty of the lightning witnessed by everyone on the battlefield left no room for doubt; this was not the same weak, cowardly woman from the past. Nami, wielding the power of the Goro Goro no Mi, was undergoing a terrifying transformation.

With a cold smile, she said, "I thought a swordsman of your experience could handle this... It seems I was wrong, or I overestimated your strength, as I managed to defeat you after just two months of practicing swordsmanship." It seemed she said the last part to provoke Vista. Vista was in a pitiful state at that moment. A large bloody wound stood out on his broad chest, now exposed due to the tearing of his clothes from the force of the lightning. Vista gasped for air, trying to rise and continue fighting, but his eyes widened when he heard what Nami said. "Two months?!" he shouted in disbelief. How could he believe that the woman in front of him had only started learning swordsmanship two months ago? This was a huge joke! Nami's movements suggested years of experience and training! Every movement and swing of her sword aimed for one goal: the annihilation of the enemy. Moreover, she moved in a way that made Vista question how he had never heard of such a skilled swordswoman before. How did she suddenly appear out of nowhere with all this power? She was just like her captain... the Black Death, Ren.

But now, after hearing Nami's words, Vista's previously bewildering questions had been answered.

Despite his shock, he was not in a position to dwell on such matters right now. Nami's last attack had nearly cost him his life, and he had barely escaped. He did not want to die-not now; he had a mission to rescue Ace, and he did not want to die before completing it. "Die."

Suddenly, he heard Nami's voice, sending shivers down his spine. He felt the intense aura of death approaching him. Vista raised his swords, attempting to defend himself. But he knew, just as his opponent did, that the upcoming attack would be fatal. Yet his warrior instincts could not accept defeat; he would not fall here!

The moment Nami surged toward Vista like lightning, a blue beam began to burst from her body toward the ground, targeting one of the greatest fighters of the Whitebeard Pirates!

Just at the critical moment, a massive body appeared before Nami, forcefully blocking her path and preventing her sword from decapitating Vista.

"Jozu!" Vista exclaimed with joy upon seeing the familiar massive diamond man.

"Tsk... It's you again. Do you really want to die this badly?!" Nami looked at Jozu, who had appeared before her again with frustration. She had defeated Jozu not long ago, beating him like a dog before Marco rescued him, and now he was intervening in her fight.

Her murderous intent rose like never before, and a sinister aura emerged that both Vista and Jozu could feel. Jozu swallowed hard in fear but with unwavering determination, then said to Vista, "I'll stop her here! You go help the others free Ace."

"No...! I can't leave you to die here!" Vista hesitated in refusal. Jozu was about to respond, but Nami's next move interrupted their dramatic exchange.

*Swish!!*

*Swish!!*

*Swish!!*

Three sharp blades shot out, appearing capable of cutting through anything in their path toward them. Jozu's expression tightened, and he activated his Devil Fruit ability.

In an instant, Jozu extended his arms and positioned himself in front of Vista. "Diamond Barrier!" he declared as diamond energy coalesced around him.

*Boom!!!*

Nami's lightning-charged sword blades struck the diamond barrier Jozu had created, resulting in a deafening explosion that echoed across the battlefield.

When the blades collided with the barrier, it stood firm like an indestructible mountain, but the lightning's force began to penetrate its surface.

Boom!! A thunderous roar reverberated, as if a storm had swept through the arena.

Jozu braced himself against the impact of the attack, striving to reinforce the barrier. However, the terrifying effects of the lightning on the diamond began to show. As he held his ground, he noticed Vista behind him, struggling to maintain his footing.

"Jozu!" Vista shouted. "Hang in there!"

Jozu smiled, resolute and steadfast, determined to protect Vista at all costs. Thanks to his concentration, he managed to withstand most of Nami's powerful assault, but eventually, the sharp blades pierced through the diamond barrier, striking his body and causing cracks to form in the diamond encasing him.

"Ugh... Is that all you've got? *Gasp* Hah... Hah..." Jozu gasped, his voice strained.

"Now it's my turn to counterattack!"

With those words, Jozu regained his momentum, channeling his strength as he launched a counterattack toward Nami.

"Diamond Sphere!" he shouted, transforming the diamond energy into a massive ball of solid diamond power that hurtled toward Nami like a tornado, whipping up fierce winds and cracking the ground beneath it.

Nami sensed the imminent danger emanating from the diamond sphere, charged with mysterious energy, and decided to retreat cautiously. Her observation Haki had been active since the battle began, never faltering for a moment-whether to fend off the relentless attacks or avoid ambushes. This vigilance was a lesson instilled in her by Ren.

'As expected from a seasoned pirate of the New World, he still has some tricks up his sleeve!'

she thought, watching the diamond sphere accelerate toward her.

Taking a deep breath, she exhaled a cold gust of air. In the next moment, thin threads of lightning began to coil around her body, creating a cacophony of sound.

Nami focused her observation Haki on Jozu's attack as thousands of lightning threads intertwined, forming a sphere twice the size of a watermelon!

This sphere was composed solely of raw, untamed lightning energy!

This was a move that transcended swordsmanship! For the first time since the beginning of this war, Nami sheathed her sword and relied solely on the powers granted by her Devil Fruit!

*Rumble!!*

Crack!!

*Rumble!!*

It felt as though the sky itself was reacting to the impending clash. The dark clouds above Marineford grew more menacing, and the sound of thunder struck fear into the hearts of the

weak and the cannon fodder.

"Raijin Sphere!!"

In the moment of collision, the wild lightning met the solid diamond. The two auras clashed in an explosion of light and energy. Lightning charges twisted around the diamond, attempting to pierce its shiny surface, while the diamond's energy fiercely repelled the lightning as if it were an impenetrable wall. The air around them filled with sparks and flying debris, and the explosion's noise was deafening. It felt as if the sky and earth trembled with the force of their clash, as their powers struggled in a terrifying balance between lightning

and diamond.

However, it was clear that this delicate balance was about to change! The Goro Goro no Mi, known as the most powerful Logia fruit in terms of destructive capability, was on par with Whitebeard's fruit, which is said to have the potential to destroy the world!

Of course, Nami had not yet mastered the devil fruit to that extent, but her power was not to

be underestimated!

Especially with her strong body, enhanced by systematic rewards and hard training, the force that Nami could unleash was simply monstrous!

Thus, as the clash continued, the diamond ball began to endure increasing pressure. The wild lightning charges from Nami's attack were stronger than Jozu had anticipated.

Electric sparks began to seep through the small cracks that formed in the diamond's surface, slowly expanding and destroying the diamond's defenses.

"Impossible...!" Jozu muttered, feeling the increasing pressure and anxiety filling his heart.

"Die!!"

With Nami's final shout, she unleashed all her remaining energy into the lightning sphere. The lightning charges suddenly intensified and surged forward with tremendous force, engulfing the diamond ball entirely. The diamond energy struggled to resist, but the lightning viciously pierced through it, destroying the diamond from within.

In an instant, the diamond ball exploded with a massive blast, scattering shards into the air. The wild lightning continued its path and struck Jozu directly. He wore a shocked expression on his face; this attack was his trump card. The lightning ball hit him, delivering a massive

electric shock that dropped him to his knees. The minimal power of the lightning was enough to take him down as his body screamed in pain from the impact of the charges and the fierce burns that began to appear on him!

Even his diamond body could not withstand the intense heat caused by the lightning, leading to a breakdown of his defenses.

Diamond is an extremely strong and cohesive material due to its unique crystalline structure, where carbon atoms are bonded with strong links, making it difficult to break or distort. Therefore, when diamond is exposed to lightning, it is not directly affected since diamond is considered a good electrical insulator, meaning that electric current cannot flow through it easily. This is what made it challenging for Nami to defeat Jozu!

However, no power is without its weaknesses. When diamond is subjected to extremely high temperatures, its structure can be compromised. When lightning collides with diamond, it generates immense heat. If this heat is sufficient, it can cause cracks or damage to the diamond, and this is precisely what happened to Jozu!

Simply put, diamond can resist direct electrical effects, but it is not immune to the extreme heat that can result from electrical explosions, making heat a vulnerability for diamond! "Jozu!" Vista, who had previously escaped death, cried out sorrowfully, looking helplessly at Jozu's incapacitated form, while the wound on his own chest had yet to heal and was only

worsening.

*Crackle!!*

*Crackle!!*

Nami stepped forward, her breath quickening, as the lightning surrounding her began to dissipate slowly. Victory was hers, and the sky above still rumbled as if celebrating her power.

"Pathetic weaklings!" she said coldly, unsheathing her bloodthirsty sword once more,

appearing ready to end the lives of her opponents.

"Captain Jozu! Captain Vista!"

"They will die!" "Did she defeat both of them despite their combined efforts?!"

"Save the commanders from this woman!"

The shouts of Whitebeard's pirates drew the attention of everyone on the battlefield. Even Whitebeard, who was engaged with Akiji and Kizaru, paused and looked at the scene as Nami advanced to kill Jozu and Vista, his eyes wide and filled with fury.

"Do not dare do this, you wretched girl!"

*Roar!!*

Whitebeard's roar silenced the battlefield for several seconds, and then in an instant, he

swung his naginata towards Nami and shouted, "Marco! Bring Jozu and Vista here, now!" Marco understood what his father wanted, transforming into a flaming bird and flying swiftly toward his brothers. At the same time, a white energy appeared at the head of Whitebeard's

naginata!

It was the power of the his devil Fruit!

All this occurred within a matter of seconds. When Ren saw that Whitebeard intended to attack Nami, he shouted angrily: "Mikita! Stop Marco!"

Mikita appeared in the sky, a serious expression on her face. "Understood!" she exclaimed, and then she quickly launched herself towards Marco, who was racing toward Nami. This did not stop Whitebeard, who was about to swing his attack at Nami. However, at that

moment, his eyes narrowed in shock when he realized that just a moment ago, Ren had been standing with the other Warlords. He had used Shave multiple times and reached directly in front of Whitebeard, leaving the onlookers in disbelief. Was he planning to fight Whitebeard?

Given the latter's weapon, infused with the power of the Quake Fruit, all the spectators could feel an invisible pressure at the sight of the towering figure of Whitebeard.

But now Ren was volunteering to face his attack?

This left many people in shock. Some of Ren's enemies were delighted by his predicament,

while his allies watched neutrally, but his crew in Alabasta, Nami, and Miketa were

All these different thoughts raced through everyone's minds in just a moment. In reality, Ren

"I'm glad you came, brat!" Whitebeard bellowed with immense anger as the naginata rose

into the air, a symbol of his absolute power. Ren looked at the massive weapon, feeling the sense of danger rise in his heart.

Ren's Observation Haki warned him, with warning sounds echoing in his mind.' I have to

escape!', 'This attack could destroy my body!' .he whispered to himself, realizing that his body would not be able to withstand this powerful strike. But he did not retreat! If he backed down, it would be his woman who would be hit by this attack! "Whitebeard!!!" Ren shouted furiously, just before Whitebeard lunged at him. From his eyes shot out blazing rays of fire like lasers; the heat emitted from his counterattack surpassed even the flames of Ace's Devil Fruit! The flames danced in the air, igniting everything around

them.

"You're bold, but you won't be able to stop me!" Whitebeard replied, tightening his grip on

the naginata. With a single movement, he unleashed a strike like a storm, crashing down on

blow was incredibly brutal, sending him flying towards a warship that was docked nearby. "Whitebeard!!!!" were Ren's last words before crashing into the warship and destroying it. An absolute silence fell over the battlefield...

Chapter 120: [119] The True War beginning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2100 Words]

Under the overwhelming power of Whitebeard, Ren's body was hurled like a kite in a violent storm, smashing into the anchored battleship along with a fleet of Marine ships. The massive structure, made of sturdy wood and reinforced with iron, was reduced to a pile of wreckage. The ship's glass windows shattered, and debris flew through the air as thick plumes of smoke rose high into the sky, turning the scene into a nightmare that struck fear into the hearts of all who witnessed it.

One blow! One single blow was enough to crush the powerful warlord Ren!

The younger spectators, who had only heard of Whitebeard's legendary strength through stories, now believed those tales. Meanwhile, the older ones trembled as they recalled the terrifying force that Whitebeard was-and still is a monstrous figure, even in his old age.

At Sabaody Archipelago.

The usual chatter and noise fell into an unnatural silence as journalists and civilians of all ages watched the live broadcast, their mouths hanging open in disbelief.

"So, this is the legendary power of Whitebeard..."

"Did... did the Black Death Ren really die?!"

"There's no movement, and the dust cloud is blocking the view, so I can't be sure..."

"I don't think he'd die from just one Attack..."

Conversations erupted among the crowd, while journalists eagerly snapped photos of the scene, driven by both greed and awe.

"Is this the end for the ambitious man?"

Away from the civilians' noise, atop a giant tree, an old man with white hair watched the live broadcast with a neutral expression. This man was none other than the legendary right-hand man of the former Pirate King, the Dark King Rayleigh!

....

In Alabasta.

"Captain... h-he..." Vivi's eyes widened as she covered her mouth with her hands, tears welling up in her eyes, threatening to fall.

She wasn't the only one with such a sorrowful expression; the Sin crew had been in a foul mood ever since Whitebeard's attack on Ren.

"No... Captain Ren won't be defeated that easily. Trust him, Vivi," Robin said, trying to reassure Vivi and the others. Her words seemed to calm them, but what they didn't know was that Robin's own heartbeat was erratic-she was deeply worried about Ren at that moment.

"Brother..." Leila muttered softly, her voice filled with tension.

Kalifa remained silent, her cold expression unchanged. Internally, she thought: 'He's just a target to spy on, like I used to do, so I don't need to worry about him... On the contrary, if he dies, that would be to my advantage. I could leave this place sooner. But... why am I feeling this strange, unfamiliar sensation?'

At that moment, Kalifa didn't realize that what she was feeling was concern or maybe she was pretending not to know. Who could tell?

Regardless of Kalifa's thoughts, Zala turned to the anxious Kaya and said in an attempt to reassure her, "I think he'll be alright... after all, he's Captain Ren."

"Mm... that's right, the captain won't go down so easily!" Kaya nodded quickly, without a hint of hesitation.

....

Back at Marineford.

The battlefield remained silent. Marco, who had been rushing to save Vista and Jozu, glanced at the spot where Ren had fallen, his expression gleeful. He then shouted loudly, full of joy, "This is pops power! You got what you deserved!"

"Fufufufu- Whitebeard's strength really is something else..." Doflamingo laughed wickedly, but his gaze toward Whitebeard was cautious and filled with fear. "But is that really enough to kill you, Black Death?"

'So, this is the power of the world's strongest man... 'Hancock whispered under her breath, barely audible, as she gazed at the destroyed battleship. Her face showed an unreadable expression.

"Is this really his end? I don't think so..." Mihawk spoke briefly, his piercing gaze seeming to cut through the dust and debris, focusing on Ren's battered body after Whitebeard's strike. On the Marine side, Tashigi, who was standing next to Hina, struggled to hide her concern. She silently prayed in her heart: 'Captain Ren, please be okay!'

At the same time, Hina muttered, "It seems Hina won't be able to avenge Smoker-kun and the rest of the Marines on her own..."

"So scary~ If I took that hit, my body would've been completely obliterated..." Kizaru said in his usual slow, dull tone, glancing at Whitebeard and adding a false hint of "fear." "Arara... it looks like Whitebeard is really angry!" Aokiji, who had earlier exchanged blows with Whitebeard, was preparing for a serious battle with him.

"Hmph, pirates fighting each other-there's nothing better to watch than this," Akainu sneered coldly.

Back with Marco, who was enjoying Ren's defeat, a stark contrast to his joyful mood was Mikita, who had been following him, utterly shocked. Gradually, her shock turned into sorrow, then into rage as she heard Marco's voice. Clenching her fists, she rushed toward Marco at full speed: "Shut up, you son of a bitch!!"

Marco, who had transformed into his phoenix form and was flying in the sky, was ready for Mikita's attack. However, at that very moment, Nami appeared behind him, using Moonwalk. With a cold expression on her beautiful face, she swung her sword mercilessly at his head, aiming to kill!

Marco had no idea that Nami was currently angrier than anyone else due to Whitebeard's attack on Ren. Her killing intent had reached an entirely new level, incomparable to when she fought Jozu and Vista.

"Shit...!" Marco felt danger from both sides. Using Observation Haki, he swiftly dodged and flew away from both Nami and Mikita.

"Damn... These two women are insane," Marco muttered as he realized he was sweating coldly, despite his fiery abilities.

"Rorororo! Pops' power is unbeatable! Who cares if it's the mysterious Warlord? Who cares if he beat Crocodile? Pops is simply invincible!" A man from Whitebeard's crew laughed maniacally amidst the battlefield's tense silence.

This man was Rakuyo, the Seventh Division Commander of the Whitebeard Pirates.

Rakuyo was a tall man with dark blonde braids. He had a mustache consisting of two sharp, diagonal points. His typical pirate attire included a yellow coat with green linings, a green vest under an unbuttoned shirt, a light green pirate sash, checkered pants, and plain boots. He approached the wreckage of the destroyed warship slowly, a mocking grin on his face. "Look at you, dying like a dog!" he sneered, laughing. After all, it was Ren who had captured Ace and cut off his arm. Members of Ren's crew had also killed one of Rakuyo's brothers and severely injured two others. His hatred was fully justified.

At that moment, Rakuyo's actions were being closely observed by everyone on the battlefield. Some were waiting for him to finish off Ren, who appeared unable to stand, while others thought of using Rakuyo as a test to check Ren's condition.

"You bastard... I remember your face well!" Mikita yelled in rage. The smile she had worn earlier had long since vanished, replaced with an expression filled with murderous intent. Nami, standing beside her, glared coldly at Rakuyo. But in the next moment, a smile spread

across her face.

"Rakuyo! Fall back!" Whitebeard shouted at his son, telling him to retreat, but it was too late!

A powerful arm emerged from the dust cloud, grabbing Rakuyo by the neck. Ren rose from the wreckage without a scratch on his body or a tear in his clothes!

In truth, Whitebeard's attack had destroyed Ren's clothes, and even the defense of his "Eternal Clothes" wasn't enough to completely withstand the full force of the attack. However, they helped reduce its impact. Along with his Armament Haki, Ren only suffered 50% of the attack's true power!

Yet, even with the power halved, it was still something Ren's current body couldn't endure. That's why he had been thrown like a broken kite, crashing into the warship and destroying it. But while the world was stunned by his clash with Whitebeard, Ren had purchased a mid-level healing potion from the system shop and consumed it!

If it weren't for the bloodstains on the upper half of his body, people might have not believe that he had taken Whitebeard's attack head-on!

Back in the present, Ren stood on his feet with a cold expression. He looked at Rakuyo, who was suspended in the air by one hand.

Ren was holding him up by the neck with one arm, while his left hand formed a fist, enveloped

in Haki. "Rakuyo!! Stop, you-!!" Marco yelled as he realized what Ren was about to do. He tried flying toward him to stop him, but there was no way he could be faster than Ren's fist, which pierced through Rakuyo's abdomen, tearing apart his internal organs and guts.

"Blaaaaargh!!"

Pain overtook Rakuyo as he felt the crushing grip tighten around his neck. His eyes widened in terror as muffled screams escaped his throat, turning into a torrent of blood-soaked vomit. A gurgling, choking sound filled the air as it became clear his insides were shredded by the sheer force of the blow. The stench of blood filled the surroundings, and at that moment, Rakuyo knew his fate was sealed.

Ignoring the system's notification, Ren tossed Rakuyo's lifeless body to the ground. He then looked at the battlefield, his gaze sweeping over the others. "It seems I've disappointed some of you here, haven't I?" His words were directed at those who had rejoiced in his supposed 'death'. The people in question trembled in fear, while Whitebeard could only stare at his son's lifeless body, ignoring Ren's taunting completely.

"Captain Ren!" Mikita shouted joyfully, flying toward Ren at high speed, killing a few pirates along the way who had mocked her captain after Whitebeard's strike.

Nami felt the same rush of happiness and couldn't hold back her wide smile. Similarly, many others across the world-more precisely, many women-were thrilled by Ren's survival. Whether in Alabasta's royal palace, among the Marines, or in Skypiea, every woman who had a good impression of Ren was relieved by his escape.

Nami decided it was time to reunite with Ren. Grabbing the unconscious Vista and Jozu with

one hand using a rope founded in the battlefield, she dragged them across the battlefield

toward Ren.

"Damn it! I can't let her take Jozu and Vista!" Marco, remembering the mission Whitebeard

had given him, shook off his grief over Rakuyo's death and was about to attack Nami again

when his Observation Haki picked up something unbelievable. 'My son... forgive me for dragging you into this pointless war-'

Before Whitebeard could finish his sentence, a large sword pierced through his stomach,

emerging from his back!

"SQUARD!!! What have you done, you bastard?!" Marco's face turned to pure horror as he screamed at Squard, who had stabbed Whitebeard with a calm expression on his face.

"This can't be... Captain Squard... a traitor?"

"Pops... he's bleeding..."

All the fighters on the battlefield watched this new drama unfold in eerie silence. The focus had already shifted from Ren to Whitebeard, who had been stabbed by his own "son." Seizing the moment while no one was paying attention, Ren quietly leaped into the air using Moonwalk, landing beside Nami and Mikita who have been stopped by Marco, they hugged him in silence. Smiling at them, he reassured them, "I'm fine, girls."the he turned his gaze to Whitebeard, who was now kneeling on one knee, staring at Squard with a look full of sorrow. "Squard... what are you doing?" Whitebeard's voice was filled with anger and killing intent. Squard's expression wavered as he heard the tone and saw Whitebeard's towering figure glaring down at him. Trembling in fear, Squard mustered his courage and spoke.

He began explaining to everyone on the battlefield how Whitebeard intended to sell out his

allies to the Marines in exchange for Ace's freedom and the safety of his own crew. His so- called "proof" was how the Marines' cannons and soldiers seemed to focus strangely on the allies rather than Whitebeard's main crew members.

As Squard's excuses filled the air, Whitebeard's expression softened, then turned cold again. Just when everyone expected him to kill Squard, Whitebeard did something no one anticipated-except for Ren. He hugged Squard!

"Pathetic..." Ren sneered disdainfully at Whitebeard's actions. The stab from Squard was critical, significantly weakening Whitebeard's ability to free Ace and fight against the

Admirals.

"You foolish son!" Whitebeard stood, leaving the tearful Squard behind. "You've been

deceived, you fool..."

His gaze then turned icy as he looked toward Sengoku and the rest of the Marines, shouting,

"And now, they'll pay the price! Those who trust me, fight with me-let's destroy the Navy!"

*ROAR!!!*

In the moments following Whitebeard's words, the battlefield erupted with more intensity than ever before.

Whitebeard had officially joined the Battle!

Chapter 121: [120] Ren VS Marco
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2100 Words]

The battlefield erupted once again when Whitebeard entered the fray personally. The pirates' morale soared to the skies once more, leading to the swift deaths of many Marines in mere moments. The sounds of gunfire and cannon blasts were accompanied by the splatter of blood and the slicing of bodies by swords in every direction. The brutal scene resembled a painting straight out of the depths of hell.

The cloud-filled sky over Marineford hinted at an impending catastrophe, but the fighters on the corpse-strewn ground paid no attention to the weather. All that mattered to them was survival—and killing as many enemies as possible.

"Death to the pirates!" one Marine soldier shouted as he blew off the head of a fallen pirate with his rifle. The soldier's triumph was short-lived as his eyes widened in horror at the sight of a sword piercing through his chest.

"Y-you--" These were the soldier's last words before collapsing dead. The pirate who had struck him laughed as he pulled his sword from the Marine's chest, but his laughter was cut short when his own head was swiftly sliced off by another Marine.

Scenes like these unfolded all over Marineford. Life had lost its value in this moment. It seemed as if both sides had descended into madness, becoming killing machines-existing only to slaughter!

"W-what's happening... in the name of God..." a boy with pink hair muttered, fleeing the battlefield, terror etched on his face. A boy with blond hair and an odd hairstyle followed closely behind him.

"Koby, wait! Where the hell are you going?!" Helmeppo shouted in panic, running at full speed from the infernal chaos.

Without stopping, Koby replied, "This is madness... I have to hide!"

Helmeppo couldn't deny that he was just as frightened as Koby. But he also knew that deserting the battlefield was considered disgraceful for a Marine. Who could blame them, though? After all, both he and Koby had only joined the Marines two months ago!

Battles of this scale were simply too much for them!

"Koby, watch out!!" Helmeppo screamed as he saw a pirate, with a savage expression, swinging his sword, aiming for Koby's neck!

"!!!"

Koby, hearing Helmeppo's warning, froze for a moment, but soon felt a chill run through his entire body. Death was closing in on him.

He shut his eyes, realizing it was too late to avoid the attack. But the expected pain never came. He opened his eyes to see the pirate who had targeted him standing still, shock and hatred frozen on his face.

"Hmm... are you alright, young Marine?" The pirate collapsed dead, revealing the figure of Koby's savior.

"Th-the Black Death... a W-war... lord..." Helmeppo stammered as he recognized the man who saved Koby, while Koby looked at Ren with a mixture of fear and gratitude.

No one could blame him-who wouldn't be afraid of such a powerful pirate? The only thing that reassured Koby was that Ren, Currently, was a Warlord on the side of the Marines...

Otherwise, his death would have been certain.

"Th-thank you for saving me, sir!" Koby gathered his courage and expressed his gratitude.

Ren looked down at the boy and thought, 'He could be useful...'

He broke off his thoughts, keeping his expression cold, and said to Koby, "No problem... I'm sure you'll repay me for this favor in the future... you'll pay me well..."

"Huh?" Koby looked at him in confusion, but in the next moment, his eyes became void of emotion. His face turned as cold and lifeless as a doll's.

Ren had used his hypnosis skill again!

Taking advantage of the battlefield's chaos, Ren glanced at Helmeppo, who stood a bit farther away, then leaned toward the hypnotized Koby and whispered in a low voice, ensuring only Koby could hear him: "You will become my secret Spy... from today onward, my orders are absolute. You are not allowed to resist, not allowed to defy me, not allowed to question my decisions, and you must not raise suspicion about yourself. Be cautious. You will report to me about the situation in Marineford every month. Make sure our communications are neither spied on nor traced. If the risk is too high, cancel the report, but if something urgent happens, you must inform me immediately, even if your life is at risk."

Taking a deep breath, Ren spoke again in a cold voice: "And you must act naturally, like your usual self. Do you understand everything I've said?"

When Ren finished speaking, Koby nodded with the same empty expression. After Ren clapped his hands, Koby's face returned to normal.

Ren vanished from sight, heading elsewhere, just as Helmeppo ran up to Koby, asking, "Are you alright, Koby? Did you get hurt?"

"N-no... I'm fine, just... that Warlord... he's truly terrifying!" Koby swallowed nervously, his expression still shaken.

"No doubt about it! Let's find a place to hide!"

"Aye!"

....

Back to Ren.

He had returned to Nami and Mikita, who were fighting Marco. Marco's goal was to free Vista and Jozu, but Ren was about to settle the matter with those two.

"Hey, Marco," Ren called out loudly, causing Marco-who had been pressuring both Nami and Mikita in combat-to lift his head and look at Ren standing in front of the unconscious

Jozu and Vista.

With a frustrated expression, Marco shouted, "You bastard! If you dare touch them—"

"Blah, blah, blah... Always with the same threatening nonsense, aren't you, Marco? You think your words always carry a value..." Ren mocked with a harsh expression, then transformed his weapon from a sword into a multi-shot gun.

"What are you planning to do? Stop!" Marco yelled as blue flames flared up around him even stronger, forcing Nami and Mikita to retreat due to the intense heat and land back on the

ground.

"You'll see what I'll do... Die!"

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

With a cold smile, Ren pulled the trigger. In the next moment, shots of Blood Metal energy from Versa, now in its gun form, exploded the heads of both Vista and Jozu.

The heads of both Vista and Jozu were reduced to ash by the explosive red energy, leaving massive craters in the ground, showcasing the destructive power of the Blood Metal weapon,

"Vesta."

"Noooo!! Jozu!! Vista!!" Marco screamed in fury, launching himself at Ren with murderous

intent.

Ignoring the system notifications, Ren commanded Miss System to extract Jozu's Devil Fruit. As Marco's scream echoed and he shot towards Ren like a bullet, Ren grinned wickedly and shifted his weapon back into sword form, sheathing it.

The next moment, Marco's kick-coated in Armament Haki and blue flames-was aimed at Ren's face! But with the help of Observation Haki, Ren dodged the attack with precision. Without hesitation, Ren launched a counterattack, clenching his fist tightly, coating it with Armament Haki, and delivering a powerful punch to Marco's face.

*Boom!!*

"Rage only clouds your judgment! Rahahaha!"

Ren was genuinely excited at this moment. Nothing got a man's adrenaline pumping-except for sex-more than battle! Bloody fights! Where the risk of injury or death was high! This was the essence of men, their true nature and spirit. They were savage creatures that thrived on

strength!

Marco crashed to the ground with a swollen face that quickly healed, then rose once more, charging violently toward Ren, who was now hovering in the sky using Moonwalk.

"Ho-In!!"

Marco coldly yelled out the name of his attack as he prepared to launch a powerful strike.

While in mid-air, Marco's phoenix claws gathered energy into a swirling blue flame and launched a massive fireball at Ren!

"This..." Ren recognized the attack from his past life, so without hesitation, he drew his sword to defend, his expression serious.

"One-Sword Style: Death Blade!"

*Boom!*

*Whoosh!*

In the sky, a captivating scene unfolded on the battlefield, even catching Whitebeard's attention as he clashed with Akainu and Kizaru. But he quickly refocused on his enemies.

Back to Ren and Marco's fight-the giant sword slash collided with the massive blue fireball, creating a loud explosion on the battlefield. After a few seconds, the blue flame ball swallowed Ren's sword slash but had lost much of its size!

Ren advanced toward the remaining smaller fireball and sliced through it with his sword

coated in Armament Haki. then He resheathed his sword, covering his fists with Haki again, and moved toward Marco.

Why he wasn't fighting with his sword? Simply because Marco's regenerative abilities were exceptional. Fighting him with a sword would be tedious, and wasting his stamina on someone who could heal from mortal wounds would be inefficient. He needed to find a way to capture Marco after beating him down. Perhaps he could find a chance to use Seastone

shackles?

Even without that, physical combat against Marco was still beneficial-Ren could feel his body growing stronger, evolving. He had never felt this much pressure from any enemy since arriving in this world. He had always been a step-or ten steps-ahead of the rookies from the Four Seas and Paradise. But now, things were different.

As if to confirm Ren's thoughts, Marco quickly launched his next attack without hesitation,

despite the setback of his previous one.

"Onguru!"

Marco's leg transformed into a phoenix leg with four sharp talons, and he kicked at Ren with

astonishing speed, far faster than before!

"Ugh..!!" Even though Ren's Observation Haki warned him of the imminent danger, his reaction time wasn't fast enough. Luckily, his entire body was coated in Armament Haki, along with the defense of his clothing. He endured the pain and grabbed Marco's leg, still in

its Zoan form.

"I'll roast you today, Little bird!"

*Boom!*

Ren grabbed Marco's leg and slammed him into the ground with tremendous force, causing

Marco to spit blood and struggle for breath. But Marco had no time to rest, as Ren's next

attack had already arrived.

*Boom!!*

Ren's next kick was meant to obliterate Marco's head, but Marco barely dodged in time. Ren

turned to face him, glaring as Marco's body became engulfed in blue flames, visibly healing

his wounds.

"I can't let him recover," Ren thought, charging swiftly toward Marco. Pirates and Marines

alike who stood in his path were sent flying, their bodies torn apart by the sheer power of his

strikes.

Meanwhile, Marco, now fully healed, exhaled deeply, his face turning stern as an ominous aura radiated from him.

"Phoenix Gōen!!" Marco unleashed a massive fiery shockwave from his wings, flying toward

Ren with lethal intent. Ren didn't break his stride, hurtling toward the flames until both

forces collided violently.

"Hell Eyes!!"

*Roar!!*

Marco let out a thunderous roar, propelling himself like a blazing arrow. The blue and red

flames clashed in midair, generating a tremendous explosion that shook the battlefield and

dramatically raised the temperature in the area.

Marco's flames were primarily defensive, meant for healing. In contrast, Ren's Hellfire

technique was a vicious, destructive attack-designed to kill.

"Zōn-kei... Genjūshu!!"

Marco emerged from the fiery explosion with minor burns, moving quickly to seize Ren-who

was coated in Armament Haki-with the intent to finish him off.

His plan was simple: lift his opponent high into the air, slam him down, and follow up with a

skull-crushing strike capable of shattering bones.

"Naive!" Ren sneered, vanishing with Shave, closing the gap between them in an instant. He delivered a powerful punch to Marco's midsection.

*Bam!*

Marco's eyes widened, a sharp pain ripping through his insides. He tried to retreat, but Ren's

grip was like steel, locking him in place.

"Damn you! Let go of me, you-"

*Boom!!*

Marco couldn't finish his insult as his face was smashed into the ground with brutal force. The

impact carved a large crater, cracks spidering across the ground.

*Boom!*

*Boom!*

*Boom!*

Ren showed no mercy, grabbing Marco by the leg and slamming him into the earth

repeatedly. Despite Marco's regenerative flames, the relentless assault left him severely

weakened.

Ren tightened his grip, lifting Marco by the neck. His other fist, enveloped in Haki, struck Marco's abdomen with crushing force. Marco coughed up a torrent of blood before collapsing, unconscious.

Reaching into The Infinity Space, then he pulled out Seastone cuffs and shackled Marco's

wrists.

"In the end...it seems I'm the one who won this fight."
