Chapter 122: [121] A Legend's Last Stand
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2400 Words]

"Captain Marco has been captured!" one of the Whitebeard Pirates shouted, alerting his comrades, who quickly widened their eyes in shock upon seeing their commander

unconscious and bound in handcuffs.

"We have to save him!" declared another pirate, though hesitation was clearly visible on his face.

"We can't do it with our current strength... it would be nothing short of suicide!"

"B-but... what will happen to Captain Marco?"

...

Meanwhile, Ren, who had carried Marco back to Nami and Mikita's side, glanced at the girls and pondered deeply.

He was contemplating what to do with Marco. Should he imprison him and try to recruit him into his fleet later?

This would be incredibly difficult without using hypnosis, and even then, the chances of Marco breaking free were relatively high since Marco was close to Ren in overall combat power.

Ren's hypnosis skills were not infallible. The basic requirement for hypnosis to succeed was that the target had to be weaker than the user. The greater the power difference between Ren and his target, the higher the chances of either success or failure of the hypnosis.

'So, should I just kill him?' Ren thought about killing Marco and giving his Devil Fruit to Kaya or another crew member.

This was because he realized it was impossible to escape this battlefield with Marco as a captive.

First, the World Government might demand he hand Marco over to be imprisoned in Impel Down and publicize the event to boost their reputation among the seas. If that really happened, Ren wouldn't hesitate to comply.

Why? It was obvious-the World Government was strong! Far too strong for him to oppose or challenge their authority!

Who cares about the laws and the rights of a Warlord? When the World Government speaks, all laws are nullified. Just like what happened in Ohara and several other islands that had been wiped from history over the eight centuries of the World Government's rule.

These were the privileges of the powerful, and the weak had no choice but to obey!

This wasn't the only reason Ren believed he couldn't take Marco out alive. There was another issue... Red-Haired Shanks!

Based on what he had seen in the original story, Shanks would ask Ren to hand over Marco. Shanks would ensure Whitebeard and Ace received proper burials, and of course, Marco would need to be present at the funeral of his captain and brother.

Ren could only look at the unconscious Marco and think: 'Your luck is really bad. The person who's supposed to save you is the one making me kill you right here and now...or maybe not...'

"Captain Ren, what are you thinking about?" Nami's warm voice interrupted his thoughts. He looked at his orange-haired girl and leaned in to kiss her, gently brushing her lips, while ignoring Mikita's strange mix of embarrassment and jealousy.

"I was just deciding this man's fate," he said, gesturing toward the unconscious Marco lying on the ground.

"So, are you going to kill him?" Mikita asked, after pushing aside her earlier embarrassment. "Kill him? No, no. Do you think I'm cruel enough to kill an unarmed, unconscious man?" Ren smiled at her with a teasing grin, and Mikita puffed her cheeks slightly, playfully hitting his broad chest with her small white hands, before scolding him in an annoyed, childlike voice: "Mou~! Captain Ren, you're playing this game again!"

"Rahaha..." Ren chuckled lightly and patted Mikita's hair as she blushed, pressed against his chest. Then, his expression turned cold again as he crouched down beside Marco, taking out a small glass bottle containing a purple liquid.

This was the poison Kaya had developed earlier, the same one Ren had used on Crocodile!

"You won't die right now, and I'll spare myself the trouble of killing you Infront of people. But your Devil Fruit is far too tempting, so your fate is already sealed..." Ren smiled coldly as he poured the contents of the bottle into Marco's mouth and stood up after removing the Sea- Prism Stone handcuffs that had restrained him.

"This poison will slowly weaken your body until it reaches vital organs like your heart, lungs, and kidneys. Once that happens, your death will be inevitable..." Ren wasn't speaking to anyone in particular, but it seemed he had already decided Marco's fate.

Because of Marco's strong Body, it would take some time for the poison to kill him. However, since he had ingested the poison and his Devil Fruit powers were suppressed by the Sea-Prism Stone, there were no concerns about the poison failing to take effect.

Of course, this poison couldn't kill just anyone. People with extraordinary bodies like Big Mom, Kaido, and even certain unique individuals wouldn't be affected by this type of poison. Normally, Marco should have been one of those individuals, especially since he possessed a Devil Fruit with incredible regenerative abilities. However, his previous battle with Ren had left his body severely weakened, and his immune system was powerless to stop the lethal poison from spreading.

Of course, if it weren't for the Sea-Prism Stone, Marco's Devil Fruit powers would have responded, protecting his internal organs with blue flames and burning the poison away...

But for Marco, his luck had run out. By the time he awoke, it would already be too late to resist the spreading poison.

...

"Hellhound!!"

Admiral Akainu roared fiercely, launching a colossal hound made of searing magma at the towering figure of Whitebeard. The fiery creature appeared capable of melting both the air

and the ground beneath it.

The battlefield of Marineford was engulfed in chaos, with the sounds of combat echoing all around and explosions shaking the very earth. Yet, amid this turmoil, the clash between Whitebeard and Akainu, alongside Kizaru, overshadowed everything.

Whitebeard advanced, his massive frame radiating power despite the minor injuries he had sustained, including the wound inflicted by Squard. His steely gaze remained fixed on Akainu, who hurled a gigantic molten fist in his direction.

"Gurarara! Are you trying to light a birthday cake here, you magma brat?" Whitebeard taunted with a confident and mocking tone.

*Boom!*

*Zzzzzz!*

*Splash!*

Akainu chose not to respond verbally and attacked immediately. His magma-clad fist surged forward like a blazing thunderbolt aimed at Whitebeard. With lightning speed, Whitebeard raised his massive naginata to intercept the strike. Sparks erupted as the molten rock collided with his weapon, which was enveloped in Armament Haki. The magma splashed and ignited

unlucky pirates nearby.

With a sneer, Whitebeard declared, "That's not enough!"

Unwilling to retreat, Akainu intensified the magma swirling around him, making his fist burn even hotter and brighter. He lunged forward with renewed ferocity. Yet, Whitebeard quickly countered by thrusting his hand to trigger a violent earthquake beneath Akainu's feet. The ground cracked open, almost swallowing the Marine Admiral, but Akainu leapt just in time, redirecting his energy into a massive magma punch aimed skyward.

At that moment, a blinding flash of golden light descended from above. It was Kizaru, casually pointing his finger at Whitebeard. "Wow, things are really heating up here!" he quipped in his usual teasing manner, firing off a barrage of light beams toward Whitebeard at

incredible speed.

*PEW! PEW! PEW!*

Whitebeard raised his weapon once more, easily blocking the incoming barrage. The beams of

light collided with his naginata, and despite their immense speed, he had no trouble keeping up. The clash illuminated the battlefield with a dazzling explosion. "You Marines are really annoying..." Whitebeard growled, raising his arm to unleash a colossal shockwave that shook

the very skies.

Kizaru retreated at light speed but seized the opportunity to release several more beams from different angles, attempting to flank Whitebeard's defenses. However, Whitebeard's reflexes were astonishing, and he countered each attack with pinpoint accuracy.

Akainu, now repositioned, prepared for another assault. "Kizaru! Don't let him destroy Marineford with his damned devil fruit!" he shouted as his hand morphed into a massive ball of molten lava. He hurled it toward Whitebeard, but this time, it wasn't a single punch; it was a barrage of flowing magma, attempting to engulf him from every direction. "Gurarara!" Whitebeard's wild, arrogant laughter echoed across the battlefield. In the next instant, he raised his hand, unleashing another massive shockwave aimed directly at Akainu and Kizaru. The force of the quake was so immense that the surrounding buildings in Marineford began to crumble, and the very earth split beneath their feet.

Kizaru dodged effortlessly at light speed while Akainu braced himself to face the attack head-

on. But the quake was more than even he could endure. With a furious roar, Akainu formed

another magma hound, obliterating the shockwave in a fiery display of power.

In the following moment, Kizaru, who had previously been retreating, took aim at

Whitebeard's exposed torso. With a swift movement, he fired a deadly laser straight for

Whitebeard's abdomen.

Whitebeard, ever alert, raised his naginata to block the beam. But just then, a sharp, searing pain shot through him-the wound from Squard's earlier betrayal.

"Aghh...!!" Whitebeard groaned in pain as Kizaru's laser pierced his gut, leaving a small but

deep hole that passed clean through his body.

"Oyaji!!" The Whitebeard Pirates watching from afar screamed in horror and concern.

But Akainu wasn't about to let such an opportunity slip away. He lunged toward Whitebeard with the ferocity of a predator closing in on its prey, his molten fist now capable of melting

even diamond.

"Die! Your era ends here- at my hands!" Akainu roared as his molten fist smashed into Whitebeard's chest, leaving a horrifying crater and searing marks across his body. At that moment, everyone watching the battle widened their eyes in shock.

"Is this your end, Whitebeard?" Doflamingo muttered as he observed the battle from a

distance.

Hancock remained silent, her expression cold, but this time she was seated atop a small mound of shattered stone statues, remnants of both pirates and Marines. To Hancock, there

was no distinction between them; they were all merely men.

||||

Kuma's expression was as stoic as ever, not a word escaping his lips.

"This war is truly nearing its end... it's a decisive victory for the Marines," Mihawk thought

aloud, a serious look on his face.

Suddenly, his expression shifted as he spotted someone flying toward Whitebeard and the

admirals.

"What does he intend to do?"

In the execution square, Aokiji, who was with Sengoku, glanced at the figure racing toward Whitebeard. He was about to intervene, but Sengoku's arm halted him.

"Use your head, Aokiji. If he wanted to betray us, he wouldn't have performed so well in this

war," Sengoku said, his voice imbued with wisdom.

"You're right..." Aokiji replied.

Back to Whitebeard, who had just been struck by two fatal attacks. He looked at Akainu, whose

face bore a look of arrogance, and gathered his strength in anger, gripping Akainu by the

collar!

"I thought you...*gasp*...were going to end...*gasp* my era, you scoundrel?" he said, his voice weak yet filled with immense pride.

Akainu's expression shifted as he attempted to break free from Whitebeard's grip, but it was

futile; Whitebeard's hand was like a vise.

*Roar!*

Whitebeard roared and delivered a punch infused with the power of earthquakes at Akainu, who spat a large mouthful of blood and was sent flying back several meters!

Standing tall, Whitebeard gazed at the battlefield with the eyes of a legendary pirate and

shouted, "I am The Whitebeard!"

Suddenly, a warning from his Observation Haki alerted him to a hidden danger behind him.

He quickly turned, enduring the pain in his chest, and launched a punch infused with his Devil

Fruit powers toward Ren, who had just entered the battlefield!

"You're back, Brat?! Take this!"

*Crack!*

*Boom!*

Whitebeard struck Ren with a swift, oppressive punch, sending him flying backward for

several meters!

However...

To Whitebeard's astonishment, Ren sprang back up from the debris once again and charged

toward him!

But this time, Whitebeard felt a lethal danger emanating from Ren's body!

There was something different about him this time! Ren's skin began to emit steam, and his complexion turned a reddish-pink hue. His

Moonwalk skill had multiplied his speed several times, quickly closing the distance between him and Whitebeard!

'I'll finish him off before the effect of the Rage Pill wears off!' Ren resolved firmly in his heart.

Indeed, Ren had consumed the Rage Pill he received as a reward after the recent events in

Skypiea.

The pill's effect was straightforward: it tripled the user's combat power. Returning to the present, Ren tightened his fist, now saturated with Busoshoku Haki, which

began to show signs of development, and struck Whitebeard's face with a speed

imperceptible to the naked eye!

*Boom!*

"Old man!!" Ace shouted, still shackled to the execution platform, sorrow filling his voice as

he witnessed his father being struck violently. Despair deepened in his heart, and self- loathing grew within him. "Fleet Admiral! The executioners are ready!" At that moment, a Marine soldier informed

Sengoku of the situation. Upon hearing this, Ace could only feel fear creeping into his heart...

He would die...

In a most painful manner.

Returning to Whitebeard.

Despite the immense power behind Ren's punch, Whitebeard did not fall. He contained the pain on his face and delivered a powerful punch toward Ren's exposed flank! Ren's Observation Haki rang alarms in his mind, so he was already prepared. He used

Moonwalk to leap and twisted his body in an acrobatic move to evade Whitebeard's attack, which literally shattered the air!

At that moment, countless people around the world were watching this battle in shock. Since

the beginning of Whitebeard's fight against Akainu and Kizaru, Ren's intervention had made them realize the true nature of their world and the incredible characters that inhabited it! Countless eyes across the seas were glued to Ren's agile figure as he exchanged blows with Whitebeard. Despite quickly finding himself in an unfavorable position, he held his ground remarkably well! He even countered with fierce attacks, especially when he drew his sword,

which had cut down countless enemies! "Hellhound!" "Yasuki no Majita!"

"Blade of Death!"

Soon, the two admirals joined the fray. They aided each other in defending against Whitebeard's attacks, but they paid no attention to Ren's situation. The latter reciprocated in

kind; time was running out, and he needed to deliver the fatal blow to Whitebeard himself!

Why?

Of course, it was for the Quake Fruit! Whitebeard had to die here, at his hands!

To be continued.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 123: [122] One Piece Is Real !
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[3000 Words]

The battlefield was more chaotic than ever before, yet a fundamental change was occurring. The pirates were being driven to despair by the Marine soldiers, and while the casualty numbers among the Marines were decreasing, the desperate attacks from the pirates became more intense and frenzied.

The situation worsened for the pirates as many of their first-tier combat forces, including leaders and allied commanders, had been killed. Powerful individuals like Jozu, Vista, and Corelle, along with many influential figures in the New World, had fallen.

Fortunately for them, Marco, who had previously been defeated by the Warlord Ren, returned to the battlefield and helped the pirates advance toward the execution platform to rescue Ace. *Bang!*

*Bang!*

*Bang!*

*Boom!*

*Boom!*

The sounds of gunfire and artillery did not cease for a moment. Marco looked toward a distant side of the battlefield with concern: "Pops... you must live!"

He muttered under his breath and refocused on the advancing Marine soldiers, determined to prevent them from reaching the execution platform.

At the same time, Whitebeard was facing three opponents simultaneously: Ren, Akainu, and Kizaru.

What was astonishing was that Whitebeard was still able to hold his ground despite facing three of the strongest warriors on the battlefield.

Under normal circumstances, Ren would barely be considered above average in a battle filled with admirals, Sengoku, Garp, Whitebeard, and Mihawk. However, his power had tripled, and although he hadn't reached the level of those previously mentioned, his body's reactions and speed had witnessed a terrifying improvement.

Unfortunately, this enhancement did not extend to his Haki; otherwise, he would have been able to defeat the wounded Whitebeard alone.

Why wasn't Haki enhanced by the Rage Pill? It was because Haki represented an

understanding of willpower and spirit. It was not something like physical abilities, speed, or swordsmanship that could merely be enhanced through training. Haki required more than just training; one had to take risks and win battle after battle to achieve that kind of qualitative leap in Haki.

So why did Ren and his crew learn this power without having undergone these experiences? The simple answer was: the System!

The System acted as a divine force that disregarded the limitations and rules imposed on developing Haki. Even the dead could be revived through the power of the System, so why would the System have a problem with Haki?

"Hellhound!!"

Ren's thoughts were interrupted by a fierce shout from Akainu, who lunged at Whitebeard with his hand covered in the cursed magma.

Despite being covered in numerous minor wounds and severe gaping holes, Whitebeard remained steadfast, standing like an unyielding mountain. He met Akainu's punch head-on, quickly using his other hand to deflect the laser beams fired at him by Kizaru.

"This old man can still fight despite all these injuries... this is terrifying..." Kizaru said in his usual tone, as he materialized to kick at Whitebeard's head.

"Gurarara!" Whitebeard laughed, reacting quickly. He had already pushed Akainu away and lifted his naginata to block Kizaru's kick, then seized the latter's leg and slammed him to the ground!

*Boom!*

Kizaru's body shattered into particles of yellow light, which quickly dispersed and reformed into Kizaru, who still regarded Whitebeard with a bored expression.

"Death Blade!"

This time, it was Ren who attacked. He felt a sense of urgency as the effect of the Rage Pill would soon wear off. He estimated that he had about five minutes left, but he knew that this time was enough to weaken Whitebeard as long as he and Akainu attacked together!

Swish!

As he swung his sword at Whitebeard, a terrifying sword blade formed, measuring 30 meters in length and 5 meters in width!

This was an attack with his full power, his strength having tripled!

As the blade approached Whitebeard at high speed, it seemed as if the air screamed in pain and space warped under the sharpness of the blade, which appeared capable of splitting the ocean at that moment.

Whitebeard wore a serious expression on his face. Without a second thought, he gritted his teeth, endured the pain in his body, and gathered the power of the Earthquake Fruit in both hands while leaving his weapon planted in the ground. He took a deep breath and struck a massive earthquake ball toward the sword blade!

*Boom!*

*Crack!*

*Screech!*

The sound of the two attacks colliding echoed throughout the battlefield. Ren watched this scene, panting as he chose to retreat to catch his breath. But in the next moment, he smiled fiercely when he saw Kizaru poised to attack Whitebeard, who was still countering his assault.

Whitebeard's eyes widened when he saw Kizaru appear Infront of him at the speed of light, pointing his finger at his head!

*Pew!!*

Laser beams shot from close range, targeting Whitebeard's face!

Just a second before Kizaru's attack, Whitebeard chose to stop resisting the sword blade launched by Ren and swiftly readied his weapon to counter the laser beams!

*Creek!!*

A screeching sound erupted as his Haki-coated naginata met Kizaru's laser beams. The latter regarded this scene with his usual bored expression and took a step back, sensing danger. His instincts proved correct as Whitebeard struck the ground where Kizaru had been standing, causing it to crack and resulting in a devastating earthquake across Marineford!

"Phew..." Ren breathed out, regaining his stamina in just a few seconds, then launched himself like a bullet towards Whitebeard upon seeing Akainu firing several blazing meteors at him once more.

Kizaru quickly ascended into the air, bombarding Whitebeard with dozens of laser beams!

The battle was far from over, but elsewhere on the battlefield, particularly on the execution platform, Sengoku observed the fight from a distance with eyes full of intellect.

No one knew what he was thinking, but in the next moment, his aide rushed in, his face filled with terror, and reported, "Fleet Admiral Sengoku! We have a report!"

"What's happening?" Sengoku turned to his subordinate, commanding him to speak while suppressing the uneasy feeling in his heart.

"T-this... I had previously informed you, sir, that we lost communication with Impel Down..."

"Has communication been restored? What happened? Speak quickly!" Sengoku began to feel anxious as he realized it was related to Impel Down.

Even Kuzan, who stood beside him, could only listen intently to his subordinate.

He had learned earlier from Sengoku that they had lost contact with Marineford, and there was no doubt something was happening there, perhaps a riot from the prisoners or something

similar.

Unfortunately, the admirals could not move to assess the situation in Impel Down due to the onset of the war with Whitebeard. The Marines knew their priorities, so Sengoku suppressed the news and trusted Magellan's ability to handle any prisoner uprisings.

Back with the trembling Marine soldier, he gritted his teeth in fear and said in one breath, "It's Blackbeard... According to the warden's report, Blackbeard has stormed Marineford and

released several dangerous Level 6 prisoners!!"

"!!!"

"What?!" Sengoku and kuzan shouted in unison. Sengoku quickly gazed into the horizon, his expression filled with concern, while Kuzan remained silent but could already feel that chaos would engulf the sea once again...

'So, even if Whitebeard falls here, is it destined for these seas to remain as chaotic as they are now? Or will they become even more chaotic?' he thought internally.

Sengoku and Kuzan's thoughts were interrupted once more by the soldier's voice: "That's not all, Fleet Admiral! We just received a report that the Gate of Justice have opened without permission! And a stolen Marine warship is heading this way!"

"Damn it..."

As the old saying goes, calamities never come singly...

Chaos engulfed Marineford. The sounds of battle between Whitebeard's pirates and the Marines filled the sky, while the ground was littered with debris and bodies, accompanied by the thunderous clashes of immense powers.

Amidst all this turmoil, everyone noticed a strange change in the atmosphere. The fighters who had been engaged in combat paused momentarily, and then, in the next instant, an unfamiliar ship appeared, heading toward the square. All eyes were drawn to that ship, which carried a dark shadow, a silhouette so large that even giants seemed small in comparison. At the ship's bow stood a figure among the varying shadows.

In that moment, all fighting ceased, and everyone turned to gaze at the ship rapidly approaching Marineford. Whitebeard, looking extremely exhausted, stared at the newcomer with eyes filled with rage and shouted, "Teach! You ungrateful son!"

"Zehaha! Father, I see you're in good health, aren't you?"

Marshall D. Teach, known as Blackbeard, stood at the front of his ship with calm and

confident strides, wearing his black cloak, and his sinister laughter echoed in the air. Behind him stood his new crew, a group of the most dangerous prisoners who had escaped from Impel Down after his recent attack. He had formed an unholy alliance with Level 6 criminals, individuals who knew no mercy.

As his ship drew closer to the square, Teach raised his hand, releasing a wave of darkness that engulfed the air around him. His Devil Fruit, the Yami Yami no Mi, showcased its power, covering the sky with layers of darkness, as if night had replaced day in that area. "Zehahahaha! What a magnificent show! It seems I've arrived just in time to make things more exciting!" Blackbeard laughed wickedly and jumped from the ship alongside his crew

members.

'Good... All the players are gathering to play their roles...'

A wicked smile formed on Ren's face when he saw Blackbeard's arrival. Blackbeard's presence

was a sign of the approaching end of this war. He could only laugh silently at the thought of the expression that would be on Blackbeard's face if his plan succeeded.

He focused on Whitebeard, whose body was showing signs of surrender as he retreated silently like a snake waiting for its prey... slowly and patiently.

"Teach... you have committed a forbidden act on my ship," Whitebeard said to him in a

majestic tone.

Blackbeard smiled wickedly upon hearing what his "Pops" said and replied, "Are you

referring to my killing of Tatch? I had no other choice! He found the Darkness Fruit and had no intention of handing it over to me! So I killed him!" Whitebeard looked at him with disappointment, sadness, anger, and all the complex emotions before taking a deep breath and saying, "So all this was for just a Devil Fruit? All

these years?" "Zehahahaha! That's right, Pops!"

"Do not call me 'Pops' again! Teach, you are expelled; from now on, you are my enemy!" Whitebeard shouted angrily, advancing toward Blackbeard, who continued to smile while watching Whitebeard approach him. He quickly ordered his crew to retreat and faced

Whitebeard. "Zehahahaha... You will soon know why this fruit is so amazing!"

Whitebeard did not respond. He concentrated the power of his Devil Fruit into his fist and

punched toward Blackbeard!

Blackbeard grinned wickedly as a black vortex appeared in his hand, then collided with

Whitebeard's massive fist!

The usual destructive scene did not occur; there was no earthquake. It seemed that

Whitebeard's power was being absorbed and consumed by an insatiable black hole. Whitebeard opened his eyes in shock when he saw this scene, then quickly heard Blackbeard's

vile voice again: "Do you see this, old man?! This is the power of the Darkness Fruit! That's

why I killed Tatch! And I will kill anyone who tries to take my treasure!" Blackbeard shouted wickedly, but his expression changed again when he saw Whitebeard lift

his weary body once more and prepare to throw another punch at him!

But this time, without the power of the Earthquake!

*Boom!!*

Blackbeard failed to withstand the punch and fell to the ground with a swollen face.

Whitebeard quickly struck him again multiple times without any resistance.

Thick steam was rising from Whitebeard's body, and it seemed that he was about to reach his

limits! "P-pops! Are you really going to kill me?! I'm your son!!" Blackbeard screamed in terror, but

in his mind, he was thinking of a way out of this predicament. Whitebeard hesitated, and when he saw Blackbeard's hesitation, he was about to order his

crew to kill Whitebeard and empty their bullets into him!

But then!

At that moment, something happened that made all the viewers in Marineford Square and

across the live broadcast widen their eyes!

A long sword pierced through Whitebeard's back and exited from his stomach; it was Blackbeard, who was still lying on the ground, the closest person to this sword.

But the shock didn't stop there; another sword swiftly pierced the other side of Whitebeard!

The area of his right lung!

"Let me join in too~" A soft voice came at that moment as Mikita fell from the sky next to Ren

and Nami. She then unleashed her Armament Haki in her folded umbrella and stabbed it into

the area of Whitebeard's left lung!

Three fatal stabs!

All the viewers were left speechless at that moment, even people like Kizaru and Akainu were

shocked by the perfect timing and speed of the assassination!

Previously, everyone had been focused on the battle between Whitebeard and Blackbeard, so

no one noticed Nami and Mikita, who were lurking with Ren for the perfect opportunity to kill Whitebeard. The two girls were just waiting for the right moment! And they succeeded, perfectly.

"Pops!!!!" Ace screamed in sorrow, tears staining his face; his heart nearly broke! "Pops..." Marco fell to the ground, weakened. He didn't know why, but he had felt weak since

he woke up. When he had awakened, he was confused about why Ren hadn't killed him, but he never knew the reason.

But that didn't matter now because he saw his father being stabbed with fatal wounds Infront

of him and could do nothing but watch.

"This is the moment! Execution, executioners!"

Sengoku shouted loudly, capturing everyone's attention, whether in the battlefield or the

viewers through the live broadcast!

"Ace... I'm sorry..." Whitebeard spoke softly. He didn't let out any groan of pain, but at the

moment he heard Sengoku's shout, he knew Ace's fate had been sealed. Sengoku was very cunning; he ordered Ace's execution immediately after Whitebeard was

fatally stabbed, and the most important factor was that the ones who stabbed Whitebeard were a Warlord and his crew!

What was a Warlord? They were individuals sanctioned by the World Government and not just

random pirates, so they considered themselves to have a stake in the accomplishment of killing Whitebeard.

Under the astonished gaze of the pirates and the shocked Marines, along with hundreds of

millions of people watching everything with their mouths agape, the executioners plunged their spears into Ace's body...

Red blood poured onto the execution platform as Ace lost all signs of life, closing his eyes

with an expression filled with regret and resentment-regret for dragging his brothers into all this and resentment toward himself, for his weakness.

Garp exploded into silent tears, but fortunately, no one had time to focus on him now.

On the other side, small tears streamed from Whitebeard's eyes, but in the next moment, he

lifted his head and moved his body.

"!!!"

"This monster is not dead yet?"

"What is his body made of, for the Creator's sake?!"

Blackbeard's crew and Blackbeard himself shouted when they saw Whitebeard's movement. "Rahahaha... he has no strength left in his body... a mere touch will bring him down." Suddenly, Ren spoke, pulling his bloodied sword from Whitebeard's body, and Nami and

Mikita did the same, drawing their weapons.

Nami looked at the dying Whitebeard with a proud expression; this man was a legend, and she

was one of the ones who dealt the fatal blow!

Mikita smiled as usual, ascending into the sky and opening her umbrella after wiping the

blood off.

There was a deadly silence on the battlefield, and what broke this silence were Whitebeard's

next words: "Not you, Ren..."

He looked at Ren with a profound expression, then turned to Blackbeard: "And not you either,

Blackbeard..."

"Eh?" Blackbeard looked at the dying Whitebeard in confusion.

"It is not you who will inherit Roger's will..."

Sengoku, who heard Whitebeard's words related to Roger, felt a bad feeling in his heart...

"I don't know who he is or where he is... but he exists... the one who will inherit the will of Roger and Ace. However..."

He smiled at that moment with reassurance and said, "But it is not you two, at least."

"Sengoku! You and the World Government fear the impending total war! The war that will erupt everywhere, where people will fight for their freedom!"

"You bastard...!!" Sengoku's brow furrowed, and his expression twisted due to Whitebeard's

words.

"But that day... will inevitably come!"

"The man who will inherit Roger's will will lead that war, Sengoku... It is a destined day!"

Whitebeard looked up at the sky and then at his family, who were crying for him in the background.

"My children... get out of here safely; this is my last command as your captain..." Then, after taking a deep breath, he added: "I love you, my family."

In a moment of absolute silence, Whitebeard raised his head to the sky and shouted with all

the remaining strength he had.

"One piece...One piece is real!!"

Chapter 124: [123] Devil's Fruits Hunt
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2700 Words]

Silence.

This was the title of what was happening in Marineford Square right now. No, not just in Marineford but all around the world, where Den Den Mushi was broadcasting the events of the war.

In the four seas, there were few Den Den Mushi linked to the ones broadcasting the war, as this luxury was possessed only by a select few, such as rulers of kingdoms and prominent figures in the underworld, or retired Navy soldiers. They all stared at the live broadcast with their mouths agape.

In the Grand Line, many residents from various islands had gathered to watch the war since its beginning; they were all rendered speechless upon hearing Whitebeard's last words.

As for the New World, this sea, dominated by the Yonko, heralded an approaching era of chaos that would reap both the living and the dead. After Whitebeard's death, this fate became inevitable.

Back at Marineford.

He's dead. Ace-the world, with all its noise and chaos, suddenly quieted. The sea ceased its whispering, and the cannons stopped firing. The only sound that could be heard was the wind carrying with it the scent of blood and sorrow. Everything came to a standstill.

Whitebeard... is dead too.

An oppressive silence engulfed Marineford. The sounds of battle gradually hushed, as if even war respected death. The Navy flags, which had been flying fiercely moments ago, now drooped as if in mourning.

On the battlefield, the members of Whitebeard's crew stood in silence. Their eyes were filled with disbelief; no words were spoken. How could words express the loss of two of their most important members? How could they continue sailing without Pops? What was their fate? These questions weighed heavily on them.

It was unknown who started crying, but soon others from Whitebeard's pirates followed suit; Marco, along with the rest of the surviving commanders, cried hysterically.

"Zehahaha! Those were great dying words... and you're still standing, Pops! Zehahaha!" Teach laughed wickedly and rose from the ground, glaring at Ren with malice. Ren had stepped back from Whitebeard's corpse with Mikita and Nami, creating distance between himself and the Blackbeard crew.

When Teach saw this, he let out a sigh of relief and then looked greedily at Whitebeard's corpse.

"Whitebeard... even in death, you still cause trouble...!" Sengoku gritted his teeth with worry and anger. He wanted to tear apart Whitebeard's corpse at that moment; he knew that Withebeard's last words would cause many problems for them in the future.

"I'm too old for this nonsense... let the new generation deal with it... sigh..." Sengoku sighed. After a moment of weakness, he glared at Blackbeard and added, "But I still have to deal with you first!"

Back to Blackbeard, he laughed loudly and then shouted, drawing the attention of everyone on the battlefield, including Akainu and Kizaru, who had fought alongside Ren earlier, and Aokiji, who was still on the execution platform with Sengoku.

"Let's start the show that will shock the world! Zehahaha!" Teach laughed and gestured to his crew, quickly pulling out Shiryu, who covered Whitebeard's corpse with a black cloth.

"W-What does he intend to do?" One of the Marines was confused by Teach's actions.

"This bastard is desecrating Pops' body!" Marco screamed with a weary expression on his tear-streaked face.

Ren looked at Teach as if he were looking at a clown. He held Nami and Mikita around the waist and pulled them close to him; the girls said nothing and enjoyed his embrace in an intimate moment, creating a strange contrast in the bloody battlefield.

Under the watchful eyes of Sengoku, Garp, Tsuru, and various Navy figures, along with different warlords and hundreds of millions of inhabitants of the sea world, Teach entered the black cloth and concealed his body.

Seconds passed slowly, turning into tens of seconds, and soon a minute or more had passed.

Nothing happened; people were still waiting to see what would occur, but Teach remained silent, and nothing changed on the battlefield.

"E-eh... it should be here... w-what's happening..." Teach's confused voice was heard, even though he hadn't emerged from the black covering yet.

"Captain Teach, is everything alright?" Shiryu looked at him with a strange expression. Teach had told him earlier that he would show him a miracle and shock the world, but why did he feel that something was wrong?

"T-this is impossible! What's happening here?!!" Teach screamed hysterically, realizing at that moment that he had embarrassed himself in front of hundreds of millions of people. "What is he doing? Is he a clown?" one of the Marines said disdainfully.

"Arara... I was expecting something serious." Aokiji said with his usual cold expression.

"Hmph, I'll burn this clown right away!" Akainu sneered coldly.

"Very scary~ everything today has been very scary...~"

"Tch!" Akainu ignored Kizaru's bored tone as he pretended to be afraid.

In the meantime, it wasn't just the Marines mocking Teach and finding his actions strange; hundreds of millions of people were baffled by the actions of this pirate.

"NOOOOOO!! Where is the Devil Fruit?!" Teach screamed with all his might as he emerged from the black covering. His eyes were red and bloodshot, his body trembling, and he felt waves of anger, shame, and embarrassment crashing over him as he noticed the strange looks from the pirates and Marines, some of whom looked at him with... disdain?

"What the hell is going on here..." Shiryu puffed out a cloud of smoke from his cigarette as he observed Teach's state, beginning to question the wisdom of following such a captain.

The rest of the newly formed crew shared Shiryu's thoughts, but it was still too early to judge their captain.

At this moment, Teach didn't notice that Ren was smiling coldly while stifling his laughter. Nami looked at him and whispered, "Captain, you did something, didn't you?" Nami knew him well; when her boyfriend had that expression, it meant he had done something bad to someone and made them suffer.

Mikita looked at Ren in confusion. She too had laughed at Blackbeard's antics, but Nami and Ren's conversation drew her attention.

"Rhaha...you'll find out soon enough... this man really embarrassed himself." Ren smiled, then released the girls' waists and looked at Teach, who was still confused, and then ordered

Nami and Mikita.

"We've done enough in this war... Our next goal is to capture as many Devil Fruit users as possible. They must be alive so I can kill them myself."

"Understood, Captain!"

"Aye Aye!"

The two girls smiled and then disappeared from their spot.

*Whoosh!*

*Bang!*

*Boom!*

*Bang!*

This sudden movement from Ren's crew caught the attention of everyone on the battlefield. The moment Nami cut down a group of pirates, it seemed as if the battlefield had come alive again, and people began killing each other like crazy once more.

The Navy's artillery resumed bombarding the pirates, turning them into pieces once again, while Navy soldiers attacked with unmatched ferocity.

They were in high spirits now; Whitebeard was dead, and Ace had been executed, along with

many of Whitebeard's commanders.

This was undoubtedly a major victory for the Marines!

On the other side... the pirates were completely the opposite; they fled the battlefield like headless chickens. Without a captain to give them orders and unify them, they became easy prey despite the fierce resistance.

Marco looked weakly at his comrades being ruthlessly slaughtered by the Marines. He gritted his teeth, suppressed his sorrow and tears, and shouted, "Follow me, comrades! Pops wished for us to leave here alive, so I'll make sure as many of us as possible do just that!"

"Captain Marco!"

"Long live Captain Marco!"

"Prepare the ships, and let's escape!"

The desperate pirates shouted joyfully; Marco's words brought them hope-the hope they thought they had lost forever. They didn't hesitate for a moment to allow Marco to lead them.

....

Meanwhile, back to Teach and his crew.

"Hellhound!"

Akainu attacked Teach without wasting any time, launching a punch infused with molten magma aimed at Teach with the intent of incinerating him.

"Don't think you'll escape easily after what you did in Impel Down!" Akainu shouted as he

struck with his fist, but he narrowed his eyes when he saw the magma vanish into a black vortex conjured by Teach.

In reality, Teach was extremely confused; he was lost, saddened, and angry. The plan had been going smoothly-what had changed?

Wasn't he supposed to gain Whitebeard's power and amaze the world? Make the world fear his name and tremble at its mention?

"I can't accept this!!" Teach shouted, countering Akainu's scorching magma with his dark powers while suppressing the sadness and unwillingness in his heart.

"You scoundrels, attack with me and let's get out of here!" he yelled at his crew, who quickly

heeded his words and clashed with Akainu. Even though the latter faced Teach and many dangerous escaped prisoners from Level Six, he did not back down.

"I can't let you do as you please... you fearsome pirates..." At that moment, Kizaru's dull voice

echoed in the ears of Teach and his crew. He attacked and supported Akainu, leading to another fierce battle, but it was clear that Blackbeard's pirates were not in a good position.

...

"Fall back, comrades! Focus on escaping!" A middle-aged man with a slender but tall body, standing at three meters tall. He had short black hair styled like a ponytail.

His name was Jason, one of the allied commanders under Whitebeard's fleet. Jason currently

bore an expression of worry and sadness while fighting for his life to ensure as many of his

men escaped as possible.

"Kill the pirates! Don't let anyone escape!"

A rear admiral shouted and advanced toward Jason, brandishing his sword at his neck to sever

it from his body, but a strange scene shocked the rear admiral. His sword stopped just a few centimeters from Jason's neck! It looked as if his sword was stuck in the air!

"I ate a Paramecia-type Devil Fruit, the Air Pockets Fruit (Kuu Kuu No Mi), which allows me

to create air pockets that render this kind of regular attack ineffective against me!" Jason

shouted angrily before launching a counterattack.

"Air Explosion!"

At the same time as his shout, a pressure wave erupted from an air pocket created in front of

the stunned rear admiral's head!

*Boom!* The air exploded with enough force to blast the rear admiral's head into a pulp of

flesh and blood!

'An interesting ability...' Ren, who was hunting for Devil Fruit users like a mad hound, felt

greed fill his heart as he watched Jason kill the rear admiral with ease. He then made his move.

The battlefield was filled with screams, pain, and curses, along with clouds of dust from the heavy cannon fire. Such an environment was ideal for launching stealth and assassination attacks.

Returning to Jason, he saw that one of his men was about to fall into the hands of a Marine, and he was about to rush to help him, but...

His Observation Haki sent a warning! Jason's heart was racing! He wanted to respond to the approaching danger, but the problem was that he didn't know its source!

*Swish!*

*Splash!*

A thin line passed through Jason's neck; even the air pockets he hastily created to protect

himself could not withstand the overwhelming power of the sword that shattered these pockets and made the air howl!

With a low thud, Jason's head fell away from his body, and Ren appeared before Jason's crew,

who stared blankly at their captain's corpse...

'Ms. System, extract this Devil Fruit.'

At Ren's request, the fruit was extracted and transported to The Infinity Space. He looked

around and saw the other members of Jason's crew.

'Let's kill them and see if any of them have a Devil Fruit...' With this thought, Ren decided the

fate of these dozens of pirates and began to behead them one by one with a cold expression.

....

At the same time, a beautiful girl with orange hair was staring coldly at a man kneeling on his knees. He had numerous wounds on his body and looked exhausted and on the verge of death, but Nami didn't care. She took out a Den Den Mushi and contacted Ren, who quickly appeared

before her.

"Oh, a user of the Paper Fruit (Kami Kami no Mi), huh..." Ren spoke to the muscular man who

was struggling with his serious wounds. When he heard Ren's voice, he looked at him with pure anger and hatred, shouting, "You bastard! You Marine dog! You're going to die a miserable death! You'll die alone-"

"Cut it."

Ren spoke in a cold voice, then waved his sword and severed the pirate's head, extracting his

Devil Fruit. 'Paper... this ability reminds me of a certain female character with blue hair from a specific

"Well done, Mikita." Ren patted her head and ruffled her blonde hair, making the girl nod like

a compliant cat in her master's arms.

"Do you really want to kill a beautiful girl like me..?" At that moment, the voice of the pirate

his skill "Cold Heart" and stared at the pirate with no expression on his face; there were no emotions-neither anger nor joy-nothing at all, just a routine, mechanical sound. Nami and Mikita knew that this was one of their captain's skills, but they still felt uneasy

concern, unable to react when the girls hugged him.

He was at a loss for words, but all he could do was gently pat their heads and stroke their hair

with affection. "Okay okay, stay alert. We're still in the battlefield after all." They stepped out of his embrace and nodded, "Aye aye, Captain!" Not far away, Hancock watched this scene unfold from beginning to end. She had heard Mikita

talking about the abilities of that dead pirate and wanted to see what would happen next. She smiled arrogantly when she saw the pirate attack Ren with her powers, eagerly anticipating his submission and the sparing of the pirates' lives. After all, all men were like this-weak in the face of beauty and turning into disgusting fools when it came to women, or so she

thought.

But to her surprise, she saw Ren's cold expression, which sent chills down her spine even from

a distance. Then she watched as he ruthlessly severed the woman's head.

"This man is strange..." Hancock murmured softly, barely loud enough for herself to hear.

Chapter 125: [124] Sudden Entrance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2200 Words]

In The Marineford, the war entered its final phase, aptly titled: a massacre of pirates.

Indeed, the current scene in Marineford was nothing short of a one-sided slaughter. The pirates had lost the will to fight; they had been defeated horrifically, failing to achieve any of their goals in this doomed battle. With the death of Whitebeard, their morale was at an all- time low, and all they wanted was to survive.

What made matters worse was the appearance of robotic soldiers resembling the Warlord Kuma. These automatons were responsible for slicing and blasting hundreds of pirates with their scorching laser beams.

Due to Ren's intervention in the war, these weapons had not been deployed from the beginning. The Marines had not suffered losses at the hands of Whitebeard's commanders, as they were killed by the Sinful Crew, while Whitebeard himself was suppressed by the admirals with Ren's support.

Furthermore, Sengoku had hesitated to deploy them because they had only recently

completed their testing and trials, unlike in the original Work where the Summit War began six months after Luffy's adventures started. Here, it commenced two months in, creating a significant time difference.

Under Sengoku's orders, the Marines, having lost many of their loved ones, transformed into relentless hunting dogs, pursuing the retreating pirates without mercy.

"They killed my brother! I will avenge you, brother, and eradicate all pirates from this world!" A soldier shouted wielding a rifle, consumed by rage and madness. He pulled the trigger and blew the head off a surrendering pirate on the ground.

*Bang!*

"Don't take any prisoners! The good pirate is The dead pirate!" An old veteran from the Marine side shouted. He had lost his promising apprentice during Whitebeard's rampage, driving him to madness. His sword sliced through pirates of any age or gender, leaving them in a pool of blood and flesh.

The denizens of the sea observed all these scenes in silence through live broadcasts. This war fundamentally changed their understanding of their world and its powers-be it the man who could unleash natural disasters like earthquakes, the admirals with their overpowering abilities, or Ren with his sword capable of cutting giants and delivering a fatal blow to Whitebeard.

Back in Marineford, where the massacre continued unabated, Ren watched the chaos with a slight smile on his face. He was in a good mood at that moment, and the reason? He had killed many Devil Fruit users and made a significant harvest.

Ren's harvest wasn't just about Devil Fruits; don't forget he possessed a system feature called "Pirate Hunter," which allowed him to gain half the bounty of any pirate he killed.

He had already accumulated billions of berries. Money was incredibly important; it didn't matter when or where-money was power, and that was a proven fact.

He also received several bounties from the system.

But the details would come later.

"Captain Ren, what's the next step?" Nami's soft voice pulled him from his thoughts. He looked at his Orange-haired girl and pondered deeply.

'The Blackbeard Pirates are slowly retreating under Akainu and Kizaru's assault, while the Marines slaughter the fleeing pirates with no intention of letting them go... and that man hasn't shown up yet...'

As he thought of Shanks, his mind drifted to Koby, whom he had previously hypnotized and turned into his Slave.

'What I did will change Koby's fate... Koby, who was supposed to shout for an end to the madness unfolding, won't stand against Akainu and risk his life, which would awaken his hidden talent like the Original Work... what a shame.' Ren sneered coldly, but soon pushed the thought of Koby to the back of his mind.

Koby wasn't important to him. Yes, he had talent, but that was it. If he became strong enough that Ren could no longer control him with his hypnotic skills, wouldn't that mean he had raised an enemy who would stab him in the back later?

All these thoughts crossed his mind in mere seconds before he replied to Nami, "We will just watch for now."

"Okay~" She nodded with a smile, then took Mikita, who was off to the side, and walked away to talk about something that made the blonde girl blush.

"..." Ren remained silent, but he didn't follow the girls.

When girls talk to each other, men should not interfere.

Something like that.

Putting all jokes aside, Ren then looked at Hancock. He had previously noticed that the Pirate Empress had been watching him for a brief moment, so he felt curious.

Ren couldn't deny that he was attracted to Hancock. What man wouldn't be? Only a castrated man or someone with a rubbery mind like Luffy would not be. Although Ren thought of Luffy as a mix of the two.

'But still, his Devil Fruit is dangerous... Nika, the Sun God and Liberation...' Greed filled his heart, and some dark thoughts and plans began to form in his mind.

Ren suppressed all that and used Shave to disappear, then reappeared in front of Hancock, who was sitting atop the stone statues.

The woman was startled to see Ren appear in front of her suddenly, staring at her. She looked at him coldly and arrogantly, then said in a resonant voice that could soften the hearts of even the toughest men, "Hmph, why are you looking at this princess?"

'She's acting like a young lady from a xianxia novel...' Ren was at a loss for words, but he still maintained a neutral expression and replied, "That question should be directed at me,

peeper."

"W-What?!" Hancock exclaimed in anger and embarrassment, thinking to herself, 'Have I been discovered?!'

For a moment, Hancock didn't know how to respond. She suppressed her embarrassment and said angrily, "Don't overestimate yourself! I was just looking randomly."

Seeing her proud, arrogant expression, Ren couldn't help but let out a laugh, which didn't go unnoticed by Hancock, who fell into another childish fit of anger.

"Hmph! You annoying and disgusting men deserve to submit to this princess--" Hancock's words were cut short when she sensed an imminent danger approaching her. Narrowing her beautiful eyes, she quickly vanished from her spot, reappearing five meters away in front of a large man wielding a massive sword.

"Die, scum." Her cold voice made the attacker's heart nearly stop from fear.

She was about to petrify him and shatter him into a thousand pieces, but someone got to him before she could.

"He is my prey, 'Princess.'" The intruder, who was none other than Ren, grabbed the attacker by the neck and lifted him into the air with one hand.

"So, our little guest is none other than Blenheim, the captain of the ninth division of the Whitebeard Pirates. Why didn't you run away like the rest of your loser friends?"

Hancock paused and looked at Ren with a frown. The speed he displayed was something even she could only hope to achieve with effort, yet he did it effortlessly. This reminded her that the man in front of her fought against Whitebeard and even participated in his death. Although Whitebeard was old and sick, it didn't change the fact that Ren had defeated Marco, Vista, and the other powerful commanders.

Suddenly, Hancock felt a sense of danger. If he wanted to attack her, could she resist? And if that happened and she lost, what would happen to her sisters and Amazon Lily? 'But he's also a Warlord, so it's unlikely that would happen...' She realized how foolish and chaotic her thoughts were when she noticed that Ren wasn't even paying attention to her; he was focused solely on Blenheim, who was struggling to breathe.

'Tsk... he's ignoring me again! I'm the most beautiful woman in the world, and yet he ignores

me!'

Such feelings were strange for Hancock, who had always been the center of attention wherever she went. Even the admirals had to be wary of her beauty and the power of her fruit if her Haki matched theirs. But now she being disregarded by a man...

"Humph!"

Hancock scoffed coldly and irritably before leaving without saying a word, suppressing her

turmoil.

'There's no point in staying in this place full of disgusting men!' With this arrogant thought, she went off to find a spot to spend time alone without interruption until the end of this war

was announced.

Back to Ren, he noticed Hancock leaving but before that, he observed her troubled expression as she pondered something, realizing that his goal had succeeded: he had made the Empress think of him, even if it was negatively and filled with anger.

He understood her personality and what she had gone through, so he didn't blame her. He also knew who she really was and what her true personality was, the side she would only show

to someone she would fall for.

"Now, let's deal with you, Blenheim..."

Blenheim trembled as he felt the grip on his neck tighten. He tried to scream, but his throat was constricted. He began kicking like a slaughtered sheep, but the grip wouldn't release him; he could only gaze at the sky as tears streamed down his face.

*Crack~!* With a sound of cracking bones, his neck snapped, and Ren extracted the Devil Fruit from him,

which turned out to be a regular Zoan fruit-nothing impressive.

to intervene or what?' Ren thought with slig

the benefits he had gained today.

Because they were ridiculous in quantity.

The hypnotic ability was truly impressive.

"Alright, this should do it."

His voice was weak compared to the sounds of artillery fire and gunfire, but it began to seep into the ears of the warriors. The soldiers, immersed in killing and chaos, seemed to be waiting for a savior-the one who would pull them back to reality and stop them. "Koby... what is that kid doing?!" Garp, whose eyes were red from crying over Ace, looked at

Koby in confusion.

Ren stood at a distance, observing Shanks with a thoughtful expression. "It's time to go, girls." Ren stood up, ready to leave the battlefield, following the paths of Mihawk and Doflamingo, who had already departed. But before he could leave, he caught a glimpse of Hancock staring at him. Their eyes met, and for a moment, time seemed to freeze as they held each other's gaze.

After what felt like an eternity, their eyes finally parted, and they went their separate ways. Though their paths were divided for now, who could say whether fate would intertwine their destinies again? Only the future held the answer.

[The Next Day]

The war had ended, leaving tens of thousands dead on both sides. This battle, now known as the "Summit War," was a monumental clash between the titans of the world, each fighting for their place at the top.

The Marines emerged victorious-or so the story went, echoing from the lips of every citizen when the Summit War was mentioned. And indeed, they had achieved their objective, though at a devastating cost-the lives of tens of thousands of their comrades.

But these soldiers were hailed as heroes, martyrs even. Their names would be etched into history, remembered as the brave souls who stood against the mightiest pirate crew in the chaotic Pirate Era.

Yet, the story of the war wasn't just about Marine glory. New figures emerged from the shadows, capturing the world's attention. Among them, one stood out above all others--the Warlord, the pirate who had fought alongside the Admirals and helped bring down Whitebeard-the Black Death, Ren.

By now, the name Black Death Ren was known everywhere, his strength acknowledged across the seas. Even in the New World, his name became synonymous with power. His performance in the war was undeniable proof of his prowess.

Even on the most remote islands, cut off from communication, the people learned of the war's events through newspapers that fell from the skies the following day.

The headlines read:

[Summit War... The Death of Whitebeard and the Victory of Marine Heroes!]

Accompanied by images from the war, the headlines featured pictures of the Admirals and Ren in the heat of battle against Whitebeard.

The bias in the reporting was clear, as was typical of the World Government's control over the media. In this world, press freedom was an illusion.

Another headline, equally eye-catching, read:

[Warlord Black Death Ren and His Crew Hunt Down Whitebeard's Commanders.]

What followed that headline was a concise overview of the actions of Ren, Nami, and Mikita. While the details were not exhaustive, this was to be expected since Ren was merely a pirate, albeit one with a license.

his voice.

In the New World.

In the territory of the missing Whitebeard Pirates, specifically on the islands that once proudly displayed Whitebeard's flag.

At Marineford.

Chapter 127: [126] Kalifa's Choice
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[3000 Words]

In the royal palace of Alabasta, specifically in Ren's room, an interesting scene was unfolding. Leila sat on Ren's lap like a docile cat, while Vivi chatted happily with the blue-haired warlord.

Beside them stood a striking blonde woman with a calm expression, exuding the aura of a professional secretary.

"All the newspapers are talking about you, Captain-," Vivi, the blue-haired princess, looked at Ren with pride. She was amazed by the power Ren had displayed in the recent war. She knew he was strong, but his strength had far exceeded her imagination.

"That's right, brother. There's no doubt you've attracted the attention of some big players." This time, it was Leila who spoke. Coming from another world and knowing the story of One Piece, she naturally understood this world's rules and mysteries better than anyone besides Ren.

Kalifa listened to their conversation with a calm demeanor, but inside, her mind was racing, trying to pick up any critical information to report back to her superiors. It was her job as a spy, after all.

'But this is strange... I don't understand why I haven't reported anything yet...' The blonde secretary was confused. Whenever she attempted to send her routine report to Rob Lucci, she found herself in a peculiar situation. It was as if she had been asleep during that period.

Back to Ren, who was chatting with the girls, he glanced at Kalifa and noticed her abnormality. He thought to himself, 'Impressive mental resilience... even though she's weak and can't escape my control, she's sensed something odd.'

He smirked coldly and gently moved Leila off his lap. Vivi and Leila looked at him in confusion as they watched him approach Kalifa.

"Oh, Kalifa... I know what you're thinking."

Kalifa's heart skipped a beat as she saw Ren standing Infront of her, and hearing his words only made it worse.

The atmosphere in the room grew cold. Leila stared icily at the blonde secretary, naturally knowing who Kalifa was as someone who had watched the original work and understood that Kalifa had no true loyalty to Ren.

Vivi, sensing the shift in the room's mood, looked at Kalifa with concern.

Kalifa tried to remain calm and responded with a composed expression, "What do you mean, Captain-"

"Shhh..." Ren placed a finger on her lips, signaling her to be quiet, making Kalifa's body tremble with tension.

"I know you have no loyalty." He removed his finger and looked at her with a calm expression. "T-That's not true..." Kalifa's heart raced as she thought to herself, 'He Caught me! It's over!' She didn't entertain the thought of escape, not for a moment. Let alone the monster standing in front of her, whose power she had witnessed firsthand, she wouldn't even stand a chance against Mikita, Nami, and the rest of the crew.

"Is it really worth it?" Ren sighed and fixed his gaze on Kalifa once more.

"What do you mean, Captain..." Kalifa asked, with terror flooding her heart.

"I mean, is it worth dying for the World Government? I mean Look, they didn't hesitate for a moment to throw you into the lion's den, and you, with all that brainwashing, didn't hesitate to take on this espionage mission. What were you thinking? That I wouldn't notice your intentions? It's Ironic."

With every word that left his mouth, the expression on Kalifa's face grew more conflicted. As someone raised and trained by the World Government to be their weapon from a young age, she had never considered such questions. Agents like her weren't supposed to think, only obey orders-even if it meant their death.

Kalifa sighed in defeat, realizing there was no escape now. She stopped pretending and said coldly to Ren, "Just kill me."

Ren smiled upon hearing her words, while both Vivi and Leila's expressions shifted. "Captain Ren..." Vivi spoke, looking at Kalifa with a complex expression. Although Vivi knew Kalifa wasn't loyal, her kind nature had forged a friendship with her. But she wasn't sure if Kalifa had ever considered her a friend.

"Come on, Kalifa, are you really going to throw your life away for the World Government? You're far too young to die like this." Leila, understanding what her brother was trying to do, used her knowledge of Kalifa's character to plant a seed of doubt in her heart.

"You want to die? Really? Alright, then." Ren spoke as he gazed deeply into Kalifa's eyes. Then, in the next moment, Versa transformed into a gun, and Ren held it.

Without hesitation, he pressed the barrel of the gun to her chest, right between the collarbone and the heart, so that Kalifa felt the cold metal pressing against her skin through her clothes. Her body shivered involuntarily.

Kalifa could feel her heart pounding, its beats hammering against the gun. Her body stiffened, her mind racing between fear and the desire to survive. 'Is this it? Is this where I die?' she wondered silently, beads of sweat rolling down her forehead.

Kalifa stood frozen in place, her eyes lost between confusion and fear. Over the years, her life had been heading in one direction-loyalty to the World Government. There was no room for thinking, only orders filled her life. Yet, with every word that left Ren's mouth and the other girls in the room, seeds of doubt began to sprout.

"Is it worth it?"

Kalifa began to think about what she had given to the World Government. Yes, it had provided her with power and status, but it was so low that it was barely worth mentioning... A CP9 agent? What is that? If a Celestial Dragon wanted to make her a slave, she wouldn't survive, not even CPO agents would, let alone her. So, she wondered deep inside: What was the government truly offering her? Or rather, did they care about her as a person?

"You've served them loyally, and yet they sent you to spy on me as if you were a tool, just a means to an end. Do they truly care what happens to you?" Ren's words struck her harder than

she expected.

Inside her mind, thoughts clashed. 'I am Kalifa, a loyal agent of the World Government... but do I really want to die for them?'

"You are strong, smart, and you know your fate isn't tied to them. You deserve a life better than this, not just some pointless end as a disposable tool." Ren continued, though he still didn't lower the gun from her chest.

Then he whispered coldly, "You can join me, live freely, and make your own decisions. I won't be like the government-I value true loyalty, not blind devotion."

Kalifa trembled again, but this time it was different. She felt something inside her starting to break free, a wall she had been building for years was beginning to crack.

'Is this the path I want to follow?' Kalifa asked herself. 'Is it really worth sacrificing everything for a government that didn't hesitate to send me to my death?'

"You are nothing but tools."

She remembered the words her trainer had told her when she was young. Those cold, emotionless words were strange and terrifying to her as a child, but as she grew up, she got used to them, feeling as if that was how things should be.

"Now, Kalifa, decide. Live or die?" Ren's words echoed in her mind like a devil's symphony. She knew that one word would determine her fate at this moment.

"I-I..." Kalifa was terrified. She knew the answer in her heart, but she couldn't say it. It felt

like an invisible force was holding her back.

"Loyalty, Kalifa!"

"Loyalty!"

"Loyalty to the World Government!"

"Loyalty!"

"The World Government is everything. You must give everything to the World Government!"

These phrases echoed in her mind. She had heard these lines for so many years, more than she had heard her own name. Slowly and steadily, her mind was washed with them, and her loyalty became blind and absolute to the World Government.

"I... I... W-want..." Kalifa muttered, and small tears began to form in her eyes, as if her head was about to explode.

Ren narrowed his eyes, sensing something strange happening to Kalifa. 'Is this the effect of brainwashing? Or has her subconscious been hypnotized, erasing any thought of betrayal?' His expression became serious. Without hesitation, he removed the hypnosis technique he had previously applied on her to prevent her from reporting anything to Rob Lucci. He then closed his eyes and focused on her mind.

'So, this is it... The World Government's methods truly run deep!' He thought to himself, realizing what was happening to her. She wasn't hypnotized by an external force, but rather conditioned from a young age for loyalty to the World Government and absolute obedience. Over the years, this brainwashing had taken on a hypnotic-like effect...

Kalifa looked at Ren with teary eyes, her body tense as the barrel of the gun pressed more firmly against her chest. She couldn't escape his piercing gaze. His words were cutting through the walls she had built as an agent of the World Government. "What do you want?" Ren repeated, his voice deeper and sharper this time. Then, noticing her troubled expression, he added: "Kalifa, follow what your heart truly desires. That suppressed desire, the one you've been denying, is your reality. It's your true thoughts... the thoughts of Kalifa, not the cold agent of CP9."

His words were like two swords; one severing her false loyalty to the government, and the other uncovering her hidden desire to be more than just a tool. She knew what he said was true —that her life as an agent had been nothing but a cage, and now, he was opening the door for her to choose her own fate.

Her tears fell quietly as she felt the deep internal struggle. For years, she had trained to be emotionless, without a personal identity. But now, in front of Ren, she was facing her true self for the first time.

In a trembling voice, she whispered: "I... I want to live."

As soon as Kalifa's shaky words, "I want to live," left her lips, a moment of silence filled the

room. Ren lowered the gun from her chest, then looked at her with a smile-one different

from his cold one before.

"You've passed the test," he said softly. "Welcome to the crew, Kalifa. Remember, this is just the beginning of your new journey."

He then turned quietly and gestured for Vivi and Leila to leave. "Come with me, Vivi and Leila;

let's give her some time to calm down." He said this as if giving Kalifa a moment to process what had just happened.

Vivi, who was watching the scene with a worried expression, hesitated for a moment before

following Ren and Leila outside. Before leaving the room, she gave Kalifa a deep look that combined sadness and relief. Vivi always tried to see the good in others, and even now, she hoped Kalifa was sincere in her desire to join them.

Leila, on the other hand, was more reserved. Her gaze was cold and neutral. As they exited the room, she whispered to Ren in a low voice, "Are you sure, brother? She had a deep loyalty to

the World Government, and she could be a threat to us."

Ren didn't reply immediately but nodded briefly, as if he understood Leila's concerns perfectly. "Don't worry; I see determination in her eyes."

As for Vivi, her feelings were mixed. She said calmly, "I believe she's sincere in her desire to

join us. We should give her a chance... maybe she will find a place among us." When they closed the door behind them, Kalifa was left alone in the room. She stood by

herself, her eyes still slightly tearful, but now she felt the weight lifted off her shoulders. However, she also knew that her journey was far from over, and her decision to live meant a new and completely different beginning from her past.

*purururu...purururu...purururu*

Suddenly, the Den Den Mushi rang on her pocket .There was no doubt who was calling. She

picked it up slowly, and as the features of the Den Den Mushi mimicked Rob Lucci's face, the calm and confident voice spoke:

"Kalifa," Lucci began in a cold tone. "What's new? I haven't heard a report from you in a

while."

Kalifa looked at the Den Den Mushi with a steady, calm expression, the feelings of weakness

and hesitation that had plagued her earlier completely vanished. It was time to prove that she was no longer a CP9 agent, but Kalifa.

"Everything is going well," she replied coldly, as she was accustomed to doing. "There's nothing to worry about."

Lucci was silent for a moment before continuing, "The routine report?"

Kalifa smiled coolly as always and said: "Everything is completely under control. There's

nothing to be concerned about; there have been no movements from Black Death since his return from the war."

"Well, just continue working, and don't do anything that would raise his suspicion." Lucci's voice was dry, but he suspected nothing.

She hung up and returned the Den Den Mushi to its place, a sense of power enveloping her.

She had never felt this way before; it was as if she were free, like a bird spreading its wings and soaring away into the sky without any worries.

Her decision to join Ren's crew meant she chose to live freely and to be part of a bigger

adventure. In that moment, she realized that she was no longer just a government agent, but a part of something more significant, with people she had chosen with her heart.

...

Far from the room, Ren smiled as he observed Kalifa's conversation through Observation

Haki.

'You didn't disappoint me, Kalifa...' he thought, feeling in a good mood. He then opened the

system interface and checked Kalifa's status.

¶[Name: Kalifa.]

¶[Age: 25.] [Occupation: Not specified yet.]

¶[Affiliation: Sin Pirates.]

¶[Status: Stable.]

¶[Title: None.]

[Bounty: None.] I[Physical Power Level: 42,500 (D)]

[[Loyalty: 70%.]

¶[Devil Fruit: None.]

¶[Devil Fruit Rating: None.]

¶[Skills: Shave (Low) - Moonwalk (Low) - Storm Kick (Low) - Iron Body (Low) - Finger Gun

(Low) - Kami-e (Low).]

[Haki: None.]

What intrigued Ren most was that her loyalty level had risen from 0% straight to 70%!

Some might think this percentage is low, but the truth is that it is very good, considering Kalifa's character. As a previously cold-blooded secret agent, she would never have placed her trust in anyone. Her loyalty reaching 70% was a sign that she indeed trusted Ren.

And Ren would make sure to make her even more loyal to him in the future...

....

Ren returned to Elsama using Moonwalk while carrying Vivi and Nami, then went back to his

room to find Kalifa about to leave.

"Follow me, Kalifa; I'll show you something." He looked at the blonde and smiled, guessing

her reaction to the Dimension in Elsama.

Kalifa looked at him with a rare slight smile on her face before her usual professional expression returned, and she nodded, "Please, Captain."

A minute later, Kalifa stood Infront of a medium-sized island, her mouth agape in shock. "C-Captain... Is this island... inside the ship?!" she exclaimed in disbelief. Just moments ago, she had been standing on the deck of a strange ship floating in the sky. Initially, she thought it

was the work of a specific Devil Fruit, but what she saw after entering that strange portal was what had left her shocked.

Ren laughed at her shock and led her to the large house where all the crew members had

gathered. Ren took a few minutes to explain to the crew about Kalifa's situation and how they should trust her. Initially, Kalifa was a bit tense since she didn't have anyone to share this "friendship" bond with. However, when she saw that the girls treated her kindly and did not

shy away from her, she relaxed.

As for the girls, their reactions were not particularly strong regarding what had happened in

the few hours Ren had been away, especially among smart members like Nami and Robin, who had known from the beginning that Kalifa would become one of them. "Now, integrate with each other while I take care of some business here." He gently nudged

Kalifa towards the group of girls and then sat down on a distant couch, looking at the medium-sized dining table in front of him.

He waved his hand, and in the next moment... many different Devil Fruits appeared!

It was time to check the harvest!

¶[-Ding! Congratulations to the Master, for completing the main mission "Kill Whitebeard," and earning: Armament Haki Scroll (Advanced) + Observation Haki Scroll (Advanced) + Conqueror's Haki Scroll (Intermediate) + 50,000 Physical Points.]
