Ren was so stunned by the reward that the girls at a distance noticed his reaction. He gave them a nod to signal that everything was fine, then refocused on the system interface, suppressing the overwhelming excitement swelling within him. Yet, he couldn't hide the wide, excited grin that spread across his face.

'This is incredible! I didn't expect that merely contributing to Whitebeard's death would count as completing the main mission...'

The main missions had always been visible on the system's interface and never changed. All of the rewards were labeled as "random." Most of these missions involved engaging in sexual encounters with female characters from the original story, but there were still tasks linked to killing major figures such as the Yonko, Marine Admirals, World Government agents, and the Five Elders and others...

Ren was so thrilled that he immediately accepted the rewards. The process of enhancing his Armament and Observation Haki began as soon as he asked Miss System to use the scrolls on him.

He felt as though an eternity had passed, though in reality, only a single second had elapsed in the outside world.

When he came to his senses, everything around him seemed different. He could perceive the world in a way that was far more detailed and precise, as if his eyes had been opened to the true nature of reality.

'So this is the advanced level of Observation Haki... sensing intentions and emotions... the final stage must be reading thoughts and The future Sight...' Ren muttered in awe. Soon, he noticed another change-this time in his body.

(Author's note: A slight modification. Future Sight is now at the Grandmaster level, not part of the advanced Observation Haki stage.)

He could feel that his body had grown significantly stronger. He hadn't yet used the 50,000 Physical Points he'd earned, but he knew this sudden surge in strength came from the advanced level of Armament Haki.

The system had enhanced his body to withstand the advanced Haki, and this brought even more benefits.

Ren was already reveling in the powerful sensation.

After a few moments, he composed himself and checked his current status.

"This technique opens up entirely new possibilities," Ren thought, gazing at his hands, focusing the Haki energy into his palm. "By allowing the energy to flow from any part of my body, I can unleash a powerful force without needing physical contact. It's like having an invisible weapon."

He looked out the window at the trees in the distance, carefully watching them. "Imagine if I could direct my energy toward distant targets. By emitting invisible force, I could damage enemies or destroy objects without touching them. This opens up whole new strategies in combat and would allow me to face even the mightiest sea creatures!"

Ren imagined how this technique would serve him in future battles. "I could use Emittance to repel enemy attacks. For instance, if someone tries to strike me, I could let the energy flow from my hand to create an invisible shield, deflecting the attack. I wouldn't even need to counterattack-this energy alone would protect me."

He closed his eyes, attuning to the energy coursing through his body. "I could also use it to trigger small explosions. At close range, I could project Haki energy onto an object, like a stone, turning it into a miniature projectile that detonates on impact."

Ren smirked coldly as he envisioned using this technique to eliminate his enemies without them even knowing what hit them.

"Even in the toughest battles, I could keep my distance while still dealing serious damage. A simple release of energy would hit them with the same force as a physical punch, but without any direct contact."

Opening his eyes again, Ren resolved to begin training this technique immediately. "This ability will give me the advantage I need over the Yonko and other major figures...but against people like Doflamingo? I'll be able to crush them!"

"Now let's see Conqueror's Haki..." Ren was incredibly excited, yet he maintained a calm demeanor on the outside. An unknown power flowed into his mind and body, and within moments, Ren felt that the range of his Conqueror's Haki had increased significantly.

The most important aspect was that he could now control the range and determine who this Conqueror's Haki would affect!

"There's still one reward left. Ah... I'm already overwhelmed by all this power..." Ren laughed with a satisfied expression before consuming his remaining reward, which consisted of physical strength points. This enhancement greatly boosted his physical strength, to the point where Ren felt he needed to fight to acclimate to his new power. However, he forced himself to suppress this urge, as he still had things to accomplish.

He had already reached level 'S' in physical strength! Ren felt an enormous power flowing through his veins, vast as the ocean and strong as mountains. Filled with excitement and strength, he wanted to crush an island or two.

After a few minutes, he managed to relax and adapt to the changes he had undergone. Then he

¶[-Ding! Congratulations to Master, for completing the main mission "Kill Marco" and earning: 4 Low-Level Armament Haki Scrolls + 4 Low-Level Observation Haki Scrolls.]

When Ren read this notification, he raised his eyebrows and thought, 'So, did Marco really die? It seems the poison got him.'

As for the rewards, he intended to give them to the girls.

Ren recalled that he had three Low-Level Armament Haki Scrolls from a hidden mission when

Leila joined the crew, meaning he now possessed 7 Low-Level Armament Haki Scrolls and 4 Low-Level Observation Haki Scrolls.

'The members who have not awakened their Haki are Leila, Robin, Zala, Kalifa, and Kaya who only has Observation Haki...' Ren pondered. He had a surplus of Haki scrolls, and even after awakening both types of Haki for his crew, he would still have 3 Low-Level Armament Haki

Scrolls remaining.

In the next moment, they saw several scrolls flying towards them, landing in the hands of

Fortunately, Nami explained the scrolls to her, and with each word, Kalifa's expression changed until she was completely shocked.

"Y-you mean... if I consume this scroll somehow, I'll gain... Haki?!"

Kalifa knew what Haki was, of course, but she had been far from learning it. First, because mastering it required high physical strength, and second, because she hadn't done anything for the World Government that warranted her learning it.

But now... she would simply learn it?

Kalifa's gaze was confused, and she quickly glanced at Ren with her beautiful eyes. Then she

noticed the many Devil Fruits laid out on the wooden table. Her gaze froze, and she asked Nami with a trembling voice, "Is it possible... that the captain has a way to find Devil Fruits?"

It must be said that Kalifa used all her imagination to come to this conclusion upon seeing the

many Devil Fruits on the table. She would never have suspected, even in her wildest dreams, that Ren could extract Devil Fruits from those he killed.

Not to mention his ability to teach Haki to anyone he wanted just through mysterious scrolls; he had even more mysterious and terrifying methods, such as extracting Devil Fruits.

attack and defend simultaneously.]

Ren possessed a photographic memory but still read the system's description of the skill.

After that, he activated his photographic memory and absorbed all the information related to this technique.

Iron Body is a technique that relies on muscle strength; the stronger the person, the more

effective the technique. Yet, despite the hardening, the user's body is still made of flesh and blood, meaning that extremely powerful attacks can break through this defense.

The Iron Body Fist Technique used by some CPO members is unique because it overcomes the fundamental flaw of the Iron Body technique: the inability to move while activated.

'Miss System, when will this skill develop into the Iron Body Fist Technique (Tekkai Kenpo)

The system responded without delay. Ren nodded in satisfaction, then consumed the scroll

and felt the power of the new skill permeating his body. He felt he could now tense his muscles to make his body harder than steel!

He still had 4 scrolls left to distribute to the crew members who needed them in battles:

Nami, Nojiko, Mikita, and Robin.

Chapter 129: [128] Revealing Rewards[2]

[2100 Words]

With the new addition to the "Devil Fruit Extraction" feature, multiple messages containing information about each fruit laid out on the wooden table appeared.

-¶ Name: Tremor-Tremor Fruit - (Gura Gura no Mi).

- Type: Paramecia.

-¶ Description: A pale white fruit with dark blue spots, pulsing as if a coming earthquake.

This fruit grants its user the ability to create devastating quakes, capable of literally destroying the world.

-¶ Abilities: Massive structural destruction, generating seismic waves that can shatter the earth, ships, and even split the sea. There's a chance to destroy islands and tectonic plates after awakening.

-¶ Rating: ★★★★★ (Top-tier fruit).

'A top-tier fruit... tectonic plate destruction!' Ren inhaled sharply upon reading the system's description. This fruit truly lived up to its reputation as a world-destroying power.

But the question now was, who should he give this fruit to? Should he eat it himself? What if he did, and a more powerful or valuable fruit appeared later? Could he remove it thanks to the new feature?

Ren hesitated for a moment, glancing at his crew. Nami was bickering with Nojiko, who was intentionally teasing her, while Robin watched them with a resigned expression. Lila and Vivi were chatting with Kalifa, trying to strengthen their bonds, while Zala and Mikita were greeting Bell-mere, who had just entered the house. Lastly, his eyes landed on Kaya, who was focused on reading a medical book, serious despite the noise around her.

'Nami is doing well with the Thunder Fruit, and Nojiko is developing new techniques with the Bomb Fruit... As for Robin, I can change her Devil Fruit later. Mikita needs a stronger fruit, but the Tremor Fruit doesn't suit her style. The rest... well, there are better-suited fruits for them.'

Ren's mind worked quickly, cycling through numerous thoughts in just a fraction of a second. Not only had his body become stronger, but his mind had also undergone significant changes thanks to the evolution of his Haki. He felt like he could fight for a week without needing sleep.

Returning to the matter at hand, Ren held the pale fruit, feeling the immense power contained within it.

He took a deep breath, clearing his mind and...

*Bite!*

He ate the fruit without hesitation. There was no awful taste; it tasted like the finest peaches.

The crew stopped chatting upon hearing the sound of chewing, turning their eyes to Ren with surprise.

"The captain is eating a Devil Fruit!" Nami exclaimed excitedly, followed by a series of reactions.

"Let's see what fruit he ate!" Nojiko, just like her sister, was excited.

Even Kaya, who had been focused on her book, paused and looked at Ren with curiosity.

She wasn't the only curious one-everyone in the house, including Bell-mere, was eager to know which fruit Ren had eaten.

Within seconds, the space Ren had occupied alone was now filled with beautiful women.

Ren opened his eyes after finishing absorbing the new power flowing through his veins. He relished the euphoric feeling of growing stronger every time.

"The captain's eyes... they look different..." Robin whispered quietly, but her voice was audible to everyone. They all stared at his eyes, which seemed to glow with a white light, full of power and majesty.

"Do you want to know which fruit I ate?" Ren asked the women gathered around him.

"Yes!" they all replied. Even the beautiful Bell-mere, different from her previous self, nodded her head, clearly curious.

"Unfortunately, I can't test its power here because that would destroy this house... and maybe this entire island..." Ren sighed in frustration.

The women were shocked by his words, and Kalifa asked in a professional tone, "Is the fruit that powerful?"

Ren looked at her gently, a stark contrast to the cold gaze he had given her earlier when he threatened her with a gun to her chest. He had noticed Kalifa's loyalty growing since she entered the dimension, so his attitude toward her had softened.

Chapter 131: [130] A Beautiful Sunset
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2000 Words]

[After half an hour.]

Ren was in a remote desert area in Alabasta, with nothing in sight but the barren, wild desert. The golden grains of sand shimmered under the scorching sun beating down on his face.

A few minutes ago, Ren had finished his brief meeting with his crew members after defining each of their roles.

Starting with the first member, Nojiko, she was the sniper, and the reason was clear: her weapon was a modern sniper rifle equipped with Sea Stone bullets, making it a deadly weapon.

Next was Nami, the navigator, whose role had been established a few months ago, so there was no need to discuss the reason.

Then came Kaya, the ship's doctor, whose medical skills had rapidly improved thanks to the Devil Fruit she had eaten.

Following her was Mikita, the blonde girl who puzzled Ren; he wasn't sure which position to assign her. After deep consideration, he decided to make her the crew's fighter.

This meant her primary duty would be to fight. In any battle or war the Pirates of Sin entered, Mikita would be at the forefront.

Next was Vivi, the blue-haired princess, whom Ren decided would remain in Alabasta and not sail with them; she was the crew's second fighter.

Why did Ren assign her this role? Simply because her strength was rising at a rapid pace. She had consumed the Sand Fruit extracted from Crocodile that Ren had killed with poison, in addition to mastering Shave, Moonwalk, and Iron Body.

She hadn't mastered Haki yet, but her strength had shown significant development.

After that was Robin, whom Ren appointed as the archaeologist, making her extremely happy. Before the last member was Zala, whom Ren decided to make the "Strategic Defender," promising her he would search for a better Devil Fruit to replace her current weak one.

Finally, there was Kalifa. Without hesitation, Ren made her his secretary, which made the blonde woman happy, although she didn't show it on her face. But how could her feelings escape his observation's Haki?

However, there was still one more member, Tashigi. Unfortunately, this girl was not with them at the moment, so he couldn't assign her a position she hadn't determined for herself. After finalizing the assignment of the crew members' roles, Ren checked his inventory and was astonished to discover that he possessed over 5 billion berries!

Where did all this money come from? First of all, Ren received 1 billion berries from Garp, and he had approximately 1 billion before that. However, during the last war, Ren lost hundreds of millions of berries purchasing medium-level potions. He managed to recoup these losses by defeating numerous pirates in the war and claiming half of the bounty on their heads directly. As for the high-profile targets, he earned a substantial sum from them, detailed as follows: 1. Half of Whitebeard's bounty: 2.5 billion berries.

2. Half of Marco's bounty: 687 million berries.

3. Half of Jozu's bounty: 295 million berries.

4. Half of Vista's bounty: 250 million berries.

5. Half of Koriel's bounty: 150 million berries.

Calculating these amounts yields:

687 +2,500 + 295 +250 + 150 = 3.882 billion berries.

When combined with the money he still has and the bounties from the hundreds of pirates he defeated, Ren amassed a fortune estimated at 5 billion berries!

Returning to the present, Ren looked at the vast desert and took a deep breath, sensing energy coursing through his body, conjuring images of Whitebeard and how his power flowed like an explosive river. With each passing moment, his charge grew stronger, as if the very earth was responding to his calls.

•

·

"Broken World!"

Ren shouted, directing his hand toward the ground. "Crack!"

At that moment, the ground beneath his feet shook violently, as if struck by lightning. Violent tremors began to sweep through the desert, and deep cracks appeared in the sand, as if rage had erupted from the depths of the earth. Small rocks shattered, and clouds of dust rose into the sky, mingling with the sand, creating a surreal scene.

With each step, the earth shook even more. The sight became a nightmare for the surrounding environment. The rare trees growing at the edges of the desert shook violently, as if dancing in a storm. Small stones left a clear trail in the sand, as if they were walking toward the abyss. In the distant villages of Alabasta, the residents felt the powerful tremors. People trembled in their homes, and distant sounds came like the noise of a storm. "What is this?!" one shouted, their eyes widening in terror. Panic ensued as families rushed outside their homes, searching for safety.

"An earthquake! It's an earthquake!" one man shouted, trying to calm the frightened children. The adults' voices rose, warning them not to go far, but chaos had already spread.

Even residents of distant cities like the capital and the Rainbase felt light tremors, looking toward the distant desert in confusion and fear of the unknown.

As Ren unleashed energy intensely, he slowly felt how his effects extended beyond just a personal experience. "I can affect everyone around me!" he said to himself, noting how the earthquake caused immediate destruction on the horizon.

With every tremor, Ren sensed the tension of the energies around him. He unleashed another strike, directing his power toward a specific point in the desert. "Crack!" he exclaimed, and at that moment, a deafening explosion occurred. Waves of energy shot out, and large sand masses soared into the air, forming clouds of dust resembling small tornadoes.

The cracks in the earth multiplied, and masses of sand exploded under the effect of his power. In a scene of chaos, Ren felt his strength rising, as if the entire world was yielding to his will.

As the desert trembled from violent quakes, Ren began to sense something deeper within him. He realized that this power was not merely a means of destruction but also a tool for defense. However, he needed to master it. "I can't let this power slip away," he whispered, focusing his thoughts on his abilities.

He continued to experiment with the earthquake power, summoning the strength needed to create unique effects. "Sand Wall!" he shouted once more, gathering sand to form a sturdy barrier in front of him. This wall, shaped by his ability to influence the surrounding environment with his tremors, was reminiscent of how Whitebeard used the sea to create tsunamis powerful enough to flood Marineford.

As he finished his experiment, Ren turned to survey the devastation he had caused. "An incredible power, capable of destroying the world. I look forward to awakening this Devil Fruit," he said with a cold smile, suppressing his excitement.

Then he noticed a figure flying toward him and recognized her. "Hello, Captain! Was that your doing? It's amazing!"

He heard Mikita's voice, floating in the air, but now she wasn't using a parachute to assist her flight; instead, she had a pair of white, paper-like wings.

"Mikita, I see you're doing well with your new Devil Fruit powers." When she heard his praise, she smiled brightly. "Kyahaha~ I can barely keep these wings stable, let alone use

them in battle."

She descended to the devastated ground and gazed at the man responsible for all this destruction with deep admiration. Ren noticed her gaze and approached her. "Let's watch the sunset together," Ren suggested, pointing to the sun beginning to set.

"Sure~."

In the heart of the Alabasta desert, where the setting sun cast its orange rays over the sands, Ren stood with Mikita, contemplating the distant horizon. The atmosphere of the desert reflected a strange calm, as if time had stopped. Mikita wore a light dress that danced with the breeze, her hair flowing like golden waves under the sun's rays.

Ren looked at her, his eyes holding a strange light, but there was something different about them this time. "I never imagined a sunset could be this beautiful," Mikita said softly. Ren smiled, a glimmer in his eyes shining like stars in the night sky. "You're right... but at this moment, in front of me is something even more beautiful than the sunset." "W-What..." Mikita blushed at his words, and to her surprise, Ren took slow, steady steps closer to her, looking into her bright green eyes.

Chapter 132: [131] A Night With Mikita [R-18]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2300 Words]

The sun had set behind the horizon, leaving the desert shrouded in darkness and deep silence. The cold winds whistled through the sands, capable of piercing even the toughest hearts and making anyone shiver from the chill. The sands, which relentlessly struck against each other, formed small whirlwinds that roamed randomly in the vast expanse, as if searching for life in this desolate place.

Hina exhaled a large cloud of cigarette smoke, gazing out at the blue sea from the window as she contemplated what to say. "Garp-san told Hina that it's not that simple..." Before Tashigi could respond, Hina continued, "If he were just a low-level Warlord without significant influence or power, like Moria who fell, things wouldn't be so complicated... But Black Death, his sheer power is a problem, not to mention the abilities of his crew members, and no one knows how many there are or if there are any hidden forces."

Hina flicked away her finished cigarette and grabbed another, lighting it under Tashigi's

attentive gaze.

*Huff...*

"Hina believes that to convince the World Government to withdraw a deterrent force against pirates like the Sin Crew, Hina must provoke a significant event... an event so condemned that the World Government will have no choice but to strip him of his status and send a powerful individual to subdue him!"

"And the person who will be sent is...?" Tashigi asked, her eyes hiding strange thoughts.

"That's right, it will be Garp-san, but... we must first strip him of his status... Ah, this is tough! Hina just wants to rest!" Hina complained, taking a deep drag from her cigarette. "If I may ask, Hina-san? Did Vice Admiral Garp tell you where he is going?" Tashigi inquired cautiously, hoping her words wouldn't sound suspicious.

Hina didn't think long before replying, removing the cigarette from her mouth, "He has gone

to the East Blue for a long vacation."

"I understand, Hina-san! If there's anything I can do, just let me know!" Tashigi nodded seriously and excused herself, claiming she would return to training.

'This is dangerous! I must inform the captain about this!'

...

At the same time, in the vast sea as usual, a naval ship sailed swiftly toward its destination

without stopping.

What distinguished this ship was the dog head emblem painted on its sails, making any informed pirate tremble and change course to avoid encountering the captain of this ship.

Currently, inside one of the ship's rooms, specifically in the captain's cabin, an interesting

scene was unfolding.

"Y-You say Ace is...dead?" A trembling voice filled with panic came from the young man with black hair and a straw hat, Luffy.

"That's right... I couldn't prevent it... I couldn't stop it..." Garp muttered with a bitter expression as he looked at his grandson, Luffy, who was still grappling with the shock of losing his crew members. Then, more sorrowful news struck him: the death of his brother Ace.

"T-This can't be true... It can't happen!!" Luffy shouted, rising from his seat, attempting to leave the room, but Garp restrained him, looking at him sternly.

"Ace is gone! You must accept this! This was the path he chose, Luffy! You would have become like him if I hadn't locked you up here..." Garp shouted with a fierce tone, anger resonating in

his voice.

Luffy was about to scream and resist, but his tear-stained face froze when he saw the tears streaming down his grandfather's face.

Throughout his 17-year life, Luffy had never seen his grandfather cry. This was the first time

he discovered the sad side of his usually cheerful and irritating grandfather, deepening the pain in his heart in an indescribable way.

Garp was crying silently, his face expressing deep sorrow as he looked at his grandson,

continuing to speak: "You and Ace, both of you are foolish and reckless... but you don't

deserve to die!"

"Ah... Grandpa... Ace... he's really gone, isn't he?" Luffy's tone, once filled with anger, shifted

to profound despair as he looked at his sad grandfather. He realized that what he had previously thought was not just nonsense. Perhaps he knew that his grandfather was genuinely serious, but his mind forced him to reject this reality. And seeing his grandfather surrender to his grief, he thought, maybe... maybe it's okay to mourn and break down here,

isn't it?

"Yes... Ace is gone! And I don't want you to leave too... I have no family left since your foolish father ran off to become a revolutionary! You damn idiot!" Garp's words were laced with curses, yet they were filled with affection for his only grandson.

Luffy looked at Garp with tear-filled eyes, not focusing on his words about his father; even if

he heard them, he wouldn't care in such a situation.

*Weeping*

*Weeping*

"Ace..."

"Ace!!!" Luffy screamed, beginning to show signs of instability, prompting Garp to bring him

back to reality with a painful slap.

"Stop being pathetic! You idiot!" Garp roared, looking at his grandson with a weary expression. "Ace is dead, yes, but that doesn't mean it's the end of the world! Instead of drowning in your sorrow, get up, start training, and become stronger! Strong enough to make those who killed your friends and caused your brother's death pay the price!"

"Stop crying like a child and get up, Luffy! Stop being pathetic!"

Garp's shouts echoed throughout the ship, causing the crew to feel terrified of approaching

Garp's furious room.

But the truth was that Garp was not just angry; he was angry and sad, furious at himself for his weakness and helplessness. The Marine hero? That was nonsense; he was just an old man who couldn't do anything while his grandson was executed, the child he watched grow up to

become a man.

"I... I..." Luffy cried, his tears streaming onto the floor, remembering what had happened began, that battle against the with blue hair.

before his nig

Slowly but surely, Luffy realized what had happened to him and who was the cause of his

nightmare.

....

Back at Sabaody Archipelago.

In the quiet streets of Grove 13, three figures in black cloaks walked by, ignoring the greedy

and harsh stares of the pirates that seemed to gaze at them with great interest.

"Shall I clean them up?" A feminine voice came from the shortest figure, seemingly waiting

for a response from the taller one, obscured by the black cloak.

"Do it quickly."

When she was given the green light, Mikita did not hesitate. White sheets erupted from her concealed body beneath the cloak, hundreds of white sheets fluttering in the air, forming

numerous white blades that flew in different directions.

"Ah! What is this?!" "Ugh! Damn, my hand!!"

"What the hell is happening?! This--"

"Our captain is dead! Run for your liv--" "Damn it, I knew something was wrong with these peop--"

"Ahhh!!"

As the paper blades shot towards their designated locations, cries and wails echoed from the

dark alleyways.

Dozens of pirates were cut to pieces in mere seconds, their severed hands, legs, and necks bearing witness to the previous massacre, while the ground turned red from the tainted blood spilled from the slain corpses. It seemed as if Mother Nature mourned for having to drink the

filthy blood of the pirates.

*Thud Thud*

*Thud Thud*

*Splat Splat!*

Light raindrops began to fall from the sky, quickly intensifying, washing the fresh blood away

to an unknown place. Fortunately, Kalifa, Mikita, and Ren had left the area a while ago, so their cloaks wouldn't get

wet.

"This is our destination..."

Ren stared at the tavern a few dozen meters away, then saw the sign boldly written:

Shakky's Rip-off Bar.

Chapter 135: [134] Ren's Dream
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Bonus Chapter.

[2300 Words]

The building housing the tavern featured a unique and eye-catching design that beautifully combined rustic charm with nautical elements, perfectly reflecting the atmosphere of the One Piece world.

The exterior was comprised of a single floor topped with a massive, semi-spherical green dome, lending it a distinctive and unusual character that captured attention from afar. This vibrant green dome stood out as one of the most prominent features, its radiant color breaking the monotony of the structure and evoking a sense of nature and the greenery surrounding it.

Beneath the dome, a classic circular ceiling extended, adorned with arched windows framed in traditional wood, which provided a warm and inviting feel.

The stone walls encircling the building displayed earthy tones, blending shades of gray and light brown, partially covered by creeping vines that cascaded down the walls, enhancing the sense of age and making the tavern seem like a natural part of its environment. It appeared as if this establishment had been constructed decades ago.

The main entrance was robust and prominent, topped with a large wooden sign displaying the tavern's name in bold letters, flanked by simple decorations that added a traditional and cozy touch. Small stone steps led up to the entrance, harmonizing with the overall design and creating an aesthetic balance between sturdiness and warmth.

"Captain, is the interesting person you mentioned earlier here?" Mikita asked softly as she ascended the stone steps, while Kalifa remained silent, waiting for a response from Ren like Mikita, both curious about the identity of the individual in question.

"Yes, he is a legendary figure who has been out of sight for a long time..." Ren replied calmly as he pushed open the wooden door of the tavern.

*Creek~*

Ren glanced around; the tavern showcased a warm interior and a cozy atmosphere that echoed classic and rustic styles. The ceiling was made of wooden planks, slightly sloped to add a touch of quirkiness, while soft lights danced over the wood, imparting a comforting and serene ambiance. The walls were painted in a light wood color with some exposed stone, evoking a sense of simplicity and warmth that reflected traditional vibes. The arched windows, framed elegantly in wood, lined the back wall, allowing natural light to flood the space and adding a gentle brightness.

In the center, a long, polished wooden table stretched out, showing signs of frequent use that hinted at the stories and memories shared within this space. Behind the bar, wooden shelves displayed an array of neatly arranged glass mugs, adorned with a few small paintings and menus, adding a personal touch and making the tavern feel like a beloved refuge for visitors. On the walls, simple decorations such as a circular rescue sign contributed to the tavern's understated nautical charm. The arrangement of furniture and colors struck a perfect balance between simplicity and elegance, with each piece appearing as if it were part of the rich story and history of the place.

Unfortunately, appearances can be deceiving.

At first glance, this tavern seemed like a safe haven for pirates and outlaws in the Grooves numbered 1 to 30, but an intriguing scene was unfolding at that moment.

A woman exuded confidence and allure, with short black hair and sharp features. She wore a distinctive outfit combining black and pink, with a belt adorned with a heart cinching her waist and white fabric tied around her hips.

Holding a cigarette in one hand and gripping the neck of a massive, bloodied man who was unconscious with the other, she exhaled a puff of smoke and said cheerfully, "How many times have I told you fools not to fight in my tavern?"

Her words were directed at the defeated pirate's companions, who stared at her in terror and shouted in unison, "W-we're sorry, ma'am! We won't do it again!"

The woman tossed the unconscious man at them and said coldly, "Get out of my tavern now, but first, pay for the mess you made!"

Her icy words pierced the hearts of the pirates, sending chills down their spines. In fear and submission, they pulled out every Berrie they had and handed it over with ugly smiles on their faces.

"Now get lost." It seemed the woman's mood had improved, and she allowed the troublesome pirates to leave.

"Yes! Yes! We'll leave immediately! Shakuyaku-sama!" they exclaimed joyfully, seemingly unconcerned about losing their money, with one of them grabbing the unconscious man as they hurried to the exit.

Ren, Mikita, and Kalifa watched this scene with varying expressions. Ren, familiar with Shakuyaku's character from the original work and her method of dealing with troublesome pirates, was not surprised by the unfolding events. Meanwhile, Mikita and Kalifa exchanged mild glances of surprise at the older woman. Is this the one Captain Ren is interested in?

'But she's not that strong... 'Mikita thought internally, her hidden eyes beneath the cloak fixed on Shakuyaku.

"Hey! You blind fool, get out of the way!"

The group of pirates who had previously been pardoned shouted at Ren, who stood blocking the doorway. This drew Shakuyaku's attention, causing her to furrow her brow in annoyance. "Are you still trying to cause trouble? It seems you haven't learned-"

*Boom-!*

Before she could finish, a focused wave of Conqueror's Haki emanated from Ren, knocking the four pirates to the ground.

Shakuyaku fell silent, recognizing what had just happened. She glanced at Ren, her curiosity mixed with caution.

Just then, an elderly man with white hair emerged from one of the tavern's rooms and

regarded Ren calmly.

"Hahaha, what is someone like you doing in this humble tavern?" The old man looked at Ren with a small smile, though a hint of caution flickered in his eyes.

After a brief pause, Ren replied, still looking at the old man, "That question should be directed at a legendary figure like you, right, Dark King Rayleigh?"

"!!!"

The girls behind Ren widened their eyes, but Shakuyaku and Rayleigh remained oblivious, their faces obscured.

Immediately after Ren spoke, Rayleigh regarded him in surprise. "Oh? So you recognized me; the new generation is indeed frightening~"

"But I'm just a simple coating mechanic, I am not the person I once was. You should remember that well."

Ren sensed a shift in Rayleigh's tone from friendly to something resembling a veiled threat. His Observation Haki picked up on the change, yet he remained unfazed, smiling coldly as he removed his hood, revealing his identity. He stared directly at Rayleigh and said, "So what if I

do this?"

Shakuyaku, who had been curious about the identity of the cloaked figure, now observed him cautiously. She sensed the atmosphere growing tense as she glanced at Rayleigh from the

corner of her eye.

Rayleigh remained silent, studying Ren, seemingly lost in thought.

Even Mikita and Kalifa, standing behind Ren, were on high alert, their battle instincts kicking

in. Although they knew they wouldn't stand a chance against the legendary Dark King, they were ready to protect Ren or at least confront the woman who was calmly smoking her

cigarette.

"Hahahahaha!"

"Rehahahaha!"

To the girls' shock, however, Rayleigh and Ren suddenly burst into laughter, the tension evaporating and replaced by a casual ease.

Ren turned to Shakuyaku and said, "Consider me just an ordinary customer; please serve me

and my companions your best drinks."

Shakuyaku smiled, maintaining her professional demeanor, as if she had anticipated the outcome of their earlier confrontation. Puffing out smoke, she replied, "Of course- but let me

warn you, our prices are quite steep."

"No problem."

Ren responded in a friendlier tone before addressing the two girls beside him, "Mikita,

Kalifa, come sit with us."

The two girls nodded, removing their hoods. Before sitting down, Mikita tossed the unconscious pirates out of the tavern and joined Kalifa and Ren at the table.

Rayleigh approached and sat across from Ren, while Shakuyaku went to prepare their drinks.

Ren studied the legendary old man with keen interest. This individual knew many secrets of the world, including truths that even Ren was unaware of, such as the mystery of One Piece, the Void Century, and the Joy Boy legacy along with its enemies.

'Should I ask him?' The thought crossed his mind, but would Rayleigh truly answer? Unlike his friendly interactions with Luffy and his crew in the original story, Ren doubted Rayleigh would divulge the secrets he and Roger's crew had uncovered after years of hardship gathering the Poneglyphs.

While Ren might not seem interested in a title like the Pirate King, that didn't mean he would ignore the history and reality of the World Government. His goal was to rule this world and overthrow the powerful entity known as the World Government, so knowing their history was

essential.

"You seem lost in thought, huh?" Rayleigh's voice broke into Ren's reflections. He looked at the old man and replied, "Don't mind me; I'm actually curious about something." "Oh? What is it? Go ahead and tell me." Rayleigh regarded the pirate before him calmly, but internally he was stunned by a specific detail.

'This person... he has grown stronger since that war... much stronger. This is strange. Did he hold back before and not reveal his true strength?' Though Rayleigh had retired to live a peaceful life in the Sabaody Archipelago, he couldn't overlook any unknown individual who emerged from obscurity and displayed significant power and deep plans.

He suspected that Ren had concealed his strength during the war because his aura felt different from what it had been at the Paramount War. Even though Rayleigh hadn't been present at Marineford, he could still gauge a person's power and skill from their aura alone. After all, his title as Dark King wasn't just a remnant of the past!

'If my guess is correct, this person is more dangerous than I initially thought!' Unaware of Rayleigh's thoughts, which were masked by a calm exterior, Ren quickly posed his question: "Did you know that Fire Fist Ace is the son of your captain?"

Immediately after Ren finished speaking, the atmosphere in the bar shifted. Shaki entered, carrying five glasses of wine-three placed in front of Ren and the two girls, and one for the silent Rayleigh, who seemed lost in thought.

After a few moments of silence, Rayleigh spoke in a slightly heavy voice:

"That's true... that kid had a kind heart. Unfortunately, he met you. This makes me wonder something: Did you know Ace's identity when you decided to hand him over to the Marines? Or did you just think he was merely the second division commander of the Whitebeard Pirates and believed handing him over would earn you the World Government's favor?" Rayleigh's tone changed at this moment; his words felt extremely cold.

Rayleigh knew that Ace was the son of Gol D. Roger and had recognized this truth before Ace's execution. He clearly saw the resemblance between Ace's features and Roger's, which heightened his suspicions about Ace's true identity. However, Rayleigh remained silent and respected Roger's dying wish that his former crew not interfere in Ace's life, allowing him to live on his own terms. This knowledge weighed heavily on him, creating an internal struggle between his desire to protect Ace and his loyalty to his friend's request. In the end, he decided not to intervene, leaving Ace's fate to destiny-unfortunately. Returning to Ren, his expression remained unchanged as he looked at Rayleigh, who seemed

to be in a different mood again, unlike the two girls who felt a bit tense. After all, they were in the presence of the legendary Dark King-anyone would feel anxious!

'If I lie, will his Observation Haki pick up on anything? If I get nervous while lying, that could happen... but...' Ren pondered mentally, then smiled coolly, a smile that both Rayleigh and Shaki noticed clearly. The next moment, he activated his "Cold Heart" skill and replied in an

icy voice.

"Yes, I knew that... and that's why I handed him over to the Marines. What will you do about

it, Dark King Rayleigh?"

"!!!!!"

Shaki's and the girls' expressions shifted behind Ren; no one expected him to respond that

way. They glanced at Rayleigh, but to their surprise, there was no anger on his face.

"Ah... you really surprised me."

Rayleigh sighed, took a sip of his drink to refresh his throat, then looked at Ren again.

"I think Roger didn't want his former crew to interfere in Ace's journey." He sighed sadly, appearing to reminisce about the man he considered one of his closest friends, then continued: "Ace was a pirate and lost because he was weak. I'm sure Ace was aware of the risks associated with choosing that path."

"As expected from you, Dark King; you're truly unpredictable," Ren remarked as he sipped his

cold wine and gazed at Rayleigh with a different look than before.

"But it makes me wonder, with that mind and the power you still possess, have you really

decided to retire for good?"

Under Ren's inquisitive gaze, Rayleigh smiled and replied, "As I mentioned earlier, I'm just

an old man wanting some peace..." He looked at Shaki, who winked at him while smoking her

cigarette. Then he turned his attention back to the pirate and asked, "Now it's my turn to ask you, what is your Dream?"

Silence fell over the bar once again as everyone looked at Ren, four pairs of curious eyes eagerly awaiting his ambition and goals.

Everyone has a dream they strive to achieve, don't they? So, what is the Dream of this pirate

who rose to prominence in just three months?

These were the thoughts of Rayleigh and Shaki.

It took less than half a minute before Ren opened his mouth and said:

"My dream is... to unify this world....to unify this world under my grip!"

Chapter 136: [135] Unexpected
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[3400 Words]

"My dream is...is To unify this world under my grip!"

Ren spoke this line in a calm tone, as though it was nothing at all.

Mikita and Kalifa heard his words clearly. For a fleeting moment, they doubted their hearing, thinking perhaps they were hallucinating. But that moment of doubt lasted only a split second before giving way to shock.

Absolute shock. For the first time, Mikita and Kalifa understood their captain's dream-and it was more than just difficult... No, one could simply call it impossible.

But then, memories of their captain's miracles resurfaced. The simplest example: reviving the dead. Wasn't that impossible too? And yet, Ren had managed it, hadn't he? So... perhaps... perhaps he really could achieve this dream.

Mikita, as Ren's woman and someone who trusted him more than herself, was the first to believe he would realize his dream. In her heart, Ren was a source of miracles. Control and rule over the world? Her man could absolutely do it!

As for Kalifa, despite the intense shock coursing through her, she had a feeling-a conviction that he could do it. The man before her... could actually bring down the World Government, a regime fortified by eight centuries of power and history.

These thoughts flickered through Mikita's and Kalifa's minds in mere seconds. Externally, Shakky froze at Ren's response, while Rayleigh regarded him with calm eyes.

"Are you aware that saying such things could get you killed, boy?" Rayleigh finally spoke, looking at Ren with a new level of respect.

'His ambition surpasses my expectations!' Rayleigh thought to himself, sensing the looming storm-one powerful enough to sweep away everything in its path.

"Perhaps you're right. But... failure only means death. Nothing too grand, right?" Ren smiled as a cigarette materialized in his hand. He lit it with a small flame from his right eye and began smoking.

'A devil fruit ability?' Rayleigh Though internally.

"Fuuuh... Does my answer satisfy you?" Ren exhaled a thick plume of smoke, his gaze locking onto the Dark King, who was silent, lost in thought.

His words seemed to pull the old man from his thoughts. Rayleigh looked back and asked frankly, "Just how far are you willing to go to achieve this dream?"

'These questions... Is he testing my personality or what?' Ren wondered, narrowing his eyes. His previously calm expression shifted, and the cigarette in his hand crumbled to dust.

"How far? Rehahaha... Are you certain you want the answer to that question?" His tone turned icy, and Shakky's eyes narrowed as she looked at Rayleigh seated beside her.

Rayleigh, still unfazed, continued to gaze at the blue-haired man. "Yes, I'm Sure," he replied without hesitation, his demeanor transforming from a peaceful old man to a legendary, fearsome pirate.

Ren's smile broadened at Rayleigh's unwavering response, and he uttered chilling words: "If I can't rule this world, I'll destroy it. It's as simple as that!"

"!!!!!!!

The expressions of Mikita, Kalifa, and Shakky shifted to shock. For the first two, they now understood the full extent of their captain's madness.

'Is he insane...' Shakky thought, her gaze on Ren now more cautious than ever.

'The captain really... is beyond prediction,' Kalifa murmured, though the faint smile on her face showed a fondness for his madness.

'As expected of the captain... no, my man. I wouldn't mind if he destroyed this rotten world and built a better one.' Mikita thought, and as an outlaw herself, Ren's words barely fazed her.

"You're dangerous."

The girls' thoughts were interrupted by a deep voice. It was Rayleigh, studying Ren with a look that no longer held the previous neutrality.

Ren remained indifferent to Rayleigh's chilly tone, regarding him with amusement. "And what will you do about it? Do you really wish to fight here and reveal your presence on Sabaody Archipelago to the Marines? That would be unfortunate... This bar is quite lovely, and I'd hate to see it ruined... or its owner... killed."

"You...!" Rayleigh stood, releasing a powerful wave of Conqueror's Haki toward Ren and the women behind him. But the blue-haired pirate merely smiled back at Rayleigh's fury.

In the next moment, a surge of black Conqueror's Haki shot toward Rayleigh and Shakky. The latter retreated behind Rayleigh, who shielded her with his own Haki, while Ren similarly protected the women standing behind him.

*Bzzzzz~!*

*Crash~!*

*Crack~!*

Glasses shattered, tables and chairs splintered, and countless whiskey and wine bottles tumbled to the floor as the two forces of Conqueror's Haki collided, each striving to subdue the other.

"You've really aged, Dark King Rayleigh!" Ren sneered at the old man, maintaining his struggle against the opposing Haki. Though his mastery was only moderate, he wouldn't be easily overpowered-even as Rayleigh's seasoned Haki pressed back against his own.

"You and your tongue... tsk." Rayleigh looked at Ren with noticeable annoyance, and in the next moment, he ceased using his Conqueror's Haki as he noticed the damage in the bar. Ren did the same.

The previous noise vanished as if it had never existed. The two characters stared at each other while Shakky, Mikita, and Kalifa observed them with watchful eyes.

Shakuyaku: "...."

The woman was certainly angry about the ruckus, but Rayleigh had something more important to do.

"Your previous words... I hope they were just a joke." He looked at Ren with a serious expression, then pondered briefly before continuing, "Your ambition is far too great; you remind me of an ancient monster, a human beast that perished long ago. Believe me, such a selfish dream will end in a miserable fate, just like that person." A nostalgic look crossed Rayleigh's face as he mentioned this.

'Is he referring to Rocks D. Xebec?' Ren did not react but gazed at Rayleigh again and replied, "Everyone has their own ambitions, so I don't think it's wise to interfere in people's dreams. Don't you think?"

"You're right, but... this world already has a savior waiting for it, and it's not someone who rules with an iron fist... and I don't think it's you, Ren. The savior of this world is cheerful, unlike you." Rayleigh recalled his conversation with Shanks long ago and felt curious about the young man he mentioned. Why hadn't he appeared in Sabaody with his crew yet? This puzzled Rayleigh.

On Ren's side, his lips twitched in sarcasm. Of course, he knew whom Rayleigh was referring to. He wondered what Rayleigh would think if he learned what had happened to Luffy... There was no doubt the old man might directly attack him if that were the case. Knowing Rayleigh's character, he was aware that the old man could overlook the death of his captain's son, disregard Hachi's death, and anything else, but he couldn't ignore something terrible happening to the "Bearer of Roger's Will" and "The Liberator of this World."

Fortunately for Ren, Rayleigh didn't know anything yet. Once he found out what had happened to Luffy and how it affected his mindset, he might become an enemy to the old man and be forced to kill him.

"I see... If you have nothing else to add, I'll take my leave now." Ren didn't seem angered by Rayleigh's dismissive words about his dream, where he stated that this world was already taken care of, and there was no need for his dream of uniting it.

Rayleigh appeared surprised by Ren's reaction, which made him truly realize the danger of the person in front of him. He maintained his composure even after deliberately belittling his dream... Those who possess Conqueror's Haki hate nothing more than seeing their dreams scoffed at... So why wasn't he reacting?

Rayleigh found no answer to this question even after Ren and the girls moved toward the bar's exit, having already put on their hoods to conceal their faces.

"Wait! What about the damages you caused?" Shakky, who was smoking a cigarette, looked at

Ren with a flat expression and demanded her payment.

Ren paused and glanced at Shakky before smiling, "I already paid for our drinks... As for the damages, if you want compensation, come find me in Alabasta."

"Pfttt..!" Mikita tried to suppress her laughter but failed, while even the usually professional Kalifa chuckled beneath her black cloak.

"You...!" The veins bulged on Shakuyaku's forehead, a clear sign of her anger, as she glared at

Ren in annoyance.

Rayleigh, on the other hand, had a strange look on his face... Was this brat flirting with

Shakky-chan?

Ren had no idea what Rayleigh was thinking. He looked at him and spoke, "It was truly enjoyable meeting you and chatting, The Dark King. We may meet again... then it will be up to you to decide how our relationship will be. Remember, when the time comes, choose wisely."

*Creek~*

In the next moment, only Rayleigh and Shakuyaku remained in the bar, which was currently in

disarray.

"Tsk... arrogant brat!" Shakky complained angrily and quickly lit another cigarette to calm her

nerves.

But unlike her, Rayleigh returned to his soft couch and lowered his head, seeming to be lost in thought. This state continued for a few minutes before he let out a long sigh.

"Sigh..."

"This era is heading toward chaos. Perhaps I should talk to Shanks; it's been a while since I

last spoke with him."

....

At Grove 18.

Three figures cloaked in black were hastily walking through the lawless neighborhoods,

ignoring the pirates and thieves who looked at them with curiosity.

"The trees have roots deep in the ocean... Our world is truly magnificent." Nami said softly as her delicate white hand gently touched the mangrove wood. "Fufufu- Maybe we can see the roots of these trees when we dive to the ocean floor on our way to Fish-Man Island?" Robin wondered curiously. She had previously read a book about mangrove trees that could grow to great heights underwater and live for thousands of years. She was incredibly curious about these trees, and now she finally had the chance to see one

with her own eyes.

"By the way, it's time to meet Captain Ren and the others; an hour has passed, as you know." Nojiko spoke to them quietly but with an urgent tone.

"What? Did you miss your lover that quickly, Nojiko?" Nami laughed and teased her sister.

Seeing Nami's behavior, Nojiko didn't get angry. On the contrary, she looked at her with a small smile on her face. Unfortunately, this smile was hidden because her face was covered. "That's right~ I wish I could spend all the time in this world with Ren~"

"You-"

"Sigh... Here we go again..." Just as Nami was about to respond, Robin sighed and tried to ease the tension between the two girls.

While the girls were laughing and joking with each other, their eyes suddenly narrowed, and

they looked behind them.

"You three, the This god demands to see your faces, and... why aren't you kneeling?"

The source of the voice was behind them, and the girls recognized it thanks to their

Observation Haki.

"Damn Celestial Dragon..." Nami muttered coldly under her breath, audible only to Nojiko and Robin, then turned to glare at the ugly man who was looking at them blankly.

This Celestial Dragon, like the rest of his kind, was dressed in brilliant white attire, resembling an arrogant astronaut, adorned with shiny buttons indicating his high status. His large glass helmet encased his head, giving him an impervious look, breathing only the filtered air that he thought kept him away from the common people.

He held a shiny small gun in his hand, entirely made of gold, revealing the extent of this

stupid pig's extravagance.

His silver gloves covered his chubby hands as he stood firmly on the ground, surrounded by an

air of innate arrogance, making those around him feel that he did not belong to this underworld but to a higher class-the class of Celestial Dragons, the gods.

The girls looked and saw a complete line of people kneeling in fear, and surprisingly, many

pirates were also kneeling in fear, not daring to make a single sound. They also saw several dead bodies-men, women, -who seemed to have been shot in vital areas. "Kneel, you animals! Kneel before me, Saint Leonious Supremia! Liohahahaha!" The disgusting laughter of the Celestial Dragon echoed throughout Grove 18, but no one

dared to make a sound.

Surrounding Saint Leonious Supremia were five men in black clothing, members of Cipher Pol

responsible for protecting the Celestial Dragons, and there was some slaves in the ground. "You, you stupid dog, kill those three; they refuse to kneel before me, and that annoys me."

Saint Leonious spoke with annoyance, pointing at one of the agents dressed in a suit and black

glasses.

"Yes, my lord!" the agent replied coldly, then disappeared from his spot and reappeared in

front of Nojiko, aiming his finger imbued with Haki at her heart.

"!!!"

His expression froze when he felt the imminent danger; his spine trembled in fear. He wanted

to retreat, but it was too late.

*Swish~!*

Nami quickly drew her sword and cut towards the cold-looking agent-well, his face wasn't

cold anymore, anyway.

*Splash!*

Under the terrified gazes of everyone, the agent was split in two and fell to the ground dead,

his blood pouring onto the ground.

"Y-You, you dog slave! How dare you die without my permission?! And you, the other slave,

how dare you kill my servant-" Saint Leonious Supremia shouted like a raging pig, but he could no longer speak as he felt someone behind him.

"Look who we have here... a troublesome Celestial Pig... Hmm." A cold voice spoke from behind him, making Saint Leonious Supremia, the proud, majestic, and noble Celestial Dragon, feel fear. But soon, that feeling was suppressed and replaced with anger and

madness. "I am Leonious Supremia! I am a god! How can I fear just a mere human slave?! You, guards! Kill everyone who breathes here and then kill yourselves... or don't kill yourselves; otherwise, how will I return to the Holy Land? Liohahaha, well, I'll be merciful and let you kill yourselves

when you return me to the Holy Land." The Celestial Dragon began to talk to himself like a fool, but he soon noticed that the guards were not responding to him.

In fact, he was still staring at Nojiko, Nami, and Robin, never turning around. Why should he?

They should come to him to kneel before him; mere humans should be honored to see his divine form, shouldn't that be a privilege? "Hey, you slave guards, why aren't you listening-"

Saint Leonious Supremia turned his body and looked behind him while speaking, but his eyes

widened in shock at the current scene.

He was looking at dozens of corpses; the pirates who had been kneeling before him were all

dead, as were his guards, lying on the ground without a trace of life and with deep wounds on their bodies.

"As expected from a Celestial Dragon... your stupidity amazes me." Ren spoke in a tone loud enough for Saint Leonious Supremia to hear.

Ren's figure was concealed beneath his cloak, accompanied by his crew members. A cold smile

crept onto his face as he extended his hand toward the terrified Celestial Dragon. "You-you! Lowly human, what do you intend to do to this god-" Saint Leonious was silenced by the forceful blow from Ren's fist. Without a moment's hesitation, a white tremor of energy erupted from his hand, crushing the Celestial Dragon's skull.

*Crack~!*

The small earthquake was precisely focused, targeting only the Celestial Dragon's head. In

the next instant, his head was reduced to a paste of flesh and blood.

Ren discarded the lifeless body onto the ground as if tossing aside a dead dog, then turned to

Kalifa and the others, who were in shock.

"Take us back to Elsama before the admiral arrives." Ren's tone was calm and devoid of any

fear.

Kalifa snapped out of her daze regarding what had just occurred and looked at Ren with newfound respect. She quickly composed herself and replied, "A-at your command, Captain!"

.

"Cosmic Travel."

As soon as she uttered those words, a silver aura burst forth around Kalifa, extending like a small whirlwind that enveloped her body. It gradually expanded to include everyone-Ren, Mikita, Robin, Nami, and Nojiko. From within this shimmering aura, a light resembling stardust emerged, sparkling in silver and interwoven with deep purple threads, as if they were witnessing a piece of another dimension pulsating with life.

As the aura's power intensified, the ground beneath their feet began to fade slowly, as if it were disappearing into a dark abyss. Everyone felt as though they were detaching from reality, their bodies light as if weightless, drifting in a strange sensation between dream and

reality.

Earlier, Ren had requested Kalifa to create a "focal point" in Elsama for the instant transfer experiment, and what was unfolding now was the result.

In a fleeting moment, they found themselves surrounded by a horizon of sparkling colors and twinkling starry whirlwinds, resembling a journey through space itself. The surrounding space was saturated with vibra shades of blue, silver, and purple, dancing like galaxies swimming peacefully in a cold void. A mysterious sensation of delicate vibrations coursed

through their bodies, as if they were gliding rapidly through the universe, yet everything felt strangely serene.

Ren took a deep breath, contemplating, 'Is this outer space? No, that doesn't make sense... perhaps it's part of the spatial corridor?' He sensed that Kalifa had an answer to this

enigmatic question.

By the way, Ren was capable of intercepting this process using his Haki, owing to the fact that

Kalifa's would need to be at least at the same level as him to resist his intervention in the

instant transfer.

Meanwhile, Nami felt a slight dizziness and whispered, "So this is the feeling of instant

transfer..." while Nojiko experienced mild dizziness but managed to maintain her balance

amid this cosmic journey.

Robin felt greater dizziness but, with a calm demeanor, gazed at the horizon around them

and whispered in admiration, "It's an incredible power... it allows us to see the world from a different perspective." Her words faded into the vastness.

Suddenly, the light surrounding them began to shift as if receding, and as the silver glow diminished, they felt solid ground beneath their feet once more. The air around them was warm, contrasting sharply with the coldness of the dark space. They opened their eyes to find themselves on the deck of the ship Elsama, floating high among the clouds. Moments of astonishment lingered before Mikita caught her breath and remarked, looking slightly dizzy, "This transfer... feels like flying through the void of space filled with stars... a sensation that is both enchanting and terrifying at the same time."

Kalifa, breathing heavily after her exertion, smiled faintly and said, "It wasn't easy, but it was worth every bit of effort."

Ren smiled calmly after removing his cloak and looked at Kalifa, speaking reassuringly, "Well

done, Kalifa. When you become stronger and gain Haki, this skill will improve significantly,

and you won't feel as fatigued after using it." "Thank you, Captain!" Kalifa replied with an enthusiastic smile.

Robin gazed at Ren with a look filled with wonder and awe, saying, "Still I can't believe what

happened down there..." she recalled the image of the Celestial Dragon who had been killed earlier. The other girls who remained aboard Elsama approached the returning group, curiosity evident on their faces. One of them asked, "What happened down there, Robin?" Ren glanced at Kalifa, signaling her to share the details. She began recounting the story from

their arrival at Grove 13 up to the thrilling incident at Grove 18.

As she narrated the events, shock spread across the girls' faces with each sentence. Nami,

Nojiko, and Robin were astonished by Ren and his crew's encounter with the Dark King,

Rayleigh.

However, upon mentioning the recent incident-the killing of the Celestial Dragon- The girls fell into a profound shock, realizing for the first time the audacity and madness of

their captain.

Meanwhile, Elsama glided gracefully into the depths of the sea, creating a thin barrier to protect itself from the water as it headed toward Fish-Man Island with pride and speed.

Leila was the only one who didn't call Ren "Captain"; instead, she referred to him as "brother." This was a habit she'd picked up from her past life, so how could she change it now? It would feel strange to call him "Captain."

Back in the present, the girls nodded in agreement with what Leila had said, though they were still in awe of Ren's actions.

'A Celestial Dragon... When I was with the Marines, I'd heard some legends about them. I never expected one to meet such a fate at Ren's hands,' Bell-mere thought, standing on the deck, her unreadable gaze fixed on Ren.

The transparent barrier surrounding Elsama served the same purpose as the coating applied to ships aiming to pass into the New World through Fish-Man Island, though the difference was that this barrier was stronger than a coating bubble and wouldn't easily break.

Hearing Robin's words, the girls-eight in total, including Robin-stopped discussing the previous events and instead turned to admire the stunning view before them.

They were currently in the epipelagic zone, where sunlight abounded, allowing marine life to thrive. Colorful fish swam freely in the water, like neon fish and clownfish, darting among vibrant coral reefs. The varied colors filling the scene made the girls feel as though they were in a dreamlike world.

"Wow..." Kaya murmured, her eyes wide with amazement at the breathtaking sight.

"The ocean is truly full of beauty~" Nami whispered, but her voice was clearly heard by everyone on the ship, thanks to the profound silence.

Hearing Nami's words, both Nojiko and Mikita nodded in agreement, equally captivated by the view. They were just ordinary girls from the four seas, unaccustomed to such splendid sights, so how could they not feel awe and appreciation for this journey?

Even Kalifa, known for her cold and strict demeanor, looked at the beautiful scene before her with eyes that held a subtle admiration. Such sights were not something she would have seen if she'd remained in Water 7, doing that accursed job as a government dog.

Zala, too, seemed lost in her own thoughts. For a moment, everyone fell into absolute silence.

As they ventured deeper into the sea, they entered the Disphotic zone, where the light gradually faded. In this region, marine life became more mysterious and eerie. The previous beauty had vanished.

"Be cautious." Ren, who had been silent the whole time, finally spoke, drawing the girls' attention and putting them on alert, unlike their previous awe.

Sea creatures with dark colors and unusual forms, like deep-sea fish, appeared, as though they held secrets from the ocean's depths. Amid the darkness, giant jellyfish glowed with vibrant colors, like stars sparkling in the night.

"From now on, we'll be entering zones devoid of sunlight. Uncharted marine creatures reside here, and we must also be wary of deep-sea currents. It's said that if a ship gets caught in one of these currents, it will be buried so deep it would take 2,000 years to surface again." Ren recalled all his knowledge about the world beneath the sea and shared it with the girls. "Two thousand years? That's terrifying..." Zala commented, a hint of fear in her voice. The other girls wore serious expressions on their faces as well; none of them wanted to get trapped in such terrifying currents.

Their journey continued as they encountered some Sea Kings and weaker sea beasts, all of whom were swiftly slain by Mikita. This was her role as the crew's "fighter," after all.

As for how she accomplished this? She simply created sharp blades of white paper, coated them with Armament Haki, and hurled them at the sea beasts swiftly enough that water pressure couldn't weaken her attacks. The blades were strong enough to slice through both the water and the sea beasts.

"This is actually good training." Mikita grinned with excitement as she looked forward to whatever creature would attack her next.

As they descended deeper into the ocean's abyss, they finally reached the "Abyssal Zone," a place utterly devoid of light and teeming with strange creatures adapted to these harsh depths. Peculiar beings appeared, like the deep-sea fish that glided gracefully through the dark waters. Their forms were bizarre, some bearing terrifying fangs sharp enough to instill fear in the bravest of men.

"Let me light things up a bit," Kaya said, and with a flick of her hand, a blue flame ignited, casting light across the ship's deck and revealing the monsters surrounding them.

"Is that... a Giant Sea King?!" Kaya gasped, her gaze locked onto the colossal creature. No, she couldn't see its entire form-just one of its eyes, which alone dwarfed Elsama by multiple times!

Yet, the creature showed no intent to fight. Instead, it drifted past the ship, almost as if flaunting its power and dominance over these vast waters.

As the journey continued, the crew discovered even more remarkable sea life. From swift sharks to colorful deep-sea creatures, each one was a testament to the mysteries of the deep. Gradually, the darkness began to fade, as though they were approaching a source of light.

"We're close..." Ren murmured, his words clear to the crew, sparking their excitement for the adventure.

Meanwhile, Ren was deep in conversation with Ms. System.

rewards.]

to?"

Chapter 138: [137] Arrival

[2000 Words]

In the Sabaody Archipelago.

"Did you hear the rumors...? This is truly unbelievable..." A man in his thirties whispered with a trembling voice to his friend in the streets of Grove 30 and above, where criminal activities related to pirates were absent.

"Yeah, man, I heard about it... I can't believe what's happening..." The man replied to his friend's question, then glanced at the Marines who were on high alert in the streets.

In the past few minutes, shocking news had spread... news that a Celestial Dragon had been killed!

The news was so shocking that many people initially did not believe it, but seeing the warships and Marine ships that had arrived at the Sabaody Archipelago made it seem true! "Attention all citizens! You are not allowed to leave the island for the next hundred hours!"

One of the Marines spoke through what appeared to be a loudspeaker, and this scene unfolded in many groves; a curfew was imposed on the Sabaody Archipelago that had never happened before.

"A Celestial Dragon was killed... who is crazy enough to do this!" A woman whispered from her home as she watched the Marines spread everywhere from her window.

"Mom, mom, what's happening?" Beside her was a small child who looked worried.

"Nothing, dear, come on, let's go play." The woman smiled and took her son, trying to divert his attention from the chaos outside.

...

In Grove Number 13.

"Whew... he's really bold..." Shakuyaku, smoking a cigarette in a hidden spot, watched the hundreds of Marines who had arrived at Grove 13, the site of the murder; this was natural.

She had already closed the bar; there was no way she could operate under these circumstances anyway.

"Isn't he afraid of his status as a Warlord? One wrong move could expose what he did." Rayleigh muttered, and it seemed some thoughts were wandering in his mind.

"Do you want to tell the Marines who did it?" Shaki looked at him, trying to read what was on his mind.

"Doesn't that mean I'm doing a favor for the World Government? Hell no, I would never do that." Rayleigh laughed and showed no intention of doing what Shaki suggested.

...

"This is awful~ who did this... and I thought I would enjoy a nice vacation..." A bored voice came from a tall man with an annoying monkey-like face.

Of course, it was none other than Admiral Kizaru.

Previously, he had received an urgent and critical mission from Sengoku informing him that something big had happened in the Sabaody Archipelago, which made him rush here, and thanks to his Devil Fruit ability, he arrived in just a few minutes.

"S-Sir Admiral Kizaru... we have captured some pirates for interrogation!" One of the Marines, specifically the one in charge of the Marine base in the Sabaody Archipelago, approached him.

He was terrified; something like this happening on the island he was responsible for... it certainly meant his death!

If the World Government didn't find the culprit and execute him, they would execute him and his subordinates to vent their anger, and no one would dare object...

"Hmm... let's see you there, did you see who did this?" He pointed to a body covered with a golden shroud and placed on a jewel-studded stretcher.

After all, the deceased was a god! So this was natural.

"W-We didn't see anything, sir..!!" The pirate who appeared to be the group's captain spoke in a terrified voice; he recognized the man in front of him-none other than Admiral Kizaru. "This is a problem-" Kizaru replied with the same expression, then looked at the rest of the pirates and saw one of them seemed like he wanted to say something.

"Do you know anything? Speak." His voice was as usual, but there was a hint of threat and seriousness in his tone; even the lazy Kizaru understood what this meant and the level of chaos it could cause.

The pirate Kizaru pointed to trembled, then under the threat of the Marines' guns, he opened his mouth and said in a shaking voice filled with fear: "I-I didn't see him well... there were... no, there were three people... all wearing black cloaks.... I was lurking, trying to rob them, but when we saw the Celestial Dragon walking around... we knelt and... then..."

"What happened next?" Kizaru was interested and commanded him to continue.

"A-After that, the Celestial Dragon started yelling at one of the cloaked figures, then another person like them appeared and killed him!!"

He screamed in horror as he recalled that hellish scene, "Not only did he kill the Celestial Dragon, but he also killed dozens of pirates who were watching the show! Luckily, I survived! Thank goodness! Hahaha! I survived!"

"Get him away from here." Kizaru whispered to one of the Marines to take these pirates away and then sank into deep thought.

"Cloaked figures... and a power capable of killing dozens of pirates in seconds..."

He seemed to be pondering something, then looked at one of the Marines guarding the stretcher that held the body of the Celestial Dragon, "Let me see for a moment."

Then without waiting for any reply, he lifted the shroud and saw a headless corpse... "Eh... where did his head go?"

.....

The scene shifts to a palace that stands out on a strange island, where the towering structure resembles a legendary castle sparkling under the ocean's light. The palace's towers are surrounded by enchanting architectural details inspired by marine creatures, adorned with colorful coral and gently flowing fountains.

Upon entering the palace, the interior space is vast, with walls decorated with shells and pearls reflecting beams of light, creating a magical atmosphere. The ceiling is embellished with engravings depicting mythical sea creatures, while the halls are filled with the refreshing scent of the ocean, as if echoing the rich heritage and culture of the Fishmen. Inside this palace, specifically in the throne room, an interesting conversation unfolds.

A massive, muscular mermaid captures attention with his stature and majesty as he sits on a throne clearly meant for kings. He features a prominent red nose and small eyes that match the features of his face. Despite his age, he retains long, curly orange hair and sports a thick beard. His hair covers his chest and arms, enhancing his strong and imposing appearance.

He wears an elegant golden crown resembling a tulip, encrusted with jewels, and his wrists are adorned with shiny golden bracelets. In his hand, he grips a carefully crafted golden spear, reflecting his royal status and authority as the ruler of the Fishmen's island.

"Father, the number of pirates visiting our island has been increasing since Whitebeard's fall..." spoke a tall, slender mermaid to King Neptune, who sat on his throne with a gloomy

expression.

Neptune looked at Fukaboshi, his son, and sensed his concern.

Sighing... "You are right, we need protection from one of the three Yonko. Since Newgate fell, things have not boded well..." The recent rise in mermaid kidnappings is attributed to their island, which previously deterred pirates due to Whitebeard's flag, no longer providing any deterrent. It contains many beautiful mermaids who would be sold at high prices in surface

slave auctions.

Although it is primarily the mermaids living in the impoverished Fishman district without military protection who are being kidnapped and enslaved, they are still their people! "Your Majesty! I have something important to report!" Suddenly, a soldier entered and knelt on one knee, speaking in a panicked voice.

"What is happening?" Neptune looked at him with a calm expression and ordered him to

report what he had.

With every word that came from the soldier's mouth, Neptune's expression tightened.

....

[Moments Earlier.]

"So this is Fishman Island... what a massive bubble capable of containing an island, and that smaller bubble, is there a building inside it or what is that?" Kaya exclaimed excitedly as she gazed at the beautiful scene before her.

"That's right, Kaya, the smaller bubble is where the royal palace of the Ryugu Kingdom, called Ryugu Palace, is located."

Ren replied to the curious girl without taking his eyes off the stunning view.

"Was this hole in the Red Line formed naturally...?" It seemed that Robin was pondering something else as she looked at the perforated Red Line.

Fishman Island lies deep in the ocean, at a depth of 10,000 meters below sea level, inside a massive hole that extends beneath the Red Line in a deep trench. The island is surrounded by a double-layered bubble, with half filled with water and coral, while the other half is filled with air, creating a unique environment supporting diverse marine life. At the top of this bubble is Ryugu Palace, the center of governance on the island, where the king resides and manages its affairs. The Hachin Tree lights the place with its natural glow, adding a magical atmosphere to the island. Fishman Island serves as an underwater gateway to the New World, providing an alternative route for pirates who prefer to avoid crossing the Red Line.

The entire crew was absorbed in contemplating the beautiful scene before them, where giant whales of all kinds swam peacefully in the sea.

If any ordinary pirate crew had been here, they might have died of fear at the sight of these monstrous sea creatures lying beneath ten thousand meters of ocean, but with their strength,

what should they fear?

Elsama soon reached the entrance that resembled a fish's mouth.

"Stop, intruders!" A sharp voice came from ahead, belonging to one of the guards stationed

at the entrance.

"Are we unwelcome?" Mikita whispered with a small smile on her face.

"That's normal; after all, humans and Fishmen have always been historically at odds, and our

pirate flag is clear even to the blind..." Robin remarked, prompting the crew to nod in

agreement.

"Nami, don't stop, go in." Ren spoke, uninterested in wasting his time here; he had better

things to do.

"Understood, Captain-!" Nami smiled, then directed Elsama to proceed through the gateway.

Flutter Kick Coating (Bata-ashi Kōtingu) was a special coating used to form a bubble around individuals instead of ships.

When coated, a spherical bubble forms from the knees to the head, while the legs are covered with a thin layer, allowing them to withstand deep-sea pressure with limited air to breathe. This coating grants the ability to leave the ship underwater and engage in combat when necessary.

Ren had previously obtained this from King Neptune, of course, at his own request, knowing he would have to leave the ship to deal with Vander Decken, who roamed the waters surrounding Fish-Man Island.

Every member of his crew also had the Flutter Kick Coating in case they needed it in an emergency.

It was clear she was merging her swordsmanship with the power of the Thunder Fruit, making her appear as a goddess of lightning to the terrified Fish-Men.

"Strong..." The assassin sensed the danger emanating from Nami. As the strongest swordsman on Fish-Man Island, he couldn't back down from another swordsman. "This lightning... is this the power of a Devil Fruit?" he asked with a rough voice, then laughed, "Is that what makes you so confident?"

*Whoosh!*

*Whoosh!*

*Whoosh!*

"!!!"

[2000 Words]
