*Gulp!*

Under the gaze of Ren, who had previously arrived in Fishman Island with Kalifa, Hody Jones swallowed several stimulant pills.

"You really did it... Well, you're useless anyway," sighed Ren as he transformed his weapon, Versa, into a red spear radiating an ominous glow.

"Kohahahaha! I can feel the power coursing through my veins! You weak human scum, taste the strength of the great Fishmen!"

Hody Jones laughed savagely, his body expanding several times over and his muscles bursting with tremendous power. His now-lengthened white hair gave him an ominous appearance, heightening the terror among the Fishmen present.

"Hehe~ So the captain is finally taking action." Nami, who had previously defeated the New Fishman Pirates' officers, had spotted Ren not too long ago.

If it weren't for her not wanting to ruin her captain's entrance, she would have jumped on him and kissed him as soon as she saw him.

"Let's end this quickly."

Versa, in spear form, floated in the air facing Hody Jones in his final form. Hody, now fully consumed by his newfound strength, felt invincible.

"Kohahaha! Let me show you---"

"!!!"

He tried to continue, but what happened next left him speechless.

The blue-haired man with the red spear he'd seen standing in front of him just a second ago had vanished in the blink of an eye!

Hody knew that such speed was beyond his reach, even with his enhanced power!

"Aaaaghh!!"

Unfortunately for him, it was too late, as he felt a sharp pain in his chest. Looking at the spear that had pierced through him, he was consumed by shock and utter disbelief.

"H-How... you damned human..." His breaths were ragged, filled with pain, shock, hatred, and many other emotions he never thought he'd experience.

What drove Hody even crazier was that the man who had dealt him a fatal blow in an instant wasn't even looking at him; he was gazing at the orange-haired swordswoman who had earlier struck down his crew!

This infuriated and maddened him beyond belief. He wanted to curse him but felt an overwhelming pain in his chest as his body started to weaken and grow frail.

"That's why I warned you you'd regret taking those pills..." Ren, who was now holding Nami by her waist, looked at him, then removed his spear from Hody Jones' body and left him to die on the ground.

Hody Jones didn't understand what was happening at that moment.

Everything was going smoothly a few hours ago; how did it all turn into such chaos?

He felt fatigue wash over his body and soul, but he didn't realize that the spear strike had not targeted his vital organs. The fatigue was actually from the stimulants he had consumed earlier.

There was a rule with those pills: once the consumer is defeated, side effects begin to show in their body, causing aging and brittle bones. In a few hours, Hody Jones wouldn't even be able to walk.

Back to Ren, he looked at the large, confused crowd of Fishmen, then turned his gaze to the thousands of human pirates looking at him with hope in their eyes.

"Do you all want your freedom?"

He spoke in a deep voice, not shouting, but the absolute silence in the area made his words audible to all.

The Fishmen knew he was addressing the thousands of enslaved humans who had been captured and forced to fight on their side. They wanted to do something, to resist, but the earlier events with their leader terrified them of the man before them.

"Y-Yes, sir!"

"Please free us, sir!"

"I'm willing to follow you for life! Just free me and my crew, please!"

"Please help us, Dark Death Warlord!"

As soon as Ren spoke, many of the captured pirates responded with pleas. Some even recognized Ren; those who didn't were likely imprisoned here before he started his journey.

Ren wasn't bothered; his goal in recruiting these people was that they might make for good cannon fodder.

He had also noticed a few individuals with potential among them.

"We've arrived Ren-Duno, jamon!"

Suddenly, hundreds of people burst into Fishman Island.

It was none other than King Neptune and his entourage, along with hundreds of the Royal

Guard!

Shirahoshi was also there. The girl who hadn't left the palace in ten years was incredibly happy, even if her exploration was limited to the boundaries of Fishman Island.

As for why they were here? Previously, after Ren had dealt with Vander Decken and returned to Ryugu Palace, he informed the king that Shirahoshi's curse had been lifted and that he had taken care of Vander Decken.

Neptune was so happy he wanted to host a feast for Ren immediately.

But Ren had to stop the eager king and inform him of the darkness and conspiracy unfolding in Fish-Man Island.

He also hinted that there was something significant he would discover after all this.

King Neptune was shocked and deeply concerned upon hearing about Hody Jones's plans and the tens of thousands of fish-men rallying in Fish-Man District.

They had truly neglected the Fish-Man District! Neptune felt regret, but knowing there was no time to waste, he quickly rallied his forces and came here.

Conqueror's Haki.

The sleeves were rolled up to his elbows, with interwoven leather straps, perhaps to hold light weapons or gear. Chapter 148: [147] The Truth
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Bonus chapter. [3000 Words]

On Fish-Man Island, thousands of fish-men were led into various prisons in the Ryugu Kingdom. The prisons were more crowded than ever before in the kingdom beneath the sea's surface. The mastermind behind all of this, Hody Jones, along with his officers, was imprisoned in the royal palace's prison at Ryugu palace.

Several hours had passed since the incident, and King Neptune's messengers had spread the news that Ren, the Warlord and leader of the Sin Pirates, had subdued the rebellion and helped the king capture the mastermind.

This act of gratitude from Neptune to Ren also helped elevate Ren's status and respect among the people of Fish-Man Island, which would bring benefits to his own people. Neptune didn't hesitate in doing this, understanding the advantages it would bring.

If he were a regular king of another kingdom, he might not have done it, considering that most don't care about the well-being of their people.

Back to the main point, after the capture of all fish-men, Neptune's guards helped to release the shackles of the pirates who joined the Sin Pirates.

However, small issues arose.

Ren knew that changing a pirate's treacherous nature would be difficult, but he was prepared for it.

He slaughtered dozens of pirates who attacked the royal guards after their release, setting an example for the rest and quelling any signs of riot.

"Remember, Silver Drak, no harassing civilians as long as they don't attack you, and no assaulting women, understood?"

The Black Death looked seriously at the second division commander, prompting Silver Drak to nod sincerely, "Understood, Captain. Anyone who disobeys this, I'll execute personally!"

"Good. Now, take these."

Ren accessed his system interface, focusing specifically on the newly acquired rewards.

¶ [-Ding! Congratulations on completing the main quest "Defeat Vander Decken" and earning: "Two Armament Haki Scrolls (Low) + Two Observation Haki Scrolls (Low)"]

¶ [-Ding! Congratulations on completing the hidden quest "Defeat the Flying Pirates" and earning: 5 Energy Drinks (Low)]

¶ [-Ding! Congratulations on completing the main quest "Defeat Hody Jones" and earning: 2 Shave Scrolls (Low) + 2 Moonwalk Scrolls (Low)]

¶ [-Ding! Congratulations on completing the hidden quest "Defeat the New Fish-Man Pirates" and earning: Devil Fruit Mastery Scroll (Medium)]

These were all the rewards he earned from his recent moves.

Determining the fate of these rewards was easy-Ren decided to give everything to the commanders of his fleet divisions, except for the Devil Fruit Mastery Scroll, which he would use himself.

"W-What...?" Silver Drak looked at the scrolls and strange bottles that appeared out of nowhere in confusion.

"These are my gifts to make you stronger. Let me explain..."

....

"Can this really exist...?" Silver Drak muttered in disbelief.

Ren explained to him what these scrolls were and what they could do.

Silver Drak couldn't believe his ears but had no choice but to treat it as a miracle and used the rewards.

He gained Armament and Observation Haki, strengthened his body, and acquired two of the Six Powers techniques: Shave and Moonwalk.

"Thank you, Captain! I'll make sure to keep this between us and strive to serve you well!" Silver Drak felt grateful and more energized than ever.

The overwhelming strength surging through his body was exhilarating, and he felt deep gratitude toward his new captain.

He also swore to keep the secret of these scrolls.

But he didn't realize that Ren used his special hypnosis ability, sending an unconscious command to Silver Drak's mind that would prevent him from divulging any secrets, even if he were captured and tortured.

Ren was generous, but he wasn't foolish; he wasn't ready to risk exposing the system's abilities.

Of course, the World Government wouldn't know anything about the system itself, but scrolls that can teach Haki and other powers instantly would be more valuable than the One Piece to

many people.

Although no one could use the system's rewards without his permission, would the World Government believe him? And would they truly have no means of forcing him to grant permission for the rewards to agents or marines?

It might seem paranoid, but caution is better than regret.

Drak on the shoulder and then gave him something.

Silver Drak was about to salute and leave, but his expression froze at what he saw next.

"Don't rush; let me introduce you to each of them."

storage.

The Glue Fruit, a Logia type, was one of the fruits he obtained in his deal with Garp when he captured Luffy. He had offered it to his crew members before, but none had shown interest, so he stored it, and now it would finally return to the sea.

'I'd really love to see Garp's face if he saw Drak using it to strike the Marines...'

He hypnotized him.

Ren produced a brown-colored Devil Fruit from thin air.

"A Devil Fruit...?" Neptune recognized the fruit in Ren's hand, unlike Shirahoshi, who had spent years confined to her room.

"Ren-dono... Shirahoshi is a mermaid, and swimming is an essential part of her life... especially now that she's free." King Neptune was in a dilemma; he couldn't allow Shirahoshi

to eat a Devil Fruit.

"I understand your concern, King Neptune, but there's no need to worry... After all, this Devil

Fruit is special in a way..." Ren smiled, unfazed about revealing the secret behind his unique

Devil

Fruits.

"What do you mean?" King Neptune raised an eyebrow and asked.

"I mean Shirahoshi won't be affected by seawater after eating this Devil Fruit." Ren didn't wait f King Neptune to respond but immediately showed him the proof. Out of nowhere, a massive swimming pool filled with water appeared. Ren had stored numerous random items in The Infinity Space, so with its infinite capacity, he had no reason to hesitate in storing anything that came his way.

"Huh?" Neptune was confused and couldn't understand how Ren continued to pull out so many random items in such a magical way.

But his focus shifted as Ren dived into the pool filled with water.

*Crack~!*

*Boom!*

"!!!"

"The pool... it shattered!"

The right and left ministers, as well as King Neptune's children—including Shirahoshi-

gasped in shock at the sight.

They saw Ren clench his fist, and a white energy appeared around it, followed by a loud sound

as the seawater-filled pool collapsed. Could this really be a Devil Fruit power? What kind of

Devil Fruit could possess such strength?

"Impossible... You're able to use a Devil Fruit while in the water!"

King Neptune finally understood what Ren meant when he said the Devil Fruits he offered were special. This reality was overwhelming and hard to believe.

If he hadn't seen it with his own eyes, he would have never believed it.

In fact, he almost wanted to doubt his senses, but he couldn't. Everyone who heard Neptune's words was equally stunned, except for Shirahoshi, as she

didn't know much about Devil Fruits to begin with.

Nami and Kalifa, of course, were exceptions too. The two beauties remained calm from

her?" This was the most pressing question on his mind after the matter of the Sea Curse was

settled. Though it was still shocking, he had no choice but to believe in this miracle. "You'll find out soon. Shirahoshi-chan, eat the fruit." He smiled at the tearful girl to reassure

her. "I've changed its awful taste, so there's no need to worry."

"O-ok, Ren-sama..."

The fruit was tiny in comparison to someone like Shirahoshi, so she swallowed it quickly.

*Crunch~!*

||

||

A moment of silence followed as Shirahoshi closed her eyes, seeming to process something.

Then, in the next instant, a dramatic transformation began.

Her enormous 11.87-meter body started to shrink under the astonished gazes of everyone,

their mouths agape in shock.

The change didn't stop there. A white light enveloped her shrinking form, gradually bringing

her closer to a human size.

The light shone for several seconds before fading, revealing a completely transformed

Shirahoshi!

Her large pink tail had vanished, replaced by long, slender human legs. Her already beautiful

face became even more breathtaking than ever.

As for her upper body, despite her size reduction, her chest remained prominent by human

standards.

She had become an extraordinary beauty, one that could genuinely rival the Pirate Empress

Boa Hancock!

Chapter 149: [148] Emotional Reunion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2700 Words]

[Five Days Later.]

In a place surrounded by white clouds, towering in the clear blue sky, there floated a silent island above Paradise, the first half of the Grand Line.

Sky Island had undergone substantial changes in the past few weeks since the Black Death became the god of Skypiea. Under his guidance and thanks to the efforts of his devoted followers, numerous new buildings were constructed.

The purpose of these buildings was simple: to train a new generation of fierce fighters.

This wasn't just ordinary training-it was the teaching of Haki to anyone whose physical strength had reached the level required to learn Armament Haki!

The same applied to Observation Haki; anyone who met the requirements could be trained in both types of Haki by specialized Haki instructors.

And where did these instructors come from? They were none other than the Four Priests of Pegasus: Gedatsu, Satori, Ohm, and Shura.

After consuming the Pegasus Warrior elixirs, the four priests underwent a radical transformation and unprecedented growth in strength, enabling them to awaken their Armament Haki!

As for Observation Haki, known as "Mantra" on this floating island, they had long been familiar with it.

Elsewhere, far from these training facilities, in the holiest and grandest place in Skypiea-the Temple of the God, built atop the giant Jack-a captivating scene unfolded.

A man with long blue hair and piercing blue eyes sat calmly. His eyes seemed capable of piercing through space itself, seeing the infinite stars scattered across the universe.

He wore a simple black kimono, but from one glance, it was clear that the kimono was crafted from the finest wool. In truth, it was still his eternal garment, capable of transforming into any shape.

*Tap.*

*Tap.*

His finger tapped gently on the armrest of his golden throne, and Ren appeared to be in deep thought.

'Miss System, there are many things that need to be added to my status.'

He communicated mentally with the mysterious entity that resided somewhere, and soon heard a soft voice in his mind.

[Indeed, Master. Would you like to see your status?]

'Oh, you're oddly polite today,' he chuckled internally, bracing himself for the scolding that would likely come next.

[W-What do you mean! I've always been polite! You're the rude one! Your whole family is rude!]

It seemed that Miss System's mood was worsening, so Ren had to endure her reprimand for a few minutes until she calmed down.

[Sigh... What an inconsiderate host.]

'Sigh... Alright, alright. Show me my status.' After teasing his gentle system for a few moments, Ren requested to view his status.

¶[Name: Konabar Ren.]

Many things had happened over the past five days that he and his crew spent on Fish-Man Island before returning to Alabasta and then traveling to their other base on Sky Island. Perhaps the most notable event was Shirahoshi's request to join Ren's crew.

He had intended to invite her anyway, but the girl was so enthusiastic that she begged her father for permission to sail with him.

Neptune eventually agreed after several days of Shirahoshi's pleas. But why did he agree? Ren knew the reason; it was likely because of the Devil Fruit that Shirahoshi had eaten, granting her the ability to shift from her hybrid form to a human form.

The girl felt more comfortable in her smaller human form when she was outside because of

this.

But what did this have to do with King Neptune agreeing to let Shirahoshi join his crew? It was because her true form was unknown!

She had been confined to her room since childhood, so how could anyone recognize her? Especially someone from the upper seas.

Even if there were a wanted poster of her, Neptune was relieved because, at worst, she would return to Fish-Man Island and transform back into her giant mermaid form.

Oh, and there was one more thing-Shirahoshi hadn't yet discovered that her mother's spirit

and Shave.

Ren also hypnotized him, just as he had done with Silver Drak.

Secrets are important, after all, so I have to be a bit careful, don't I? Ren thought to himself.

about a month ago.

As for how he convinced Kaya... well, that was after several rounds of lovemaking, which left many indecent marks on her body.

His Devil Fruit powers had grown significantly, with details to follow.

Previously, when she first arrived, she was quite shocked like every girl who entered this place for the first time, but she quickly grew fond of this island.

She also enjoyed swimming in the ocean surrounding this island, although it wasn't very

deep, it was enough for her.

"Shirahoshi's mood had changed a bit from her usual self. Ever since she saw natural sunlight, sailed on the open seas, and flew in the sky aboard Elsama, she was no longer the same crybaby without reason.

seeing her deceased mother.

'A surprise?' the girl asked in a soft voice, tilting her head in curiosity.

It was his house, but it seemed the girls had grown accustomed to being there, and he didn't

Even though the island was medium-sized by One Piece world standards, enough to build a small town, so each crew member could have her own house...

His train of thought was interrupted as he saw a group of girls engaged in different activities.

Nami and Nojiko were peeling some oranges while chatting with Bell-mere about women's topics, though a slight blush was visible on Bell-mere's beautiful face. Ren decided to respect their privacy and not eavesdrop on them.

Speaking of that skill, Ren had managed, after a few days, to control the sounds he wanted to

of his Warlord position. However, Ren had future plans to address this issue.

lounging on her sofa chatting with Zala.

it seems you were enjoying yourself~'

'But there shouldn't be any scent left, right?... Never mind, women are really strange when it comes to these things.'

'How about we enjoy ourselves together?' He looked at her face, which turned slightly red

from embarrassment. 'Moo~ you're teasing me again~'

!!!!!

"Fufufu- Finally, Shirahoshi-chan will reunite with her mother," Nojiko smiled, clearly

The other girls shared her sentiment.

She didn't know how this was happening or how her mother appeared before her. She didn't understand anything, but she knew she was incredibly happy at this moment. The tears she shed now weren't the usual tears of sadness; they were tears of joy!

The joy she had missed for so many years.

Chapter 150: [149] Ancient Prophecy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[2000 Words]

There was a dramatic and moving reunion in Elsama, floating above Sky Island, but far away from all this, some schemes were being plotted in the shadows.

[An Unknown Island in the New World.]

"The time has come for you to repay the favor you owe us, as the World Government," said a man wearing a mask that concealed his features and dressed in white clothes that covered his tall frame, his voice cold.

"Oh, CPO... Have the elders finally decided on the favor they want?"

The one responding was an old, bald man with a long, white beard. The elder was dressed in traditional Japanese clothing, and what stood out the most was the crystal orb he held in his hand.

"So, what is the request?" He didn't look at the CPO agents as he spoke; his focus remained solely on his crystal orb.

The agent didn't seem disturbed, and his partner, standing like a statue, was similarly unmoved. He replied in a cold voice:

"Who is responsible for the death of Saint Leonios Suprema?"

A short silence followed as the agent relayed the World Government's request-or rather, the request of the Five Elders, who needed to find the mastermind behind that incident to quell the wrath of House Suprema.

Although the Five Elders ruled the World Government, or so it seemed to the rest of the Celestial Dragons, the elders themselves couldn't ignore the anger of one of the saintly houses.

House Suprema had been seething with rage since the death of Saint Leonios, especially his father, who had visited the Five Elders multiple times, demanding that they do everything in their power to find and execute the criminal.

Now, CPO agents were just moments away from uncovering the truth.

"Very well. This is indeed a significant request, but remember... after this, I, the Seer of the Seas, will no longer owe you anything." The old man nodded gently, but his words clearly drew a line between the two sides.

The agent could only show utmost respect, replying, "Of course, Seer. The World Government always maintains mutual respect with the Omnicia Tribe."

The Omnicia Tribe was a secretive and nearly unknown clan in this world, known for producing in each generation a person titled "The Seer," who possessed a rare and

extraordinary ability to unveil the world's deepest secrets and answer questions no one else could resolve.

This ability passed down through generations in a mysterious manner. It wasn't a Devil Fruit power but seemed more like a divine gift; when the current Seer died, a new one was born to carry on the ability.

The tribe's members protected their Seer with utmost secrecy, aware of the value of the knowledge he held, making them a target for many powers in pursuit of truth and dominion.

However, even a unique tribe like this could not escape the ever-watchful eyes of the World Government.

Yet, despite the government's awareness of this tribe and its power, they couldn't impose their will on the Seer. His powers only worked when he was in a calm and stable state of mind, meaning any attempt to coerce or threaten him would disable his ability to see and know, making it impossible to control him.

Furthermore, the power could only be used once every ten years, making it a precious ability that the World Government would only employ in dire circumstances. They also needed to ensure that each generation's Seer felt indebted to them in some way to benefit from his capabilities.

"Let me see who had the audacity to kill a Celestial Noble..."

The old Seer muttered softly as the crystal orb in his hand began to glow with a dark, purple light.

The Seer closed his eyes, and it seemed the light from the orb had enveloped him, drawing him into a world of his own.

In this unique realm, the Seer appeared, gazing into an endless void, when suddenly, a slightly blurred image began to form in this still space.

It was an image of individuals in black cloaks, and the towering trees clearly indicated that the place in the hazy vision was the Sabaody Archipelago.

The Seer was intensely focused. The hazy image gradually sharpened, shifting from one scene to another: the scene of the Celestial Dragon's murder, then a scene where those responsible for the crime gathered in a place that appeared to be a flying ship in the sky.

"Have you seen enough?"

"!!!"

The Seer's heart jumped upon hearing this voice. He had used this divine ability multiple times, but this was the first time something like this had ever occurred.

"Wh-Who's there?!" The Seer stammered, causing the hazy vision to falter, and his fear peaked as he realized he couldn't return his consciousness to his body; he was left floating, staring into the endless void around him.

"Impertinent."

The same voice resounded again, echoing through the seemingly endless space, sending chills through the Seer's "body."

The voice was soft and delicate, as if a goddess herself were speaking, yet there was a strange strength and coldness in it that would make the bravest hearts leap.

"Vanish."

These were the last words the Seer heard before his consciousness faded into darkness, sinking into eternal slumber.

Yet, in those fleeting fractions of a second, he recalled a commandment passed down for generations among the Seers of the Omnicia Tribe.

[500 Years Ago]

*Splash*

*Splash*

"Where I am?" a confused woman's voice echoed as she stepped into what seemed to be a hidden village on this island.

If CPO agents were here, they would find this island strikingly similar to the one housing the Omnicia Tribe.

The only noticeable difference was the number of trees and the lower water level compared to what it would be 500 years later.

The mysterious woman entered the island, warmly greeted by the village elder.

"By the way, sir, why do people call you the Seer?"

A middle-aged man smiled at her question and replied, "Hoho, we usually don't share this

with outsiders, but you'll be an exception since you found this place, and I sense no hostility from you." He paused briefly before continuing, "I'm called the Seer because of my innate ability, which is..."

A minute later, the woman spoke in amazement, "Wow... What an incredible ability."

The man seemed pleased with her admiration and added, "Would you like to know your future? What do you say?"

A smile appeared on the woman's face, and she nodded eagerly, "Yes, please, if that's

possible!"

The Seer agreed, surprising the rest of the tribe, who couldn't understand why he treated the stranger in this way. Some even thought he might be in love with her.

"I'll begin," the Seer said, activating his gifted skill, or so he believed.

He was called the Seer because he could know everything, including people's futures.

But the Seer continued, his expression shocked, "Your future self is speaking... Ruler of the

"!!!!!!

At that moment, the Seer's eyes snapped open, bloodshot, and he cried out in pain.

The paper held a sketch of a man who appeared to be in his twenties, handsome, with relatively long blue hair and piercing blue eyes that seemed to gaze through the cosmos!

If Ren saw this paper, he would recognize the person in the drawing as himself. "Seek out this person. He's the only way to achieve your goal. By the way, I haven't asked your name yet," said the Seer, his words enigmatic, as the woman stared at the paper in

The Seer's voice broke through her swirling thoughts, and she answered with a calm façade:

"I... I am Toki, Amatsuki Toki."

[Sky Island]

The Demon's Range Archipelago-a mysterious chain of islands-was known for its rugged

But these were only rumors, and rumors are often exaggerated. According to Ren's newly-formed intelligence network and a few million berries spent, he acquired a map of the Demon's Range Archipelago. "Great job, Nami. It won't hurt to check it out, so our next destination is set!"

His deep voice echoed to all the girls currently in the house.

Nami smiled, taking control of Elsama and setting their course toward the target.

The Demon's Range Archipelago, where the ruins of God Valley await!

Chapter 151: [150] Collision Course

Bonus chapter [2300 Words]

[The First Half of the Grand Line, Paradise]

The waters of the Grand Line are known for their sudden turbulence and unpredictable storms, but in one particular place, there was an eerie and unsettling calm.

It felt like a dark abyss where lights faded away, like distant memories vanishing into a thick fog.

This fog was unlike any that fades with time; it seemed almost alive, moving slowly to engulf anyone who dared to enter. Your vision would grow hazy, and the details of the world around you would gradually dissolve, as though you were slipping into a dark dream. From above, heavy, dark clouds gathered, reflecting old rumors of a cloud island, perhaps suspended above the triangle, keeping the sea below in an eternal shadow.

But these weren't just rumors-they were truths that many refused to believe.

This was the Florian Triangle, a terrifying place to millions of pirates around the world.

In this triangle, surrounded by bleak fog, there lay a giant structure that at first glance appeared to be a small island.

But with a closer look, you'd realize it was not an island but an enormous ship, once recognized as the largest ship in the world.

But now... it was in a terrible state.

The remnants of Thriller Bark stood as a floating ghost ship, its features resurfacing quietly within the dense fog that engulfed everything.

The Gothic castle that once imposed its grandeur at the heart of the ship had turned to ruins, with shattered balconies and crumbling towers that seemed to scream with buried memories of horror.

Among the ruins, dry trees grew-remnants of the internal forest once filled with the undead, still haunting even amid all this destruction.

"Moria-sama! Where are you?! Moria-sama!"

The oppressive silence was broken by a terrified female voice calling for someone who would never answer.

"Dr. Hogback! There you are- " The strange-looking girl with pink hair stopped when she saw Dr. Hogback's eyes, which appeared as if they'd seen the most terrifying thing of his life. "Eeeeeek!" She screamed, kicking Hogback's corpse hard, then ran away as fast as she could.

"Moria-sama! Absalom! Damn it, where is everyone?!" shrieked Perona, one of the Thriller Bark Pirates, overcome with terror.

The last thing she remembered was the arrival of several strangers at Thriller Bark and the start of a deadly battle. She, gripped by fear, had chosen to flee and avoid the fight.

After all, she trusted in Moria's strength-he was a Warlord!

But unfortunately for Perona, she didn't know that the one attacking this time was Blackbeard.

In any case, when she fled from Thriller Bark on her small boat, the waters carried her through the Florian Triangle until she encountered an abandoned ship.

Or so she thought, until she found a skeleton there that frightened her so much that she fainted on the spot.

"Moria-sama couldn't possibly have been defeated as that foolish skeleton claimed!" Perona gritted her teeth, recalling her conversation with the strange skeleton named Brook.

Brook had said that Moria was defeated because his shadow suddenly returned to him, which even made the skeleton shed tears.

And how could a skeleton with no flesh, blood, or organs shed tears? Perona couldn't find an answer to this perplexing question.

A few days later, she returned to Thriller Bark, only to find ruins of the once-grand ship...

Perona ran with all her might, trying to escape this horrifying place, but she tripped over something and fell to the ground.

"Ouch!"

She groaned in pain, then saw what had caused her to fall.

"Eeeek! A monster!"

*Bam!*

It was Absalom's lifeless body staring at her-or so Perona thought-so she kicked the body away and continued running.

"Mama, I don't want to stay here! I have to get out of here!"

"Yes, it won't take me long." The long-legged woman replied calmly, and the next moment, she boarded a ship bearing the Big Mom Pirates' flag.

"Set course for Alabasta."

[The New World, Onigashima]

A proud woman sat, her long white hair gradually turning blue at the tips, flowing around her like waves as she sat quietly on the ground.

Her large, curved horns reflected a blend of strength and solitude, while her eyes shone with a mysterious light.

She wore a loose white shirt and traditional trousers that swayed with each step, and an ancient red sash adorned her waist, as though she were a warrior from an old legend.

In front of her lay a newspaper that still seemed clean; the date was old, and the news was obviously not fresh for anyone who could get newspapers at will-unlike her.

"Ace is dead."

She spoke this sentence as if struggling to believe what the paper said, unable to accept the photos that accompanied the news: Ace's stabbed body, his blood, and his hand severed by an

enemy.

[The Grand Line, Paradise]

[Sabaody Archipelago]

This well-known island had recently started to regain its tranquility, as Admiral Kizaru had returned to headquarters after finding no trace of the culprit who had slain the Celestial Dragon.

However, he had killed and captured many pirates in the meantime.

In the eyes of the general public, this was a very positive response from the Navy and the World Government after such a shocking incident. The death and imprisonment of thousands of pirates meant greater stability and a safer life for many.

But the World Government didn't feel the same way.

To them, the Celestial Dragon was priceless; the deaths of millions of ordinary people wouldn't affect the mood of these "divine" dragons.

With the gradual return of peace to the Sabaody Archipelago, pirates once again dared to venture to the island.

If Kizaru were still here, not a single one of them would dare set foot on this island.

*Crunch~!*

*Chew~!*

*Sip~!*

*Gulp-!*

In a crowded corner of one of Sabaody's taverns, Jewelry Bonney was engrossed in an enormous feast, drawing the attention of everyone around her.

The young woman appeared to be in her twenties, with long pink hair draped over her shoulders, adding a bold touch that matched her sharp purple eyes.

Her attire consisted of a small tilted hat atop her head and a leather jacket over a torn shirt- an outfit that reflected her free and unrestrained life as a pirate.

The sounds of her constant chewing filled the room, and she kept ordering more dishes. Plates piled up before her, as if she hadn't eaten in days.

She tore into the meat ravenously, devouring it non-stop, with a pile of bones and empty shells beside her.

"This... this is insane..."

The bartender stood in awe, watching the young woman eat as if she'd never seen food before. When he delayed, Bonney banged her fist on the table angrily, shouting, "Where's the food? Didn't you hear my order?!"

Her face was fierce, and she seemed to be in a foul mood, with a serious, intimidating look in her eyes.

"Damn it, damn it, damn it!" Bonney devoured her food with reckless abandon, cursing and swearing as though she were venting her anger and sorrow through eating.

Those around her avoided looking directly at her, even her crew members; some were amazed, while others were frightened. Her reputation as a Supernova and a well-known pirate kept everyone from daring to provoke her.

'Is he really gone...?' she thought to herself, recalling the scene she had witnessed a few days ago when she watched the War of the Best.

Those machines called Pacifistas... that shape... there was no doubt it was clear... her father was really dead.

"Goddammit!" Though she looked like an adult, she was, in fact, just a ten-year-old girl this year... She wanted to cry, and she did cry. No one dared say a word, avoiding her out of fear. 'I need to change my plan...' she thought, considering her next best option. Should she form a pirate alliance? This idea crossed her mind for a few seconds as she weighed the likelihood and benefits of such a move, wondering if it would help her achieve her goal.

Her objective was clear: to avenge her father, Bartholomew Kuma. Her hatred for the World Government burned in her heart with every bitter memory. This wasn't merely a desire for simple revenge; it was a battle to free her father from his grim fate. Kuma, who had suffered under government manipulation, had been turned into a human weapon due to Dr. Vegapunk's experiments, leaving him unable to reclaim his freedom.

She finally stopped eating, then looked at the various pirate posters pinned to the tavern walls.

Wanted posters were something pirates took pride in; the higher the bounty, the stronger the individual-or so it usually seemed.

"But that's not entirely true..." She looked at one particular poster that everyone either intentionally or unintentionally avoided. Bounty hunters wouldn't dare mess with him, and other pirates feared him.

Only a few in this world didn't fear him. He was beyond Bonney's reach, and she had no illusions about forming an alliance with him. What could she possibly offer him? Well, she did have something to bargain with... information she'd gathered about the World Government, her sworn enemy.

"You idiot, do you know how to reach the Black Death?" Bonney shouted at one of her crew members.

The person in question was startled, but he quickly restrained his fear and answered in a shaky voice, knowing how terrifying his captain could be when angered.

"Th-They say that the Warlord Black Death has started a major recruitment drive in recent days... and that his base is located in Alabasta." Bonney heard his response, and her eyes lit up

with a sharp gleam.

"Recruiting, huh..."

[The Grand Line, New World]

The air was filled with the scent of unique flowers, emanating from vast fields that covered the island in hues of purple and white, giving it the name "Sky Bloom."

[sinister living trees Image]

punch directly at the monster's core.

*Crack~!!*

The explosion echoed across the island, and the creature's body shattered into scattered

pieces, its broken branches turning to ash that drifted into the air. With this resounding blast,

the once-raging beast fell silent, its painful, unwilling growl fading into nothingness.

[2700 Words]

"The island was in complete chaos..." Kalifa sighed as she gazed at the once-beautiful island, now devastated by the earthquakes and violent tsunamis caused by Ren's powers.

*Roar...*

Her beautiful eyes fixed on the destructive wooden monster that roared weakly in pain. Its once mighty stance was now gone, replaced by a pitiful whimper.

"Captain, do you know what these things are?" She took silent steps toward her captain, who stood silently in the ruined scene.

"No... I've never seen such strange trees that can transform into a monster capable of easily crushing a Vice Admiral..."

This wasn't an exaggeration. The defeated monster had somehow used Armament Haki, and its speed was immense. Ren doubted that a regular Vice Admiral could defeat it; only an Admiral candidate like Momosaji Gion could manage that.

However, Ren noticed that the creature lacked Observation Haki. It relied solely on its primal instincts throughout the battle.

Of course, even if it had Observation Haki, Ren wouldn't have had any trouble defeating it. After all, Ren was as strong as an Admiral.

"I see... So, what should we do now?" Kalifa stood behind him, her alluring body brushing against his slightly.

Ren smiled faintly when he saw the blonde woman playing her little games.

"Aahhn~!" Kalifa moaned when she felt Ren's hand on her waist.

This wasn't unusual for them, but this time, she felt a strange pleasure that surged through her body, reaching her mind, causing her to let out a lewd moan.

"Ahhn- C-Captain... W-what have you done to me... Ahn~" She moaned, her face flushed with embarrassment, and even her previously steady body was on the verge of collapsing into Ren's strong arms.

"This is your punishment for trying to tease your master-" He smiled mischievously.

What Kalifa didn't know was that Ren had used his "Divine Massage" skill, which made women feel Horny and put them into a state of intense heat immediately.

*Crash~!*

*Clatter~!*

This mysterious figure was none other than the once-dreaded malevolent spirit.

"Haah... Haah... Is it finally over?" gasped Vivi, who hovered above, her lower body having transformed into a swirling mass of sand.

Below her, the ground bore marks of her presence-dry, dead patches and withered trees. Wherever Vivi went, her power seemed to sap the life and moisture from everything dependent on water.

"That was strange..." Nami sighed, still gripping her unsheathed sword tightly. Her gaze fixed on the spirit, now in an uncertain state.

The spirit had ceased its attacks some time ago, but caution lingered.

"I heard tremors from afar earlier. Could it be the captain?"

The voice came from Nojiko, the blue-haired girl holding an unusual gun in her hand. Not far from her lay the corpses of numerous beasts, each bearing holes that seemed to have been caused by bullets piercing through their bodies.

The gun in her hand was peculiar—its design complex and futuristic, as if plucked from a world beyond imagination. Its dark color and exceptionally long barrel were framed by a robust metallic casing.

A detached cylinder dangled beneath the barrel, seemingly purposeless and mysterious in its addition. Yet the most striking feature was the large orb embedded between the hammer and the barrel, radiating an inexplicable aura that gave Nojiko an unsettling sensation.

[Image here]

This weapon was known as "Emperor," and the name was no exaggeration. At first glance, it might appear as a convoluted and overly intricate firearm, but its design was no coincidence. Instead, it embodied the volatile and unpredictable nature of its abilities.

It was a gun that seamlessly blended futuristic innovation with a touch of archaic technology. The long barrel was encased in a metallic frame, while the detached cylinder hinted at enigmatic functionality. There were no visible scopes or clear details to suggest the weapon's purpose, yet its defining feature was the orb-a component that elevated it beyond mere weaponry.

And its power? Astonishing and terrifying in equal measure. The ability to control a bullet's trajectory as if one were commanding the very winds themselves. To write pathways in the air, to shape the bullet's course. No reloading was required, and the ammunition was unlimited...

Or rather, there wasn't any ammunition in the traditional sense. The bullets consumed the user's stamina instead, for this world knew nothing of "Stand" energy.

God's Weapons skill had made such radical modification possible.

Imagine a bullet circling its target, relentlessly chasing them without losing speed. It could curve into a U-shape, thread its way through a narrow passage unnoticed, and strike with unfathomable precision.

[Image here]

Yet, like all great power, there were limits. Nothing remains infinite. Despite its supremacy at close range, the bullets began to lose their effectiveness with increasing distance. The gun adhered to the laws of range-its strength diminished as the target moved further away. Furthermore, the Emperor had a special bond with Nojiko, mirroring the connection between user and weapon in its original world. This unique relationship allowed her to summon the gun directly to her hand, offering unmatched convenience.

"I'm not sure... Mikita flew off in that direction earlier. I have no idea what's happening with her," Nami replied to her older sister, her focus returning to the enigmatic spirit before them. "This... there's an unfamiliar language here... A script I've never seen before."

Suddenly, the girls were drawn to Robin's voice. She was brushing dust off ancient, mysterious statues.

The statues were relatively small, resembling animals, each roughly the size of a young child. There were five in total, and Robin had discovered them in the area minutes after the battle ended.

"What are these things?" Shirahoshi tilted her head gently, her large eyes fixated on the engravings carved into the statues shaped like various animals: a lion, a tiger, a leopard, a bear, and finally, a monkey.

"Unfortunately, even I don't recognize the ancient language inscribed here... If only the captain were here---" Robin began but was abruptly interrupted as she sensed something in

the sky.

The atmosphere grew heavy, and the tension in the air became palpable. Something significant was about to unfold.

Her lips curved into a gentle smile as she looked at the three figures descending to the ground.

To be continued....

Nidhogg is a legendary dragon in Norse mythology, considered one of the evil creatures in ancient

beliefs. It resides in the roots of Yggdrasil (the World Tree), a giant tree that connects the different

worlds in the universe. Nidhogg is said to gnaw at the tree's roots, threatening its destruction, symbolizing the destructive forces that can affect the balance of the universe. In some versions of the myths, Nidhogg is portrayed as a harmful and terrifying monster, symbolizing

corruption and death. It is also associated with the end of the world, or Ragnarok, where it is said to

take part in the destruction of the worlds.

Nidhogg also appears in some contemporary works, such as video games, where its character is developed based on the original myth.
